speaker: (Dhritarashtra)
sanskrit_sloka:
धर्मक्षेत्रे कुरुक्षेत्रे समवेता युयुत्सवः ।
मामकाः पाण्डवाश्चैव किमकुर्वत सञ्जय ॥
translation_en: On the field of dharma, at Kurukshetra, gathered and eager for battle—what did my men and the Pandavas do, O Sanjaya?

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
दृष्ट्वा तु पाण्डवानीकं व्यूढं दुर्योधनस्तदा ।
आचार्यमुपसङ्गम्य राजा वचनमब्रवीत् ॥
translation_en: Then, seeing the Pandava army drawn up in formation, King Duryodhana approached his teacher and spoke these words.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
पश्यैतां पाण्डुपुत्राणामाचार्य महतीं चमूम् ।
व्यूढां द्रुपदपुत्रेण तव शिष्येण धीमता ॥
translation_en: Behold, O teacher, this great army of the sons of Pandu, arrayed by your skilled disciple, the son of Drupada.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
अत्र शूरा महेष्वासा भीमार्जुनसमा युधि ।
युयुधानो विराटश्च द्रुपदश्च महारथः ॥
translation_en: Here are heroes, mighty archers, equal in battle to Bhima and Arjuna: Yuyudhana, Virata, and Drupada, the great chariot-warrior.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
धृष्टकेतुश्चेकितानः काशिराजश्च वीर्यवान् ।
पुरुजित्कुन्तिभोजश्च शैब्यश्च नरपुङ्गवः ॥
translation_en: Dhrishtaketu, Chekitana, and the valiant king of Kashi; Purujit, Kuntibhoja, and Shaibya, the foremost of men.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
युधामन्युश्च विक्रान्त उत्तमौजाश्च वीर्यवान् ।
सौभद्रो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्व एव महारथाः ॥
translation_en:
The valiant Yudhamanyu and the courageous Uttamauja,  
Saubhadra and the sons of Draupadi—all great chariot-warriors.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
अस्माकं तु विशिष्टा ये तान्निबोध द्विजोत्तम ।
नायका मम सैन्यस्य संज्ञार्थं तान्ब्रवीमि ते ॥
translation_en: Know too, O best of the twice-born, those who are foremost among us, the leaders of my army; these I name to you.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
भवान्भीष्मश्च कर्णश्च कृपश्च समितिञ्जयः ।
अश्वत्थामा विकर्णश्च सौमदत्तिस्तथैव च ॥
translation_en: Yourself, and Bhishma, Karna, and Kripa, ever victorious in battle; Ashvatthama, Vikarna, and Saumadatti as well.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
अन्ये च बहवः शूरा मदर्थे त्यक्तजीविताः ।
नानाशस्त्रप्रहरणाः सर्वे युद्धविशारदाः ॥
translation_en: And many other heroes, who have staked their lives for my sake, bearing varied weapons—all skilled in war.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
अपर्याप्तं तदस्माकं बलं भीष्माभिरक्षितम् ।
पर्याप्तं त्विदमेतेषां बलं भीमाभिरक्षितम् ॥
translation_en:
Our force, guarded by Bhishma, is insufficient;  
but their force, guarded by Bhima, is sufficient.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
अयनेषु च सर्वेषु यथाभागमवस्थिताः ।
भीष्ममेवाभिरक्षन्तु भवन्तः सर्व एव हि ॥
translation_en: Therefore, stationed in your respective positions throughout the divisions, let all of you guard Bhishma above all.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
तस्य सञ्जनयन्हर्षं कुरुवृद्धः पितामहः ।
सिंहनादं विनद्योच्चैः शङ्खं दध्मौ प्रतापवान् ॥
translation_en: To cheer him, the aged Kuru, the mighty grandsire, roared like a lion and loudly blew his conch.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
ततः शङ्खाश्च भेर्यश्च पणवानकगोमुखाः ।
सहसैवाभ्यहन्यन्त स शब्दस्तुमुलोऽभवत् ॥
translation_en: Then conches, drums, kettledrums, and horns suddenly sounded, and the noise was tumultuous.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
ततः श्वेतैर्हयैर्युक्ते महति स्यन्दने स्थितौ ।
माधवः पाण्डवश्चैव दिव्यौ शङ्खौ प्रदध्मतुः ॥
translation_en: Then, stationed in their great chariot yoked with white horses, Madhava and the Pandava blew their divine conches.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
पाञ्चजन्यं हृषीकेशो देवदत्तं धनञ्जयः ।
पौण्ड्रं दध्मौ महाशङ्खं भीमकर्मा वृकोदरः ॥
translation_en: Hrishikesha blew the Panchajanya, Dhananjaya the Devadatta, and Vrikodara, of mighty deeds, blew the great conch Paundra.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनन्तविजयं राजा कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।
नकुलः सहदेवश्च सुघोषमणिपुष्पकौ ॥
translation_en: King Yudhishthira, son of Kunti, blew the Anantavijaya; Nakula and Sahadeva, the Sughosha and the Manipushpaka.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
काश्यश्च परमेष्वासः शिखण्डी च महारथः ।
धृष्टद्युम्नो विराटश्च सात्यकिश्चापराजितः ॥
translation_en: The king of Kashi, supreme archer, and Shikhandin, the great chariot-warrior; Dhrishtadyumna, Virata, and the unconquered Satyaki.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
द्रुपदो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्वशः पृथिवीपते ।
सौभद्रश्च महाबाहुः शङ्खान्दध्मुः पृथक्पृथक् ॥
translation_en: Drupada and the sons of Draupadi, and the strong-armed son of Subhadra—each on every side blew his conch, O lord of the earth.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
स घोषो धार्तराष्ट्राणां हृदयानि व्यदारयत् ।
नभश्च पृथिवीं चैव तुमुलोऽभ्यनुनादयन् ॥
translation_en: That tumultuous din, resounding through sky and earth, pierced the hearts of Dhritarashtra's sons.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
अथ व्यवस्थितान्दृष्ट्वा धार्तराष्ट्रान् कपिध्वजः ।
प्रवृत्ते शस्त्रसम्पाते धनुरुद्यम्य पाण्डवः ॥
translation_en: Then, seeing Dhritarashtra's sons arrayed, as the clash of weapons was about to begin, the Pandava whose banner bore the monkey took up his bow

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
हृषीकेशं तदा वाक्यमिदमाह महीपते ।
सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये रथं स्थापय मेऽच्युत ॥
translation_en:
and spoke these words to Hrishikesha, O king:  
"O Acyuta, place my chariot between the two armies,

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यावदेतान्निरीक्षेऽहं योद्धुकामानवस्थितान् ।
कैर्मया सह योद्धव्यमस्मिन् रणसमुद्यमे ॥
translation_en: that I may look upon these men standing eager to fight, with whom I must contend in this work of battle.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
योत्स्यमानानवेक्षेऽहं य एतेऽत्र समागताः ।
धार्तराष्ट्रस्य दुर्बुद्धेर्युद्धे प्रियचिकीर्षवः ॥
translation_en: I see them gathered here, ready to fight, seeking to please in battle the evil-minded son of Dhritarashtra.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
एवमुक्तो हृषीकेशो गुडाकेशेन भारत ।
सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये स्थापयित्वा रथोत्तमम् ॥
translation_en: Thus addressed by Gudakesha, O Bharata, Hrishikesha placed that finest of chariots between the two armies,

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
भीष्मद्रोणप्रमुखतः सर्वेषां च महीक्षिताम् ।
उवाच पार्थ पश्यैतान्समवेतान्कुरूनिति ॥
translation_en: before Bhishma and Drona and all the rulers of the earth, and said: "O Partha, behold these Kurus gathered here."

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
तत्रापश्यत्स्थितान्पार्थः पितॄनथ पितामहान् ।
आचार्यान्मातुलान्भ्रातॄन्पुत्रान्पौत्रान्सखींस्तथा ॥
translation_en: There Partha saw, standing before him, fathers and grandfathers, teachers, uncles, brothers, sons, grandsons, and friends as well;

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
श्वशुरान्सुहृदश्चैव सेनयोरुभयोरपि ।
तान्समीक्ष्य स कौन्तेयः सर्वान्बन्धूनवस्थितान् ॥
translation_en: fathers-in-law and companions too, in both armies. Seeing all these kinsmen arrayed,

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कृपया परयाविष्टो विषीदन्निदमब्रवीत् ।
दृष्ट्वेमं स्वजनं कृष्ण युयुत्सुं समुपस्थितम् ॥
translation_en:
the son of Kunti was overcome with deep compassion, and in sorrow he said:

"Seeing my own people, Krishna, gathered here eager to fight,

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सीदन्ति मम गात्राणि मुखं च परिशुष्यति ।
वेपथुश्च शरीरे मे रोमहर्षश्च जायते ॥
translation_en:
my limbs give way and my mouth goes dry;  
my body trembles and my hair stands on end.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
गाण्डीवं स्रंसते हस्तात्त्वक्चैव परिदह्यते ।
न च शक्नोम्यवस्थातुं भ्रमतीव च मे मनः ॥
translation_en:
The Gandiva slips from my hand, and my skin burns all over;  
I cannot stand steady, and my mind seems to reel.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
निमित्तानि च पश्यामि विपरीतानि केशव ।
न च श्रेयोऽनुपश्यामि हत्वा स्वजनमाहवे ॥
translation_en: And I see adverse omens, O Keshava; I foresee no good in killing my own people in battle.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न काङ्क्षे विजयं कृष्ण न च राज्यं सुखानि च ।
किं नो राज्येन गोविन्द किं भोगैर्जीवितेन वा ॥
translation_en:
I long for no victory, Krishna, nor kingdom, nor pleasures.  
What use is a kingdom to us, Govinda, or enjoyments, or even life?

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
येषामर्थे काङ्क्षितं नो राज्यं भोगाः सुखानि च ।
त इमेऽवस्थिता युद्धे प्राणांस्त्यक्त्वा धनानि च ॥
translation_en: Those for whose sake we would want kingdom, enjoyments, and pleasures stand here in battle, having renounced their lives and wealth:

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आचार्याः पितरः पुत्रास्तथैव च पितामहाः ।
मातुलाः श्वशुराः पौत्राः श्यालाः सम्बन्धिनस्तथा ॥
translation_en:
teachers, fathers, sons, and grandfathers;  
uncles, fathers-in-law, grandsons, brothers-in-law, and other kinsmen.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एतान्न हन्तुमिच्छामि घ्नतोऽपि मधुसूदन ।
अपि त्रैलोक्यराज्यस्य हेतोः किं नु महीकृते ॥
translation_en: These I would not wish to kill, O Madhusudana, even if killed myself—not even for the sovereignty of the three worlds, much less for the earth.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
निहत्य धार्तराष्ट्रान्नः का प्रीतिः स्याज्जनार्दन ।
पापमेवाश्रयेदस्मान्हत्वैतानाततायिनः ॥
translation_en: What joy would be ours, O Janardana, in killing Dhritarashtra's sons? Only sin would fall upon us were we to kill these aggressors.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तस्मान्नार्हा वयं हन्तुं धार्तराष्ट्रान्स्वबान्धवान् ।
स्वजनं हि कथं हत्वा सुखिनः स्याम माधव ॥
translation_en:
So we ought not to kill Dhritarashtra's sons, our own kinsmen;  
for how could we be happy, O Madhava, after killing our own people?

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यद्यप्येते न पश्यन्ति लोभोपहतचेतसः ।
कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं मित्रद्रोहे च पातकम् ॥
translation_en: Even if these men, their minds overcome by greed, see no fault in the ruin of a family and no crime in the betrayal of friends,

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कथं न ज्ञेयमस्माभिः पापादस्मान्निवर्तितुम् ।
कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं प्रपश्यद्भिर्जनार्दन ॥
translation_en: how should we not know to turn back from this sin, O Janardana, we who clearly see the fault in the ruin of a family?

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कुलक्षये प्रणश्यन्ति कुलधर्माः सनातनाः ।
धर्मे नष्टे कुलं कृत्स्नमधर्मोऽभिभवत्युत ॥
translation_en:
When a family is ruined, its ancient dharmas perish;  
and when dharma perishes, adharma overcomes the whole family.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अधर्माभिभवात्कृष्ण प्रदुष्यन्ति कुलस्त्रियः ।
स्त्रीषु दुष्टासु वार्ष्णेय जायते वर्णसङ्करः ॥
translation_en: When adharma prevails, O Krishna, the women of the family are corrupted; and when the women are corrupted, O Varshneya, there arises confusion of order.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सङ्करो नरकायैव कुलघ्नानां कुलस्य च ।
पतन्ति पितरो ह्येषां लुप्तपिण्डोदकक्रियाः ॥
translation_en: This confusion brings only hell for the family and for those who ruin it; their ancestors fall, deprived of the offerings of rice and water.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दोषैरेतैः कुलघ्नानां वर्णसङ्करकारकैः ।
उत्साद्यन्ते जातिधर्माः कुलधर्माश्च शाश्वताः ॥
translation_en: By these faults of the family-destroyers, which bring about confusion of varna, the eternal dharmas of community and family are undone.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
उत्सन्नकुलधर्माणां मनुष्याणां जनार्दन ।
नरके नियतं वासो भवतीत्यनुशुश्रुम ॥
translation_en: And we have heard, O Janardana, that for those whose family dharmas are destroyed, a dwelling in hell is assured.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अहो बत महत्पापं कर्तुं व्यवसिता वयम् ।
यद्राज्यसुखलोभेन हन्तुं स्वजनमुद्यताः ॥
translation_en: Alas, we are bent on committing a great sin: out of greed for the pleasures of a kingdom, we are prepared to kill our own people.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदि मामप्रतीकारमशस्त्रं शस्त्रपाणयः ।
धार्तराष्ट्रा रणे हन्युस्तन्मे क्षेमतरं भवेत् ॥
translation_en: It would be better for me if Dhritarashtra's sons, weapons in hand, were to kill me unresisting and unarmed in the battle.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
एवमुक्त्वार्जुनः सङ्ख्ये रथोपस्थ उपाविशत् ।
विसृज्य सशरं चापं शोकसंविग्नमानसः ॥
translation_en:
Having spoken thus on the field of battle, Arjuna sank down upon the seat of the chariot,  
casting away his bow and arrows, his mind stricken with grief.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
तं तथा कृपयाविष्टमश्रुपूर्णाकुलेक्षणम् ।
विषीदन्तमिदं वाक्यमुवाच मधुसूदनः ॥
translation_en: Seeing him thus overcome by compassion, his eyes troubled and brimming with tears, Madhusudana spoke these words to the despondent Arjuna.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कुतस्त्वा कश्मलमिदं विषमे समुपस्थितम् ।
अनार्यजुष्टमस्वर्ग्यमकीर्तिकरमर्जुन ॥
translation_en: Whence has this faintness come upon you in the hour of crisis, Arjuna? It is ignoble, unworthy of an honorable man, and leads neither to heaven nor to fame.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
क्लैब्यं मा स्म गमः पार्थ नैतत्त्वय्युपपद्यते ।
क्षुद्रं हृदयदौर्बल्यं त्यक्त्वोत्तिष्ठ परन्तप ॥
translation_en:
Do not give way to weakness, Partha; it does not become you.  
Cast off this base faint-heartedness and rise up, scorcher of foes.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कथं भीष्ममहं सङ्ख्ये द्रोणं च मधुसूदन ।
इषुभिः प्रतियोत्स्यामि पूजार्हावरिसूदन ॥
translation_en: How, O Madhusudana, shall I fight back with arrows against Bhishma and Drona, who are worthy of reverence, O destroyer of enemies?

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
गुरूनहत्वा हि महानुभावान्
श्रेयो भोक्तुं भैक्ष्यमपीह लोके ।
हत्वार्थकामांस्तु गुरूनिहैव
भुञ्जीय भोगान् रुधिरप्रदिग्धान् ॥
translation_en: Better to live in this world on alms than to kill these noble teachers. Were I to kill these teachers, though they seek their own gain, the very pleasures I enjoyed would be smeared with blood.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न चैतद्विद्मः कतरन्नो गरीयो
यद्वा जयेम यदि वा नो जयेयुः ।
यानेव हत्वा न जिजीविषाम-
स्तेऽवस्थिताः प्रमुखे धार्तराष्ट्राः ॥
translation_en: And we do not know which is better for us—that we should conquer them, or that they should conquer us; for the sons of Dhritarashtra stand before us, and having killed them we would have no wish to live.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कार्पण्यदोषोपहतस्वभावः
पृच्छामि त्वां धर्मसम्मूढचेताः ।
यच्छ्रेयः स्यान्निश्चितं ब्रूहि तन्मे
शिष्यस्तेऽहं शाधि मां त्वां प्रपन्नम् ॥
translation_en: My very being is stricken with the weakness of pity, my mind bewildered about my dharma. I ask you: tell me plainly what is best. I am your pupil; I take refuge in you—instruct me.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न हि प्रपश्यामि ममापनुद्याद्
यच्छोकमुच्छोषणमिन्द्रियाणाम् ।
अवाप्य भूमावसपत्नमृद्धं
राज्यं सुराणामपि चाधिपत्यम् ॥
translation_en: For I see nothing that could drive away the grief withering my senses, even were I to win unrivaled and prosperous dominion on earth, or lordship over the gods.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
एवमुक्त्वा हृषीकेशं गुडाकेशः परन्तप ।
न योत्स्य इति गोविन्दमुक्त्वा तूष्णीं बभूव ह ॥
translation_en: Having spoken thus to Hrishikesha, Gudakesha, scorcher of foes, said to Govinda, "I will not fight," and fell silent.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
तमुवाच हृषीकेशः प्रहसन्निव भारत ।
सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये विषीदन्तमिदं वचः ॥
translation_en: To him, despondent there between the two armies, Hrishikesha spoke, as if smiling, O Bharata, these words:

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अशोच्यानन्वशोचस्त्वं प्रज्ञावादांश्च भाषसे ।
गतासूनगतासूंश्च नानुशोचन्ति पण्डिताः ॥
translation_en: You grieve for those who should not be grieved for, yet you speak words of wisdom. The wise grieve neither for the dead nor for the living.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न त्वेवाहं जातु नासं न त्वं नेमे जनाधिपाः ।
न चैव न भविष्यामः सर्वे वयमतः परम् ॥
translation_en: Never was there a time when I was not, nor you, nor these lords of men; nor shall any of us cease to be hereafter.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
देहिनोऽस्मिन्यथा देहे कौमारं यौवनं जरा ।
तथा देहान्तरप्राप्तिर्धीरस्तत्र न मुह्यति ॥
translation_en: As the embodied self passes, within this body, through childhood, youth, and age, so it passes to another body; the steady are not bewildered by this.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मात्रास्पर्शास्तु कौन्तेय शीतोष्णसुखदुःखदाः ।
आगमापायिनोऽनित्यास्तांस्तितिक्षस्व भारत ॥
translation_en: The contacts of the senses with their objects, O son of Kunti, bring cold and heat, pleasure and pain; they come and go and do not last. Endure them, O Bharata.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यं हि न व्यथयन्त्येते पुरुषं पुरुषर्षभ ।
समदुःखसुखं धीरं सोऽमृतत्वाय कल्पते ॥
translation_en: The one whom these do not trouble, O best of men, who is steady, alike in pain and pleasure—he is fit for immortality.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नासतो विद्यते भावो नाभावो विद्यते सतः ।
उभयोरपि दृष्टोऽन्तस्त्वनयोस्तत्त्वदर्शिभिः ॥
translation_en:
Of the unreal there is no being; of the real there is no ceasing to be.  
The seers of truth have discerned the conclusion of both.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अविनाशि तु तद्विद्धि येन सर्वमिदं ततम् ।
विनाशमव्ययस्यास्य न कश्चित्कर्तुमर्हति ॥
translation_en: Know that to be indestructible by which all this is pervaded; none can bring about the destruction of this imperishable one.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अन्तवन्त इमे देहा नित्यस्योक्ताः शरीरिणः ।
अनाशिनोऽप्रमेयस्य तस्माद्युध्यस्व भारत ॥
translation_en: These bodies of the eternal embodied self are said to have an end, but the self itself is indestructible and beyond measure; therefore fight, O Bharata.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
य एनं वेत्ति हन्तारं यश्चैनं मन्यते हतम् ।
उभौ तौ न विजानीतो नायं हन्ति न हन्यते ॥
translation_en: He who thinks it the slayer, and he who thinks it slain—neither understands; it neither slays nor is slain.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचिन्
नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूयः ।
अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो
न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे ॥
translation_en:
It is never born and never dies;  
having once been, it never ceases to be.  
Unborn, eternal, everlasting, primeval,  
it is not killed when the body is killed.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
वेदाविनाशिनं नित्यं य एनमजमव्ययम् ।
कथं स पुरुषः पार्थ कं घातयति हन्ति कम् ॥
translation_en: He who knows it as indestructible, eternal, unborn, and unchanging—how can such a person kill, or cause to be killed, and whom, O Partha?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
वासांसि जीर्णानि यथा विहाय
नवानि गृह्णाति नरोऽपराणि ।
तथा शरीराणि विहाय जीर्णा-
न्यन्यानि संयाति नवानि देही ॥
translation_en: As a person casts off worn-out clothes and puts on others that are new, so the embodied self casts off worn-out bodies and takes on others that are new.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नैनं छिन्दन्ति शस्त्राणि नैनं दहति पावकः ।
न चैनं क्लेदयन्त्यापो न शोषयति मारुतः ॥
translation_en: Weapons do not cut it, fire does not burn it; water does not wet it, nor does wind dry it.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अच्छेद्योऽयमदाह्योऽयमक्लेद्योऽशोष्य एव च ।
नित्यः सर्वगतः स्थाणुरचलोऽयं सनातनः ॥
translation_en:
It cannot be cut, burned, wetted, or dried;  
it is eternal, all-pervading, firm, unmoving, and primeval.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अव्यक्तोऽयमचिन्त्योऽयमविकार्योऽयमुच्यते ।
तस्मादेवं विदित्वैनं नानुशोचितुमर्हसि ॥
translation_en: It is called unmanifest, unthinkable, and unchanging; therefore, knowing it to be such, you ought not to grieve.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अथ चैनं नित्यजातं नित्यं वा मन्यसे मृतम् ।
तथापि त्वं महाबाहो नैवं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥
translation_en: And even if you suppose it to be constantly born and constantly dying, even then, O mighty-armed one, you ought not to grieve.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
जातस्य हि ध्रुवो मृत्युर्ध्रुवं जन्म मृतस्य च ।
तस्मादपरिहार्येऽर्थे न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥
translation_en: For one who is born, death is certain; for one who dies, birth is certain. Therefore you ought not to grieve over what cannot be avoided.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अव्यक्तादीनि भूतानि व्यक्तमध्यानि भारत ।
अव्यक्तनिधनान्येव तत्र का परिदेवना ॥
translation_en: Beings are unmanifest in their beginnings, manifest in the middle, and unmanifest again at their end, O Bharata. What occasion for lament is there in this?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आश्चर्यवत्पश्यति कश्चिदेन-
माश्चर्यवद्वदति तथैव चान्यः ।
आश्चर्यवच्चैनमन्यः श‍ृणोति
श्रुत्वाप्येनं वेद न चैव कश्चित् ॥
translation_en: One sees it as a wonder, another speaks of it as a wonder, another hears of it as a wonder; yet even having heard, no one truly knows it.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
देही नित्यमवध्योऽयं देहे सर्वस्य भारत ।
तस्मात्सर्वाणि भूतानि न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥
translation_en: The embodied self within the body of every being is forever beyond harm, O Bharata; therefore you ought not to grieve for any being.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
स्वधर्ममपि चावेक्ष्य न विकम्पितुमर्हसि ।
धर्म्याद्धि युद्धाच्छ्रेयोऽन्यत्क्षत्रियस्य न विद्यते ॥
translation_en:
Considering, too, your own dharma, you ought not to waver;  
for a kshatriya there is no greater good than a battle in accord with dharma.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदृच्छया चोपपन्नं स्वर्गद्वारमपावृतम् ।
सुखिनः क्षत्रियाः पार्थ लभन्ते युद्धमीदृशम् ॥
translation_en: Happy the kshatriyas, O Partha, who meet with such a battle, come of itself like an open gate to heaven.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अथ चेत्त्वमिमं धर्म्यं सङ्ग्रामं न करिष्यसि ।
ततः स्वधर्मं कीर्तिं च हित्वा पापमवाप्स्यसि ॥
translation_en: But if you will not wage this rightful battle, then, forsaking your own dharma and your honor, you will fall into sin.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अकीर्तिं चापि भूतानि कथयिष्यन्ति तेऽव्ययाम् ।
सम्भावितस्य चाकीर्तिर्मरणादतिरिच्यते ॥
translation_en: People will tell of your lasting disgrace; and for one who has been honored, disgrace is worse than death.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
भयाद्रणादुपरतं मंस्यन्ते त्वां महारथाः ।
येषां च त्वं बहुमतो भूत्वा यास्यसि लाघवम् ॥
translation_en: The great chariot-warriors will think you withdrew from the battle out of fear, and you will fall in the esteem of those who held you high.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अवाच्यवादांश्च बहून्वदिष्यन्ति तवाहिताः ।
निन्दन्तस्तव सामर्थ्यं ततो दुःखतरं नु किम् ॥
translation_en: Your enemies will speak many unseemly words, scorning your strength. What could be more painful than that?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
हतो वा प्राप्स्यसि स्वर्गं जित्वा वा भोक्ष्यसे महीम् ।
तस्मादुत्तिष्ठ कौन्तेय युद्धाय कृतनिश्चयः ॥
translation_en: Slain, you will gain heaven; victorious, you will enjoy the earth. Therefore rise up, O son of Kunti, resolved to fight.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सुखदुःखे समे कृत्वा लाभालाभौ जयाजयौ ।
ततो युद्धाय युज्यस्व नैवं पापमवाप्स्यसि ॥
translation_en: Holding pleasure and pain alike, gain and loss, victory and defeat, gird yourself for battle; thus you will incur no sin.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एषा तेऽभिहिता साङ्ख्ये बुद्धिर्योगे त्विमां श‍ृणु ।
बुद्ध्या युक्तो यया पार्थ कर्मबन्धं प्रहास्यसि ॥
translation_en: This understanding has been declared to you according to Sankhya; now hear it as it bears on yoga. Yoked with this understanding, O Partha, you will cast off the bondage of karma.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नेहाभिक्रमनाशोऽस्ति प्रत्यवायो न विद्यते ।
स्वल्पमप्यस्य धर्मस्य त्रायते महतो भयात् ॥
translation_en: On this path no effort is lost and no harm is done; even a little of this dharma protects one from great fear.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिरेकेह कुरुनन्दन ।
बहुशाखा ह्यनन्ताश्च बुद्धयोऽव्यवसायिनाम् ॥
translation_en: Here there is but one resolute understanding, O joy of the Kurus; but the thoughts of the irresolute are many-branched and endless.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यामिमां पुष्पितां वाचं प्रवदन्त्यविपश्चितः ।
वेदवादरताः पार्थ नान्यदस्तीति वादिनः ॥
translation_en: This flowery speech is uttered by the unwise, who delight in the letter of the Vedas, O Partha, and declare there is nothing more;

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कामात्मानः स्वर्गपरा जन्मकर्मफलप्रदाम् ।
क्रियाविशेषबहुलां भोगैश्वर्यगतिं प्रति ॥
translation_en: full of desire and intent on heaven, they offer rites that yield rebirth as the fruit of action, abounding in special ceremonies aimed at enjoyment and power.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
भोगैश्वर्यप्रसक्तानां तयापहृतचेतसाम् ।
व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिः समाधौ न विधीयते ॥
translation_en: For those attached to enjoyment and power, whose minds are carried away by such talk, no resolute understanding is established in concentration.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
त्रैगुण्यविषया वेदा निस्त्रैगुण्यो भवार्जुन ।
निर्द्वन्द्वो नित्यसत्त्वस्थो निर्योगक्षेम आत्मवान् ॥
translation_en: The Vedas treat of the three gunas; be free of the three gunas, O Arjuna—beyond the pairs of opposites, ever poised in purity, indifferent to gain and safety, and possessed of the self.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यावानर्थ उदपाने सर्वतः सम्प्लुतोदके ।
तावान्सर्वेषु वेदेषु ब्राह्मणस्य विजानतः ॥
translation_en: As much use as there is in a well when water floods on every side, so much use is there in all the Vedas for the brahmin who knows.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन ।
मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि ॥
translation_en:
Your right is to action alone, never to its fruits.  
Let not the fruits of action be your motive, nor let attachment to inaction take hold in you.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
योगस्थः कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय ।
सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः समो भूत्वा समत्वं योग उच्यते ॥
translation_en: Established in yoga, perform your actions, abandoning attachment, O Dhananjaya, even-minded in success and failure; this evenness of mind is called yoga.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दूरेण ह्यवरं कर्म बुद्धियोगाद्धनञ्जय ।
बुद्धौ शरणमन्विच्छ कृपणाः फलहेतवः ॥
translation_en: Action is far inferior to the yoga of understanding, O Dhananjaya; take refuge in understanding. Pitiable are those who act for the sake of fruits.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बुद्धियुक्तो जहातीह उभे सुकृतदुष्कृते ।
तस्माद्योगाय युज्यस्व योगः कर्मसु कौशलम् ॥
translation_en: One yoked in understanding casts off here both good and evil deeds; therefore devote yourself to yoga—yoga is skill in action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कर्मजं बुद्धियुक्ता हि फलं त्यक्त्वा मनीषिणः ।
जन्मबन्धविनिर्मुक्ताः पदं गच्छन्त्यनामयम् ॥
translation_en: The wise, yoked in understanding, renounce the fruit born of action and, freed from the bondage of birth, reach the state beyond all ills.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदा ते मोहकलिलं बुद्धिर्व्यतितरिष्यति ।
तदा गन्तासि निर्वेदं श्रोतव्यस्य श्रुतस्य च ॥
translation_en: When your understanding has crossed beyond the thicket of delusion, then you will grow indifferent to what is yet to be heard and what has been heard.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
श्रुतिविप्रतिपन्ना ते यदा स्थास्यति निश्चला ।
समाधावचला बुद्धिस्तदा योगमवाप्स्यसि ॥
translation_en: When your understanding, once bewildered by scriptural lore, stands unmoving and steady in concentration, then you will attain yoga.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
स्थितप्रज्ञस्य का भाषा समाधिस्थस्य केशव ।
स्थितधीः किं प्रभाषेत किमासीत व्रजेत किम् ॥
translation_en: What is the mark of one whose wisdom is steady, settled in concentration, O Keshava? How does the steady-minded one speak, how sit, how move?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रजहाति यदा कामान्सर्वान्पार्थ मनोगतान् ।
आत्मन्येवात्मना तुष्टः स्थितप्रज्ञस्तदोच्यते ॥
translation_en: When a person puts away all the desires that enter the mind, O Partha, and is content within himself by himself, then he is called steady in wisdom.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दुःखेष्वनुद्विग्नमनाः सुखेषु विगतस्पृहः ।
वीतरागभयक्रोधः स्थितधीर्मुनिरुच्यते ॥
translation_en: He whose mind is untroubled in sorrows and free of craving amid pleasures, gone beyond longing, fear, and anger, is called a sage of steady mind.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यः सर्वत्रानभिस्नेहस्तत्तत्प्राप्य शुभाशुभम् ।
नाभिनन्दति न द्वेष्टि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
translation_en: He who is unattached on every side, who neither delights nor recoils at meeting good or ill—his wisdom stands firm.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदा संहरते चायं कूर्मोऽङ्गानीव सर्वशः ।
इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
translation_en: And when, like a tortoise drawing in its limbs, he withdraws his senses entirely from their objects, his wisdom stands firm.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
विषया विनिवर्तन्ते निराहारस्य देहिनः ।
रसवर्जं रसोऽप्यस्य परं दृष्ट्वा निवर्तते ॥
translation_en: The objects of sense fall away from the embodied one who abstains, though a taste for them remains; even that taste falls away when he sees the Supreme.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यततो ह्यपि कौन्तेय पुरुषस्य विपश्चितः ।
इन्द्रियाणि प्रमाथीनि हरन्ति प्रसभं मनः ॥
translation_en: For even in one who strives, O son of Kunti, who sees clearly, the turbulent senses carry off the mind by force.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तानि सर्वाणि संयम्य युक्त आसीत मत्परः ।
वशे हि यस्येन्द्रियाणि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
translation_en: Holding all these in check, let him sit yoked, intent on me; for one whose senses are mastered, wisdom stands firm.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ध्यायतो विषयान्पुंसः सङ्गस्तेषूपजायते ।
सङ्गात्सञ्जायते कामः कामात्क्रोधोऽभिजायते ॥
translation_en:
In a person who dwells on the objects of sense, attachment to them is born;  
from attachment springs desire, and from desire, anger.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
क्रोधाद्भवति सम्मोहः सम्मोहात्स्मृतिविभ्रमः ।
स्मृतिभ्रंशाद् बुद्धिनाशो बुद्धिनाशात्प्रणश्यति ॥
translation_en:
From anger comes delusion; from delusion, the wandering of memory;  
from the loss of memory, the ruin of understanding; and from the ruin of understanding, the person perishes.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
रागद्वेषविमुक्तैस्तु विषयानिन्द्रियैश्चरन् ।
आत्मवश्यैर्विधेयात्मा प्रसादमधिगच्छति ॥
translation_en: But one who moves among the objects of sense with senses freed from attachment and aversion, mastered and under his own control, attains serenity.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रसादे सर्वदुःखानां हानिरस्योपजायते ।
प्रसन्नचेतसो ह्याशु बुद्धिः पर्यवतिष्ठते ॥
translation_en: In serenity all his sorrows are dissolved; for the mind of the serene soon grows steady.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नास्ति बुद्धिरयुक्तस्य न चायुक्तस्य भावना ।
न चाभावयतः शान्तिरशान्तस्य कुतः सुखम् ॥
translation_en:
For the unsteady there is no understanding, and for the unsteady, no meditation;  
without meditation there is no peace, and for one without peace, how can there be happiness?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इन्द्रियाणां हि चरतां यन्मनोऽनुविधीयते ।
तदस्य हरति प्रज्ञां वायुर्नावमिवाम्भसि ॥
translation_en: For when the mind runs after the roving senses, it carries off one's wisdom as the wind carries off a boat upon the water.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तस्माद्यस्य महाबाहो निगृहीतानि सर्वशः ।
इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
translation_en: Therefore, O mighty-armed one, he whose senses are wholly withdrawn from their objects—his wisdom stands firm.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
या निशा सर्वभूतानां तस्यां जागर्ति संयमी ।
यस्यां जाग्रति भूतानि सा निशा पश्यतो मुनेः ॥
translation_en:
What is night for all beings is the waking of the disciplined one;  
where beings are awake, there it is night for the sage who sees.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आपूर्यमाणमचलप्रतिष्ठं
समुद्रमापः प्रविशन्ति यद्वत् ।
तद्वत्कामा यं प्रविशन्ति सर्वे
स शान्तिमाप्नोति न कामकामी ॥
translation_en: As the waters flow into the ocean, which fills yet stays unmoved, so all desires flow into him who attains peace—not the one who hankers after desires.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
विहाय कामान्यः सर्वान्पुमांश्चरति निःस्पृहः ।
निर्ममो निरहङ्कारः स शान्तिमधिगच्छति ॥
translation_en: The person who, abandoning all desires, moves about free of longing, without 'mine' and without ego, attains peace.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एषा ब्राह्मी स्थितिः पार्थ नैनां प्राप्य विमुह्यति ।
स्थित्वास्यामन्तकालेऽपि ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृच्छति ॥
translation_en:
This is the state of Brahman, O Partha; reaching it, one is no longer deluded.  
Established in it even at the hour of death, one attains the nirvana of Brahman.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ज्यायसी चेत्कर्मणस्ते मता बुद्धिर्जनार्दन ।
तत्किं कर्मणि घोरे मां नियोजयसि केशव ॥
translation_en: Janardana, if you hold understanding to be superior to action, then why, Keshava, do you urge me to this terrible deed?

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
व्यामिश्रेणेव वाक्येन बुद्धिं मोहयसीव मे ।
तदेकं वद निश्चित्य येन श्रेयोऽहमाप्नुयाम् ॥
translation_en: With these seemingly conflicting words you bewilder my understanding. Tell me decisively the one thing by which I may reach the highest good.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
लोकेऽस्मिन् द्विविधा निष्ठा पुरा प्रोक्ता मयानघ ।
ज्ञानयोगेन साङ्ख्यानां कर्मयोगेन योगिनाम् ॥
translation_en: In this world, O blameless one, a twofold path was taught by me of old: the yoga of knowledge for the followers of Sankhya, and the yoga of action for the yogins.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न कर्मणामनारम्भान्नैष्कर्म्यं पुरुषोऽश्नुते ।
न च संन्यसनादेव सिद्धिं समधिगच्छति ॥
translation_en: Not by abstaining from actions does a person attain freedom from action, nor by renunciation alone does one reach perfection.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न हि कश्चित्क्षणमपि जातु तिष्ठत्यकर्मकृत् ।
कार्यते ह्यवशः कर्म सर्वः प्रकृतिजैर्गुणैः ॥
translation_en: For no one ever remains, even for a moment, without performing action; everyone is driven to act, helpless, by the gunas born of prakriti.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कर्मेन्द्रियाणि संयम्य य आस्ते मनसा स्मरन् ।
इन्द्रियार्थान्विमूढात्मा मिथ्याचारः स उच्यते ॥
translation_en: One who restrains the organs of action yet sits brooding in the mind on the objects of sense, his self deluded, is called a hypocrite.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यस्त्विन्द्रियाणि मनसा नियम्यारभतेऽर्जुन ।
कर्मेन्द्रियैः कर्मयोगमसक्तः स विशिष्यते ॥
translation_en: But one who, controlling the senses with the mind, undertakes through the organs of action the yoga of action, unattached—he excels, O Arjuna.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नियतं कुरु कर्म त्वं कर्म ज्यायो ह्यकर्मणः ।
शरीरयात्रापि च ते न प्रसिद्ध्येदकर्मणः ॥
translation_en: Perform your appointed action, for action is better than inaction; even the upkeep of your body could not be accomplished by inaction.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यज्ञार्थात्कर्मणोऽन्यत्र लोकोऽयं कर्मबन्धनः ।
तदर्थं कर्म कौन्तेय मुक्तसङ्गः समाचर ॥
translation_en: This world is bound by action, except when action is done as yajna; therefore, O son of Kunti, perform your action for that end, free from attachment.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सहयज्ञाः प्रजाः सृष्ट्वा पुरोवाच प्रजापतिः ।
अनेन प्रसविष्यध्वमेष वोऽस्त्विष्टकामधुक् ॥
translation_en:
Having created humankind together with yajna, Prajapati said of old:  
"By this you shall increase; let it be the cow that grants your desires.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
देवान्भावयतानेन ते देवा भावयन्तु वः ।
परस्परं भावयन्तः श्रेयः परमवाप्स्यथ ॥
translation_en: By this nourish the gods, and let the gods nourish you; nourishing one another, you shall attain the highest good.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इष्टान्भोगान्हि वो देवा दास्यन्ते यज्ञभाविताः ।
तैर्दत्तानप्रदायैभ्यो यो भुङ्क्ते स्तेन एव सः ॥
translation_en:
Nourished by yajna, the gods will grant you the enjoyments you desire;  
but one who enjoys their gifts without offering back to them is nothing but a thief.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यज्ञशिष्टाशिनः सन्तो मुच्यन्ते सर्वकिल्बिषैः ।
भुञ्जते ते त्वघं पापा ये पचन्त्यात्मकारणात् ॥
translation_en: The good, who eat the remnants of yajna, are freed from all sins; but the wicked, who cook only for themselves, eat sin.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अन्नाद्भवन्ति भूतानि पर्जन्यादन्नसम्भवः ।
यज्ञाद्भवति पर्जन्यो यज्ञः कर्मसमुद्भवः ॥
translation_en:
From food beings come to be; from rain comes food;  
from yajna comes rain; and yajna is born of action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कर्म ब्रह्मोद्भवं विद्धि ब्रह्माक्षरसमुद्भवम् ।
तस्मात्सर्वगतं ब्रह्म नित्यं यज्ञे प्रतिष्ठितम् ॥
translation_en: Know that action springs from Brahman, and Brahman from the imperishable; therefore the all-pervading Brahman is ever present in yajna.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एवं प्रवर्तितं चक्रं नानुवर्तयतीह यः ।
अघायुरिन्द्रियारामो मोघं पार्थ स जीवति ॥
translation_en: He who does not follow here the wheel thus set in motion, malicious and delighting in the senses, lives in vain, O Partha.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यस्त्वात्मरतिरेव स्यादात्मतृप्तश्च मानवः ।
आत्मन्येव च सन्तुष्टस्तस्य कार्यं न विद्यते ॥
translation_en: But for the one who delights in the self alone, content with the self and satisfied in the self, there is nothing that must be done.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नैव तस्य कृतेनार्थो नाकृतेनेह कश्चन ।
न चास्य सर्वभूतेषु कश्चिदर्थव्यपाश्रयः ॥
translation_en: For him there is no purpose in action done or left undone here, nor does he depend on any being for any end.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तस्मादसक्तः सततं कार्यं कर्म समाचर ।
असक्तो ह्याचरन्कर्म परमाप्नोति पूरुषः ॥
translation_en: Therefore, ever unattached, perform the action that must be done; for the person who works unattached attains the highest.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कर्मणैव हि संसिद्धिमास्थिता जनकादयः ।
लोकसङ्ग्रहमेवापि सम्पश्यन्कर्तुमर्हसि ॥
translation_en: It was by action alone that Janaka and others attained perfection; and with an eye to the holding-together of the world, you too should act.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यद्यदाचरति श्रेष्ठस्तत्तदेवेतरो जनः ।
स यत्प्रमाणं कुरुते लोकस्तदनुवर्तते ॥
translation_en:
Whatever the eminent person does, that the rest will do;  
whatever standard he sets, the world follows it.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न मे पार्थास्ति कर्तव्यं त्रिषु लोकेषु किञ्चन ।
नानवाप्तमवाप्तव्यं वर्त एव च कर्मणि ॥
translation_en: There is nothing in the three worlds I need to do, O Partha, nor anything unattained that I must gain; yet I remain engaged in action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदि ह्यहं न वर्तेयं जातु कर्मण्यतन्द्रितः ।
मम वर्त्मानुवर्तन्ते मनुष्याः पार्थ सर्वशः ॥
translation_en: For if I did not remain ever tireless in action, O Partha, people everywhere would follow my path.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
उत्सीदेयुरिमे लोका न कुर्यां कर्म चेदहम् ।
सङ्करस्य च कर्ता स्यामुपहन्यामिमाः प्रजाः ॥
translation_en: These worlds would fall to ruin if I did not perform action; I would be the maker of confusion and would destroy these creatures.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सक्ताः कर्मण्यविद्वांसो यथा कुर्वन्ति भारत ।
कुर्याद्विद्वांस्तथासक्तश्चिकीर्षुर्लोकसङ्ग्रहम् ॥
translation_en: As the ignorant act with attachment to action, O Bharata, so should the wise act, but unattached, seeking the welfare of the world.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न बुद्धिभेदं जनयेदज्ञानां कर्मसङ्गिनाम् ।
जोषयेत्सर्वकर्माणि विद्वान्युक्तः समाचरन् ॥
translation_en: Let the wise not unsettle the minds of the ignorant who are attached to action; acting with discipline, he should make all actions attractive to them.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रकृतेः क्रियमाणानि गुणैः कर्माणि सर्वशः ।
अहङ्कारविमूढात्मा कर्ताहमिति मन्यते ॥
translation_en:
Actions are in every way performed by the gunas of prakriti;  
one whose mind is deluded by ego thinks, "I am the doer."

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तत्त्ववित्तु महाबाहो गुणकर्मविभागयोः ।
गुणा गुणेषु वर्तन्त इति मत्वा न सज्जते ॥
translation_en: But one who knows the truth, O mighty-armed one, about the divisions of guna and action is not bound, knowing that it is the gunas that act among the gunas.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रकृतेर्गुणसम्मूढाः सज्जन्ते गुणकर्मसु ।
तानकृत्स्नविदो मन्दान्कृत्स्नविन्न विचालयेत् ॥
translation_en: Those deluded by the gunas of prakriti grow attached to the actions of the gunas; let the one who knows the whole not unsettle the dull who do not.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मयि सर्वाणि कर्माणि संन्यस्याध्यात्मचेतसा ।
निराशीर्निर्ममो भूत्वा युध्यस्व विगतज्वरः ॥
translation_en: Surrendering all actions to me, with mind fixed on the self, free of longing and of any sense of 'mine,' fight, your fever gone.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ये मे मतमिदं नित्यमनुतिष्ठन्ति मानवाः ।
श्रद्धावन्तोऽनसूयन्तो मुच्यन्ते तेऽपि कर्मभिः ॥
translation_en: Those who constantly follow this teaching of mine, full of faith and free from cavil, are released even from their karma.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ये त्वेतदभ्यसूयन्तो नानुतिष्ठन्ति मे मतम् ।
सर्वज्ञानविमूढांस्तान्विद्धि नष्टानचेतसः ॥
translation_en: But those who, finding fault, do not follow this teaching of mine—know them to be deluded in all knowledge, mindless, and lost.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सदृशं चेष्टते स्वस्याः प्रकृतेर्ज्ञानवानपि ।
प्रकृतिं यान्ति भूतानि निग्रहः किं करिष्यति ॥
translation_en:
Even the wise act according to their own nature;  
beings follow their nature—what can restraint accomplish?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इन्द्रियस्येन्द्रियस्यार्थे रागद्वेषौ व्यवस्थितौ ।
तयोर्न वशमागच्छेत्तौ ह्यस्य परिपन्थिनौ ॥
translation_en: In each sense, attraction and aversion are seated toward its object; let no one fall under their power, for they are one's enemies.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुणः परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात् ।
स्वधर्मे निधनं श्रेयः परधर्मो भयावहः ॥
translation_en:
Better one's own dharma, imperfectly done, than another's dharma well performed.  
Better to die in one's own dharma; another's dharma brings danger.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अथ केन प्रयुक्तोऽयं पापं चरति पूरुषः ।
अनिच्छन्नपि वार्ष्णेय बलादिव नियोजितः ॥
translation_en: Then by what is a person driven to do evil, O Varshneya, even against his will, as if compelled by force?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
काम एष क्रोध एष रजोगुणसमुद्भवः ।
महाशनो महापाप्मा विद्ध्येनमिह वैरिणम् ॥
translation_en:
It is desire, it is anger, born of the guna of rajas—  
all-devouring, deeply harmful; know it here to be the enemy.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
धूमेनाव्रियते वह्निर्यथादर्शो मलेन च ।
यथोल्बेनावृतो गर्भस्तथा तेनेदमावृतम् ॥
translation_en: As fire is veiled by smoke, a mirror by dust, and an embryo by the womb, so is this veiled by that.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आवृतं ज्ञानमेतेन ज्ञानिनो नित्यवैरिणा ।
कामरूपेण कौन्तेय दुष्पूरेणानलेन च ॥
translation_en: Knowledge is veiled, O son of Kunti, by this constant enemy of the wise—desire, an insatiable fire.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इन्द्रियाणि मनो बुद्धिरस्याधिष्ठानमुच्यते ।
एतैर्विमोहयत्येष ज्ञानमावृत्य देहिनम् ॥
translation_en:
The senses, the mind, and the understanding are said to be its seat;  
through these it veils knowledge and deludes the embodied one.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तस्मात्त्वमिन्द्रियाण्यादौ नियम्य भरतर्षभ ।
पाप्मानं प्रजहि ह्येनं ज्ञानविज्ञाननाशनम् ॥
translation_en: Therefore, O best of the Bharatas, first master your senses, then strike down this evil thing, the destroyer of knowledge and discernment.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इन्द्रियाणि पराण्याहुरिन्द्रियेभ्यः परं मनः ।
मनसस्तु परा बुद्धिर्यो बुद्धेः परतस्तु सः ॥
translation_en:
The senses, they say, are high; higher than the senses is the mind;  
higher than the mind is the understanding; and higher than the understanding is the self.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एवं बुद्धेः परं बुद्ध्वा संस्तभ्यात्मानमात्मना ।
जहि शत्रुं महाबाहो कामरूपं दुरासदम् ॥
translation_en: Thus knowing it to be higher than the understanding, and steadying the self by the self, slay the enemy, O mighty-armed one, in the form of desire, so hard to subdue.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इमं विवस्वते योगं प्रोक्तवानहमव्ययम् ।
विवस्वान्मनवे प्राह मनुरिक्ष्वाकवेऽब्रवीत् ॥
translation_en: I taught this imperishable yoga to Vivasvan; Vivasvan told it to Manu, and Manu told it to Ikshvaku.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एवं परम्पराप्राप्तमिमं राजर्षयो विदुः ।
स कालेनेह महता योगो नष्टः परन्तप ॥
translation_en: Thus handed down in succession, the royal sages knew it; but over the long course of time, O scorcher of foes, this yoga was lost here on earth.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
स एवायं मया तेऽद्य योगः प्रोक्तः पुरातनः ।
भक्तोऽसि मे सखा चेति रहस्यं ह्येतदुत्तमम् ॥
translation_en: It is this very same ancient yoga that I have declared to you today, for you are my devotee and my friend; for this is the supreme secret.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अपरं भवतो जन्म परं जन्म विवस्वतः ।
कथमेतद्विजानीयां त्वमादौ प्रोक्तवानिति ॥
translation_en: Your birth was later, the birth of Vivasvan earlier; how then am I to understand that you taught it in the beginning?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बहूनि मे व्यतीतानि जन्मानि तव चार्जुन ।
तान्यहं वेद सर्वाणि न त्वं वेत्थ परन्तप ॥
translation_en:
Many births of mine have passed, and yours as well, O Arjuna;  
I know them all, but you do not, O scorcher of foes.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अजोऽपि सन्नव्ययात्मा भूतानामीश्वरोऽपि सन् ।
प्रकृतिं स्वामधिष्ठाय सम्भवाम्यात्ममायया ॥
translation_en: Though I am unborn and imperishable, and the lord of beings, yet, governing my own prakriti, I come into being through my own maya.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत ।
अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदात्मानं सृजाम्यहम् ॥
translation_en: Whenever dharma declines, O Bharata, and adharma rises up, then I send forth myself.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
परित्राणाय साधूनां विनाशाय च दुष्कृताम् ।
धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय सम्भवामि युगे युगे ॥
translation_en: For the protection of the good and the destruction of the wicked, and to set dharma firmly in place, I come into being age after age.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
जन्म कर्म च मे दिव्यमेवं यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः ।
त्यक्त्वा देहं पुनर्जन्म नैति मामेति सोऽर्जुन ॥
translation_en: He who truly knows my divine birth and action, on leaving the body is not born again but comes to me, Arjuna.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
वीतरागभयक्रोधा मन्मया मामुपाश्रिताः ।
बहवो ज्ञानतपसा पूता मद्भावमागताः ॥
translation_en: Freed from passion, fear, and anger, filled with me and taking refuge in me, many, purified by the austerity of knowledge, have attained my state of being.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ये यथा मां प्रपद्यन्ते तांस्तथैव भजाम्यहम् ।
मम वर्त्मानुवर्तन्ते मनुष्याः पार्थ सर्वशः ॥
translation_en: As they approach me, so do I receive them; people follow my path in every way, O Partha.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
काङ्क्षन्तः कर्मणां सिद्धिं यजन्त इह देवताः ।
क्षिप्रं हि मानुषे लोके सिद्धिर्भवति कर्मजा ॥
translation_en: Desiring success in their works, people here worship the gods; for in the human world success born of action comes quickly.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
चातुर्वर्ण्यं मया सृष्टं गुणकर्मविभागशः ।
तस्य कर्तारमपि मां विद्ध्यकर्तारमव्ययम् ॥
translation_en: The four varnas were brought forth by me, apportioned by guna and action. Though I am their maker, know me as the one who does not act, the changeless.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न मां कर्माणि लिम्पन्ति न मे कर्मफले स्पृहा ।
इति मां योऽभिजानाति कर्मभिर्न स बध्यते ॥
translation_en: Actions do not taint me, nor do I crave the fruits of action; one who knows me thus is not bound by actions.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एवं ज्ञात्वा कृतं कर्म पूर्वैरपि मुमुक्षुभिः ।
कुरु कर्मैव तस्मात्त्वं पूर्वैः पूर्वतरं कृतम् ॥
translation_en: Knowing this, the ancient seekers of liberation also performed action; therefore do action, as the ancients did of old.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
किं कर्म किमकर्मेति कवयोऽप्यत्र मोहिताः ।
तत्ते कर्म प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वा मोक्ष्यसेऽशुभात् ॥
translation_en: What is action? What is inaction? On this even the wise are bewildered. I shall declare to you the action by knowing which you will be freed from ill.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कर्मणो ह्यपि बोद्धव्यं बोद्धव्यं च विकर्मणः ।
अकर्मणश्च बोद्धव्यं गहना कर्मणो गतिः ॥
translation_en: One must understand action, and must understand wrong action, and must understand inaction too; deep is the way of karma.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कर्मण्यकर्म यः पश्येदकर्मणि च कर्म यः ।
स बुद्धिमान्मनुष्येषु स युक्तः कृत्स्नकर्मकृत् ॥
translation_en: He who sees inaction in action, and action in inaction, is wise among men; he is disciplined, a doer of every action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यस्य सर्वे समारम्भाः कामसङ्कल्पवर्जिताः ।
ज्ञानाग्निदग्धकर्माणं तमाहुः पण्डितं बुधाः ॥
translation_en: He whose every undertaking is free of desire and design, whose actions are burned up in the fire of knowledge—him the wise call learned.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
त्यक्त्वा कर्मफलासङ्गं नित्यतृप्तो निराश्रयः ।
कर्मण्यभिप्रवृत्तोऽपि नैव किञ्चित्करोति सः ॥
translation_en: Having given up attachment to the fruits of action, ever content and dependent on nothing, he does nothing at all, even while fully engaged in action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
निराशीर्यतचित्तात्मा त्यक्तसर्वपरिग्रहः ।
शारीरं केवलं कर्म कुर्वन्नाप्नोति किल्बिषम् ॥
translation_en: Free of expectation, mind and self controlled, abandoning all possessions, he performs action with the body alone and incurs no fault.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदृच्छालाभसन्तुष्टो द्वन्द्वातीतो विमत्सरः ।
समः सिद्धावसिद्धौ च कृत्वापि न निबध्यते ॥
translation_en: Content with whatever comes unsought, beyond the pairs of opposites, free of envy, even in success and failure, he is not bound even when he acts.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
गतसङ्गस्य मुक्तस्य ज्ञानावस्थितचेतसः ।
यज्ञायाचरतः कर्म समग्रं प्रविलीयते ॥
translation_en: For one who is unattached and free, whose mind is settled in knowledge, who acts as yajna—his action dissolves entirely.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ब्रह्मार्पणं ब्रह्म हविर्ब्रह्माग्नौ ब्रह्मणा हुतम् ।
ब्रह्मैव तेन गन्तव्यं ब्रह्मकर्मसमाधिना ॥
translation_en: The act of offering is Brahman, the oblation is Brahman, poured by Brahman into the fire of Brahman; Brahman is what he reaches who is absorbed in the action that is Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दैवमेवापरे यज्ञं योगिनः पर्युपासते ।
ब्रह्माग्नावपरे यज्ञं यज्ञेनैवोपजुह्वति ॥
translation_en: Some yogins offer sacrifice to the gods alone; others offer the very sacrifice as an offering into the fire of Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
श्रोत्रादीनीन्द्रियाण्यन्ये संयमाग्निषु जुह्वति ।
शब्दादीन्विषयानन्य इन्द्रियाग्निषु जुह्वति ॥
translation_en: Some offer hearing and the other senses into the fires of restraint; others offer sound and the other objects of sense into the fires of the senses.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वाणीन्द्रियकर्माणि प्राणकर्माणि चापरे ।
आत्मसंयमयोगाग्नौ जुह्वति ज्ञानदीपिते ॥
translation_en: And others offer all the workings of the senses and the workings of the breath into the fire of self-control, kindled by knowledge.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
द्रव्ययज्ञास्तपोयज्ञा योगयज्ञास्तथापरे ।
स्वाध्यायज्ञानयज्ञाश्च यतयः संशितव्रताः ॥
translation_en: Others again make their sacrifice with wealth, with austerity, with yoga, and with study and knowledge—ascetics of keen vows.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अपाने जुह्वति प्राणं प्राणेऽपानं तथापरे ।
प्राणापानगती रुद्ध्वा प्राणायामपरायणाः ॥
translation_en: Others offer the outgoing breath into the incoming, and the incoming into the outgoing, restraining the course of both, intent on breath-control;

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अपरे नियताहाराः प्राणान्प्राणेषु जुह्वति ।
सर्वेऽप्येते यज्ञविदो यज्ञक्षपितकल्मषाः ॥
translation_en: and others, of measured diet, offer the breaths into the breaths. All these know what sacrifice is, and by sacrifice their faults are wiped away.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यज्ञशिष्टामृतभुजो यान्ति ब्रह्म सनातनम् ।
नायं लोकोऽस्त्ययज्ञस्य कुतोऽन्यः कुरुसत्तम ॥
translation_en: Those who eat the nectar left from yajna go to the eternal Brahman. This world is not for the one who offers no sacrifice—how then the next, O best of the Kurus?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एवं बहुविधा यज्ञा वितता ब्रह्मणो मुखे ।
कर्मजान्विद्धि तान्सर्वानेवं ज्ञात्वा विमोक्ष्यसे ॥
translation_en: Thus sacrifices of many kinds are spread out before Brahman. Know them all to be born of action; knowing this, you will be freed.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
श्रेयान्द्रव्यमयाद्यज्ञाज्ज्ञानयज्ञः परन्तप ।
सर्वं कर्माखिलं पार्थ ज्ञाने परिसमाप्यते ॥
translation_en: Better than any sacrifice of goods is the sacrifice of knowledge, O scorcher of foes; for all action, without exception, culminates in knowledge, O Partha.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तद्विद्धि प्रणिपातेन परिप्रश्नेन सेवया ।
उपदेक्ष्यन्ति ते ज्ञानं ज्ञानिनस्तत्त्वदर्शिनः ॥
translation_en: Learn this by reverence, by inquiry, and by service; the knowers who see the truth will impart that knowledge to you.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यज्ज्ञात्वा न पुनर्मोहमेवं यास्यसि पाण्डव ।
येन भूतान्यशेषेण द्रक्ष्यस्यात्मन्यथो मयि ॥
translation_en: Knowing it, O Pandava, you will not fall again into such delusion; by it you will see all beings, without exception, within yourself, and then within me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अपि चेदसि पापेभ्यः सर्वेभ्यः पापकृत्तमः ।
सर्वं ज्ञानप्लवेनैव वृजिनं सन्तरिष्यसि ॥
translation_en: Even if you were the worst sinner among all sinners, you would cross over all wrongdoing by the boat of knowledge alone.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यथैधांसि समिद्धोऽग्निर्भस्मसात्कुरुतेऽर्जुन ।
ज्ञानाग्निः सर्वकर्माणि भस्मसात्कुरुते तथा ॥
translation_en: As a kindled fire turns its fuel to ashes, O Arjuna, so the fire of knowledge turns all karma to ashes.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न हि ज्ञानेन सदृशं पवित्रमिह विद्यते ।
तत्स्वयं योगसंसिद्धः कालेनात्मनि विन्दति ॥
translation_en: There is no purifier here equal to knowledge; one perfected in yoga finds it of itself, in time, within the self.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
श्रद्धावाँल्लभते ज्ञानं तत्परः संयतेन्द्रियः ।
ज्ञानं लब्ध्वा परां शान्तिमचिरेणाधिगच्छति ॥
translation_en: The one full of faith, intent on it, his senses mastered, attains knowledge; and having attained knowledge, he soon reaches supreme peace.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अज्ञश्चाश्रद्दधानश्च संशयात्मा विनश्यति ।
नायं लोकोऽस्ति न परो न सुखं संशयात्मनः ॥
translation_en: But the ignorant and faithless, the doubting self, comes to ruin; neither this world nor the next nor any happiness is for the doubting self.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
योगसंन्यस्तकर्माणं ज्ञानसञ्छिन्नसंशयम् ।
आत्मवन्तं न कर्माणि निबध्नन्ति धनञ्जय ॥
translation_en: Actions do not bind the one who has renounced action through yoga, whose doubts are cut away by knowledge, who is possessed of the self, O Dhananjaya.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तस्मादज्ञानसम्भूतं हृत्स्थं ज्ञानासिनात्मनः ।
छित्त्वैनं संशयं योगमातिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ भारत ॥
translation_en: Therefore, with the sword of knowledge cut away this doubt in your heart, born of ignorance; take up yoga and rise, O Bharata.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
संन्यासं कर्मणां कृष्ण पुनर्योगं च शंससि ।
यच्छ्रेय एतयोरेकं तन्मे ब्रूहि सुनिश्चितम् ॥
translation_en: O Krishna, you praise the renunciation of actions, and again yoga; tell me for certain which of the two is better.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
संन्यासः कर्मयोगश्च निःश्रेयसकरावुभौ ।
तयोस्तु कर्मसंन्यासात्कर्मयोगो विशिष्यते ॥
translation_en: Both renunciation and the yoga of action lead to the highest good; but of the two, the yoga of action excels the renunciation of action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ज्ञेयः स नित्यसंन्यासी यो न द्वेष्टि न काङ्क्षति ।
निर्द्वन्द्वो हि महाबाहो सुखं बन्धात्प्रमुच्यते ॥
translation_en: Know him to be a constant renunciant who neither hates nor desires; free of the pairs of opposites, O mighty-armed one, he is released from bondage with ease.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
साङ्ख्ययोगौ पृथग्बालाः प्रवदन्ति न पण्डिताः ।
एकमप्यास्थितः सम्यगुभयोर्विन्दते फलम् ॥
translation_en: It is the childish, not the wise, who speak of Sankhya and yoga as different; one rightly established in even one of them gains the fruit of both.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यत्साङ्ख्यैः प्राप्यते स्थानं तद्योगैरपि गम्यते ।
एकं साङ्ख्यं च योगं च यः पश्यति स पश्यति ॥
translation_en:
The state reached by Sankhya is reached by yoga as well;  
he who sees Sankhya and yoga as one truly sees.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
संन्यासस्तु महाबाहो दुःखमाप्तुमयोगतः ।
योगयुक्तो मुनिर्ब्रह्म नचिरेणाधिगच्छति ॥
translation_en: But renunciation is hard to attain without yoga, O mighty-armed one; the sage yoked in yoga soon reaches Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
योगयुक्तो विशुद्धात्मा विजितात्मा जितेन्द्रियः ।
सर्वभूतात्मभूतात्मा कुर्वन्नपि न लिप्यते ॥
translation_en: Yoked in yoga, his self purified, self-mastered, his senses subdued, his self become the self of all beings—he is not tainted even while acting.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नैव किञ्चित्करोमीति युक्तो मन्येत तत्त्ववित् ।
पश्यञ्श‍ृण्वन्स्पृशञ्जिघ्रन्नश्नन्गच्छन्स्वपञ्श्वसन् ॥
translation_en: "I do nothing at all," the disciplined knower of truth would hold, even while seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, moving, sleeping, breathing,

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रलपन्विसृजन्गृह्णन्नुन्मिषन्निमिषन्नपि ।
इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेषु वर्तन्त इति धारयन् ॥
translation_en: speaking, releasing, grasping, opening and closing the eyes—convinced that it is only the senses moving among the objects of sense.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ब्रह्मण्याधाय कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा करोति यः ।
लिप्यते न स पापेन पद्मपत्रमिवाम्भसा ॥
translation_en: One who acts, offering his actions to Brahman and abandoning attachment, is untouched by sin, as a lotus leaf by water.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कायेन मनसा बुद्ध्या केवलैरिन्द्रियैरपि ।
योगिनः कर्म कुर्वन्ति सङ्गं त्यक्त्वात्मशुद्धये ॥
translation_en: With body, mind, understanding, and the senses alone, the yogins act, abandoning attachment, for the purifying of the self.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
युक्तः कर्मफलं त्यक्त्वा शान्तिमाप्नोति नैष्ठिकीम् ।
अयुक्तः कामकारेण फले सक्तो निबध्यते ॥
translation_en: The disciplined one, abandoning the fruit of action, attains lasting peace; the undisciplined, attached to the fruit through desire, is bound.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वकर्माणि मनसा संन्यस्यास्ते सुखं वशी ।
नवद्वारे पुरे देही नैव कुर्वन्न कारयन् ॥
translation_en: Renouncing all actions with the mind, the embodied one rests at ease, in command, in the city of nine gates, neither acting nor causing action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न कर्तृत्वं न कर्माणि लोकस्य सृजति प्रभुः ।
न कर्मफलसंयोगं स्वभावस्तु प्रवर्तते ॥
translation_en: The Lord creates for the world neither agency nor actions, nor the joining of action to its fruit; it is inherent nature that unfolds.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नादत्ते कस्यचित्पापं न चैव सुकृतं विभुः ।
अज्ञानेनावृतं ज्ञानं तेन मुह्यन्ति जन्तवः ॥
translation_en: The all-pervading Lord takes on no one's sin or merit; knowledge is veiled by ignorance, and by this beings are deluded.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ज्ञानेन तु तदज्ञानं येषां नाशितमात्मनः ।
तेषामादित्यवज्ज्ञानं प्रकाशयति तत्परम् ॥
translation_en: But for those in whom that ignorance is destroyed by knowledge of the self, knowledge, like the sun, lights up the Supreme.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तद्बुद्धयस्तदात्मानस्तन्निष्ठास्तत्परायणाः ।
गच्छन्त्यपुनरावृत्तिं ज्ञाननिर्धूतकल्मषाः ॥
translation_en: With understanding fixed on it, their self set on it, devoted to it, intent on it as their goal, they go where there is no return, their faults washed away by knowledge.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
विद्याविनयसम्पन्ने ब्राह्मणे गवि हस्तिनि ।
शुनि चैव श्वपाके च पण्डिताः समदर्शिनः ॥
translation_en: On a learned and humble brahmin, on a cow, an elephant, a dog, and an outcaste, the wise look with an equal eye.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इहैव तैर्जितः सर्गो येषां साम्ये स्थितं मनः ।
निर्दोषं हि समं ब्रह्म तस्माद् ब्रह्मणि ते स्थिताः ॥
translation_en: Even here rebirth is overcome by those whose minds rest in equanimity; for Brahman is flawless and the same in all, and so they are established in Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न प्रहृष्येत्प्रियं प्राप्य नोद्विजेत्प्राप्य चाप्रियम् ।
स्थिरबुद्धिरसम्मूढो ब्रह्मविद् ब्रह्मणि स्थितः ॥
translation_en: Neither rejoicing at the pleasant nor recoiling at the unpleasant, steady of understanding and unbewildered, the knower of Brahman is established in Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बाह्यस्पर्शेष्वसक्तात्मा विन्दत्यात्मनि यत्सुखम् ।
स ब्रह्मयोगयुक्तात्मा सुखमक्षयमश्नुते ॥
translation_en:
With the self unattached to outward contacts, he finds the happiness that is in the self;  
his self yoked in the yoga of Brahman, he attains unending happiness.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ये हि संस्पर्शजा भोगा दुःखयोनय एव ते ।
आद्यन्तवन्तः कौन्तेय न तेषु रमते बुधः ॥
translation_en: For the pleasures born of contact are wombs of suffering; they have a beginning and an end, O son of Kunti, and the wise take no delight in them.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
शक्नोतीहैव यः सोढुं प्राक्शरीरविमोक्षणात् ।
कामक्रोधोद्भवं वेगं स युक्तः स सुखी नरः ॥
translation_en: He who can withstand, even here, before he is freed from the body, the force born of desire and anger—he is disciplined, he is a happy man.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
योऽन्तःसुखोऽन्तरारामस्तथान्तर्ज्योतिरेव यः ।
स योगी ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं ब्रह्मभूतोऽधिगच्छति ॥
translation_en: He whose happiness is within, whose delight is within, whose light too is within—that yogin, having become Brahman, attains the nirvana of Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
लभन्ते ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृषयः क्षीणकल्मषाः ।
छिन्नद्वैधा यतात्मानः सर्वभूतहिते रताः ॥
translation_en: The seers whose sins are spent, whose doubts are cut away, who are self-mastered and rejoice in the welfare of all beings, attain the nirvana of Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कामक्रोधवियुक्तानां यतीनां यतचेतसाम् ।
अभितो ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं वर्तते विदितात्मनाम् ॥
translation_en: For ascetics freed from desire and anger, their minds controlled, who have known the self, the nirvana of Brahman lies near on every side.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
स्पर्शान्कृत्वा बहिर्बाह्यांश्चक्षुश्चैवान्तरे भ्रुवोः ।
प्राणापानौ समौ कृत्वा नासाभ्यन्तरचारिणौ ॥
translation_en: Shutting out the outward contacts, fixing the gaze between the brows, making the inward and outward breaths even as they move through the nostrils,

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यतेन्द्रियमनोबुद्धिर्मुनिर्मोक्षपरायणः ।
विगतेच्छाभयक्रोधो यः सदा मुक्त एव सः ॥
translation_en: the sage who has mastered his senses, mind, and understanding, intent on liberation, freed of desire, fear, and anger, is forever free.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
भोक्तारं यज्ञतपसां सर्वलोकमहेश्वरम् ।
सुहृदं सर्वभूतानां ज्ञात्वा मां शान्तिमृच्छति ॥
translation_en: Knowing me as the enjoyer of sacrifices and austerities, the great lord of all the worlds, the friend of every being, he attains peace.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनाश्रितः कर्मफलं कार्यं कर्म करोति यः ।
स संन्यासी च योगी च न निरग्निर्न चाक्रियः ॥
translation_en: He who performs the action that ought to be done, not depending on its fruit, is a renunciant and a yogin—not the one who lights no fire and does no rite.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यं संन्यासमिति प्राहुर्योगं तं विद्धि पाण्डव ।
न ह्यसंन्यस्तसङ्कल्पो योगी भवति कश्चन ॥
translation_en: Know that what they call renunciation is yoga, O Pandava; for none becomes a yogin without renouncing self-will.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आरुरुक्षोर्मुनेर्योगं कर्म कारणमुच्यते ।
योगारूढस्य तस्यैव शमः कारणमुच्यते ॥
translation_en:
For the sage who seeks to rise to yoga, action is said to be the means;  
for one who has risen to yoga, stillness is said to be the means.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदा हि नेन्द्रियार्थेषु न कर्मस्वनुषज्जते ।
सर्वसङ्कल्पसंन्यासी योगारूढस्तदोच्यते ॥
translation_en: When one is attached neither to the objects of sense nor to actions, having renounced all self-will, then one is said to have risen to yoga.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
उद्धरेदात्मनात्मानं नात्मानमवसादयेत् ।
आत्मैव ह्यात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव रिपुरात्मनः ॥
translation_en: Let one lift oneself by oneself; let one not drag oneself down. For oneself alone is one's friend, and oneself alone one's enemy.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बन्धुरात्मात्मनस्तस्य येनात्मैवात्मना जितः ।
अनात्मनस्तु शत्रुत्वे वर्तेतात्मैव शत्रुवत् ॥
translation_en: For one who has conquered himself by himself, the self is a friend to the self; but for one who has not mastered himself, the self stays hostile, like an enemy.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
जितात्मनः प्रशान्तस्य परमात्मा समाहितः ।
शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु तथा मानापमानयोः ॥
translation_en: For one who has mastered himself and is at peace, the supreme Atman stays steady, alike in cold and heat, pleasure and pain, honor and dishonor.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ज्ञानविज्ञानतृप्तात्मा कूटस्थो विजितेन्द्रियः ।
युक्त इत्युच्यते योगी समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चनः ॥
translation_en: The yogin content in knowledge and realization, unwavering, his senses subdued, to whom a clod, a stone, and gold are alike, is said to be yoked.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सुहृन्मित्रार्युदासीनमध्यस्थद्वेष्यबन्धुषु ।
साधुष्वपि च पापेषु समबुद्धिर्विशिष्यते ॥
translation_en: He who is even-minded toward well-wisher, friend, and foe, toward the neutral and the mediator, toward the hateful and the kinsman, and toward the good and the wicked alike, stands supreme.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
योगी युञ्जीत सततमात्मानं रहसि स्थितः ।
एकाकी यतचित्तात्मा निराशीरपरिग्रहः ॥
translation_en: Let the yogin discipline himself constantly, staying in solitude, alone, his mind and self controlled, free of longing and of possessions.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
शुचौ देशे प्रतिष्ठाप्य स्थिरमासनमात्मनः ।
नात्युच्छ्रितं नातिनीचं चैलाजिनकुशोत्तरम् ॥
translation_en: Having set up a firm seat for himself in a clean place, neither too high nor too low, covered with cloth, deerskin, and kusha grass,

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तत्रैकाग्रं मनः कृत्वा यतचित्तेन्द्रियक्रियः ।
उपविश्यासने युञ्ज्याद्योगमात्मविशुद्धये ॥
translation_en: there, making the mind one-pointed, the workings of mind and senses checked, seated on that seat, let him practice yoga to purify the self.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
समं कायशिरोग्रीवं धारयन्नचलं स्थिरः ।
सम्प्रेक्ष्य नासिकाग्रं स्वं दिशश्चानवलोकयन् ॥
translation_en: Holding body, head, and neck erect and still, steady, gazing at the tip of his own nose without looking about,

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रशान्तात्मा विगतभीर्ब्रह्मचारिव्रते स्थितः ।
मनः संयम्य मच्चित्तो युक्त आसीत मत्परः ॥
translation_en: his mind serene and free of fear, firm in the vow of celibacy, the mind controlled and thought fixed on me, let him sit, yoked, intent on me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
युञ्जन्नेवं सदात्मानं योगी नियतमानसः ।
शान्तिं निर्वाणपरमां मत्संस्थामधिगच्छति ॥
translation_en: Thus ever disciplining himself, the yogin of controlled mind attains the peace that culminates in nirvana and abides in me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नात्यश्नतस्तु योगोऽस्ति न चैकान्तमनश्नतः ।
न चातिस्वप्नशीलस्य जाग्रतो नैव चार्जुन ॥
translation_en: Yoga is not for one who eats too much, nor for one who eats nothing at all, nor for one given to too much sleep, nor for one always awake, Arjuna.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
युक्ताहारविहारस्य युक्तचेष्टस्य कर्मसु ।
युक्तस्वप्नावबोधस्य योगो भवति दुःखहा ॥
translation_en: For one measured in food and recreation, measured in his actions, measured in sleep and waking, yoga becomes the destroyer of sorrow.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदा विनियतं चित्तमात्मन्येवावतिष्ठते ।
निःस्पृहः सर्वकामेभ्यो युक्त इत्युच्यते तदा ॥
translation_en: When the disciplined mind comes to rest in the self alone, free of longing for any desire, then one is said to be yoked.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यथा दीपो निवातस्थो नेङ्गते सोपमा स्मृता ।
योगिनो यतचित्तस्य युञ्जतो योगमात्मनः ॥
translation_en: As a lamp in a windless place does not flicker—such is the image of the yogin of controlled mind, practicing the yoga of the self.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यत्रोपरमते चित्तं निरुद्धं योगसेवया ।
यत्र चैवात्मनात्मानं पश्यन्नात्मनि तुष्यति ॥
translation_en: When the mind, restrained by the practice of yoga, comes to rest, and seeing the self by the self, one is content within the self;

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सुखमात्यन्तिकं यत्तद् बुद्धिग्राह्यमतीन्द्रियम् ।
वेत्ति यत्र न चैवायं स्थितश्चलति तत्त्वतः ॥
translation_en: when one knows that boundless happiness, grasped by the understanding and beyond the senses, and, established in it, never strays from the truth;

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यं लब्ध्वा चापरं लाभं मन्यते नाधिकं ततः ।
यस्मिन्स्थितो न दुःखेन गुरुणापि विचाल्यते ॥
translation_en: and, having gained it, counts no other gain greater—established in it, he is unshaken even by the heaviest sorrow:

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तं विद्याद् दुःखसंयोगवियोगं योगसंज्ञितम् ।
स निश्चयेन योक्तव्यो योगोऽनिर्विण्णचेतसा ॥
translation_en: let it be known that this severance of all union with sorrow is what is called yoga; that yoga is to be practiced with resolve and an undismayed mind.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सङ्कल्पप्रभवान्कामांस्त्यक्त्वा सर्वानशेषतः ।
मनसैवेन्द्रियग्रामं विनियम्य समन्ततः ॥
translation_en: Abandoning without remainder all desires born of self-will, restraining the whole throng of senses on every side by the mind alone,

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
शनैः शनैरुपरमेद् बुद्ध्या धृतिगृहीतया ।
आत्मसंस्थं मनः कृत्वा न किञ्चिदपि चिन्तयेत् ॥
translation_en: little by little let him grow still, the understanding held firm; fixing the mind on the self, let him think of nothing at all.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यतो यतो निश्चरति मनश्चञ्चलमस्थिरम् ।
ततस्ततो नियम्यैतदात्मन्येव वशं नयेत् ॥
translation_en: Wherever the restless, unsteady mind wanders off, from there let him rein it back under the control of the self.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रशान्तमनसं ह्येनं योगिनं सुखमुत्तमम् ।
उपैति शान्तरजसं ब्रह्मभूतमकल्मषम् ॥
translation_en: For supreme happiness comes to the yogin whose mind is at peace, whose passion is stilled, who is free of taint and has become Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
युञ्जन्नेवं सदात्मानं योगी विगतकल्मषः ।
सुखेन ब्रह्मसंस्पर्शमत्यन्तं सुखमश्नुते ॥
translation_en: Thus ever disciplining himself, the yogin, freed of taint, easily attains the boundless happiness of contact with Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वभूतस्थमात्मानं सर्वभूतानि चात्मनि ।
ईक्षते योगयुक्तात्मा सर्वत्र समदर्शनः ॥
translation_en: His self yoked in yoga, he sees the self present in all beings and all beings in the self, seeing the same everywhere.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यो मां पश्यति सर्वत्र सर्वं च मयि पश्यति ।
तस्याहं न प्रणश्यामि स च मे न प्रणश्यति ॥
translation_en: For one who sees me everywhere and sees all things in me, I am not lost to him, nor is he lost to me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वभूतस्थितं यो मां भजत्येकत्वमास्थितः ।
सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि स योगी मयि वर्तते ॥
translation_en: The yogin who, established in oneness, worships me as dwelling in all beings abides in me, in whatever way he lives.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आत्मौपम्येन सर्वत्र समं पश्यति योऽर्जुन ।
सुखं वा यदि वा दुःखं स योगी परमो मतः ॥
translation_en: He who sees by analogy with himself the same everywhere, Arjuna, whether in pleasure or in pain, is held to be the highest yogin.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
योऽयं योगस्त्वया प्रोक्तः साम्येन मधुसूदन ।
एतस्याहं न पश्यामि चञ्चलत्वात्स्थितिं स्थिराम् ॥
translation_en: This yoga of evenness you have taught, O Madhusudana, I do not see how it can stand firm, because the mind is so restless.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
चञ्चलं हि मनः कृष्ण प्रमाथि बलवद् दृढम् ।
तस्याहं निग्रहं मन्ये वायोरिव सुदुष्करम् ॥
translation_en: For the mind is restless, Krishna—turbulent, strong, and unyielding; to restrain it, I think, is as hard as to restrain the wind.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
असंशयं महाबाहो मनो दुर्निग्रहं चलम् ।
अभ्यासेन तु कौन्तेय वैराग्येण च गृह्यते ॥
translation_en: No doubt, O mighty-armed one, the mind is hard to restrain and restless; but by practice, O son of Kunti, and by dispassion it is held in check.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
असंयतात्मना योगो दुष्प्राप इति मे मतिः ।
वश्यात्मना तु यतता शक्योऽवाप्तुमुपायतः ॥
translation_en: Yoga is hard to reach, I agree, for one whose self is unrestrained; but for one who strives, self-controlled, it can be attained by the right means.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अयतिः श्रद्धयोपेतो योगाच्चलितमानसः ।
अप्राप्य योगसंसिद्धिं कां गतिं कृष्ण गच्छति ॥
translation_en: One of faith who does not strive, whose mind has strayed from yoga and failed to reach its perfection—what end does he meet, O Krishna?

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कच्चिन्नोभयविभ्रष्टश्छिन्नाभ्रमिव नश्यति ।
अप्रतिष्ठो महाबाहो विमूढो ब्रह्मणः पथि ॥
translation_en: Fallen from both, does he not perish like a scattered cloud, without support, O mighty-armed one, bewildered on the path of Brahman?

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एतन्मे संशयं कृष्ण छेत्तुमर्हस्यशेषतः ।
त्वदन्यः संशयस्यास्य छेत्ता न ह्युपपद्यते ॥
translation_en: You are fit, O Krishna, to dispel this doubt of mine completely; for none but you can be found to remove it.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पार्थ नैवेह नामुत्र विनाशस्तस्य विद्यते ।
न हि कल्याणकृत्कश्चिद् दुर्गतिं तात गच्छति ॥
translation_en: O Partha, neither here nor hereafter is there ruin for him; for no one who does good, my friend, ever comes to a bad end.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्राप्य पुण्यकृतां लोकानुषित्वा शाश्वतीः समाः ।
शुचीनां श्रीमतां गेहे योगभ्रष्टोऽभिजायते ॥
translation_en: Having reached the worlds of those who do right and dwelt there countless years, the one fallen from yoga is born in the house of the pure and prosperous;

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अथवा योगिनामेव कुले भवति धीमताम् ।
एतद्धि दुर्लभतरं लोके जन्म यदीदृशम् ॥
translation_en: or else he is born into a family of wise yogins—though a birth like this is harder still to win in this world.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तत्र तं बुद्धिसंयोगं लभते पौर्वदेहिकम् ।
यतते च ततो भूयः संसिद्धौ कुरुनन्दन ॥
translation_en: There he regains the union of understanding gained in his former body, and from there strives once more toward perfection, O joy of the Kurus.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पूर्वाभ्यासेन तेनैव ह्रियते ह्यवशोऽपि सः ।
जिज्ञासुरपि योगस्य शब्दब्रह्मातिवर्तते ॥
translation_en: By that very former practice he is carried onward, even against his will; even wishing to know yoga, he passes beyond the Brahman of mere words.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रयत्नाद्यतमानस्तु योगी संशुद्धकिल्बिषः ।
अनेकजन्मसंसिद्धस्ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥
translation_en: But the yogin who strives with effort, cleansed of all faults, perfected through many births, then reaches the supreme goal.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तपस्विभ्योऽधिको योगी ज्ञानिभ्योऽपि मतोऽधिकः ।
कर्मिभ्यश्चाधिको योगी तस्माद्योगी भवार्जुन ॥
translation_en: The yogin is greater than the ascetics, greater, it is held, than the men of knowledge, and greater than the men of action; therefore become a yogin, Arjuna.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
योगिनामपि सर्वेषां मद्गतेनान्तरात्मना ।
श्रद्धावान्भजते यो मां स मे युक्ततमो मतः ॥
translation_en: And of all yogins, the one who, full of faith, worships me with his inmost self drawn to me—him I hold to be the most deeply yoked.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मय्यासक्तमनाः पार्थ योगं युञ्जन्मदाश्रयः ।
असंशयं समग्रं मां यथा ज्ञास्यसि तच्छृणु ॥
translation_en: With your mind clinging to me, O Partha, practicing yoga and taking refuge in me, hear how you shall know me fully and beyond doubt.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ज्ञानं तेऽहं सविज्ञानमिदं वक्ष्याम्यशेषतः ।
यज्ज्ञात्वा नेह भूयोऽन्यज्ज्ञातव्यमवशिष्यते ॥
translation_en: I shall declare to you in full this knowledge together with its realization; once you know it, nothing further remains to be known here.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मनुष्याणां सहस्रेषु कश्चिद्यतति सिद्धये ।
यततामपि सिद्धानां कश्चिन्मां वेत्ति तत्त्वतः ॥
translation_en: Among thousands of people, scarcely one strives for perfection; and of those who strive and succeed, scarcely one knows me in truth.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
भूमिरापोऽनलो वायुः खं मनो बुद्धिरेव च ।
अहङ्कार इतीयं मे भिन्ना प्रकृतिरष्टधा ॥
translation_en: Earth, water, fire, air, ether, mind, understanding, and ego—so is my prakriti divided eightfold.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अपरेयमितस्त्वन्यां प्रकृतिं विद्धि मे पराम् ।
जीवभूतां महाबाहो ययेदं धार्यते जगत् ॥
