translation_en: This is my lower nature; but know my other nature, the higher, O mighty-armed one—the living principle by which this world is sustained.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एतद्योनीनि भूतानि सर्वाणीत्युपधारय ।
अहं कृत्स्नस्य जगतः प्रभवः प्रलयस्तथा ॥
translation_en: Know that all beings have their womb in these; I am the origin of the whole universe, and its dissolution as well.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मत्तः परतरं नान्यत्किञ्चिदस्ति धनञ्जय ।
मयि सर्वमिदं प्रोतं सूत्रे मणिगणा इव ॥
translation_en: There is nothing higher than me, O Dhananjaya; on me all this is strung, like rows of gems upon a thread.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
रसोऽहमप्सु कौन्तेय प्रभास्मि शशिसूर्ययोः ।
प्रणवः सर्ववेदेषु शब्दः खे पौरुषं नृषु ॥
translation_en: I am the taste in water, O son of Kunti, the light in moon and sun, the syllable Om in all the Vedas, the sound in space, the manhood in men.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पुण्यो गन्धः पृथिव्यां च तेजश्चास्मि विभावसौ ।
जीवनं सर्वभूतेषु तपश्चास्मि तपस्विषु ॥
translation_en:
I am the pure fragrance in the earth and the brightness in fire;  
I am the life in all beings and the austerity in ascetics.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बीजं मां सर्वभूतानां विद्धि पार्थ सनातनम् ।
बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम् ॥
translation_en: Know me, O Partha, as the eternal seed of all beings; I am the understanding of the wise, the splendor of the splendid.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बलं बलवतां चाहं कामरागविवर्जितम् ।
धर्माविरुद्धो भूतेषु कामोऽस्मि भरतर्षभ ॥
translation_en: I am the strength of the strong, free of desire and passion; in beings I am desire that does not conflict with dharma, O chief of the Bharatas.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ये चैव सात्त्विका भावा राजसास्तामसाश्च ये ।
मत्त एवेति तान्विद्धि न त्वहं तेषु ते मयि ॥
translation_en: Whatever states there are—of sattva, of rajas, or of tamas—know them all to come from me; yet I am not in them, but they are in me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
त्रिभिर्गुणमयैर्भावैरेभिः सर्वमिदं जगत् ।
मोहितं नाभिजानाति मामेभ्यः परमव्ययम् ॥
translation_en: Deluded by these three states made of the gunas, this whole world does not know me, who am above them and imperishable.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दैवी ह्येषा गुणमयी मम माया दुरत्यया ।
मामेव ये प्रपद्यन्ते मायामेतां तरन्ति ते ॥
translation_en: For this divine maya of mine, made of the gunas, is hard to cross; but those who take refuge in me alone pass beyond this maya.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न मां दुष्कृतिनो मूढाः प्रपद्यन्ते नराधमाः ।
माययापहृतज्ञाना आसुरं भावमाश्रिताः ॥
translation_en: The evildoers, the deluded, the lowest of men, their knowledge stripped away by maya, given over to a demonic nature, do not take refuge in me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
चतुर्विधा भजन्ते मां जनाः सुकृतिनोऽर्जुन ।
आर्तो जिज्ञासुरर्थार्थी ज्ञानी च भरतर्षभ ॥
translation_en: Four kinds of those who do good worship me, Arjuna: the distressed, the seeker of knowledge, the seeker of gain, and the wise, O best of the Bharatas.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तेषां ज्ञानी नित्ययुक्त एकभक्तिर्विशिष्यते ।
प्रियो हि ज्ञानिनोऽत्यर्थमहं स च मम प्रियः ॥
translation_en: Of these, the wise one, ever disciplined, devoted to the one alone, stands highest; for to the wise I am exceedingly dear, and he is dear to me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
उदाराः सर्व एवैते ज्ञानी त्वात्मैव मे मतम् ।
आस्थितः स हि युक्तात्मा मामेवानुत्तमां गतिम् ॥
translation_en:
All of these are noble, but the wise one I hold to be my very self;  
for, steady in himself, he is set on me alone as the highest goal.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बहूनां जन्मनामन्ते ज्ञानवान्मां प्रपद्यते ।
वासुदेवः सर्वमिति स महात्मा सुदुर्लभः ॥
translation_en: At the end of many births the wise one comes to me, knowing that Vasudeva is all; such a great soul is very rare.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कामैस्तैस्तैर्हृतज्ञानाः प्रपद्यन्तेऽन्यदेवताः ।
तं तं नियममास्थाय प्रकृत्या नियताः स्वया ॥
translation_en: Those whose knowledge is carried off by this or that desire take refuge in other gods, following this and that observance, constrained by their own nature.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यो यो यां यां तनुं भक्तः श्रद्धयार्चितुमिच्छति ।
तस्य तस्याचलां श्रद्धां तामेव विदधाम्यहम् ॥
translation_en: Whatever form a devotee seeks to worship with faith, it is I who make that faith of his steady.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
स तया श्रद्धया युक्तस्तस्याराधनमीहते ।
लभते च ततः कामान्मयैव विहितान्हि तान् ॥
translation_en: Yoked with that faith, he seeks to honor that form, and from it obtains his desires—though it is I alone who grant them.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अन्तवत्तु फलं तेषां तद्भवत्यल्पमेधसाम् ।
देवान्देवयजो यान्ति मद्भक्ता यान्ति मामपि ॥
translation_en: But the fruit won by these small-minded ones is finite; those who worship the gods go to the gods, while those devoted to me come to me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अव्यक्तं व्यक्तिमापन्नं मन्यन्ते मामबुद्धयः ।
परं भावमजानन्तो ममाव्ययमनुत्तमम् ॥
translation_en: The unwise take me, the unmanifest, to have come into manifest being, not knowing my higher nature, imperishable and supreme.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नाहं प्रकाशः सर्वस्य योगमायासमावृतः ।
मूढोऽयं नाभिजानाति लोको मामजमव्ययम् ॥
translation_en: Veiled by my yoga-maya, I am not manifest to all; this deluded world does not know me, the unborn and imperishable.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
वेदाहं समतीतानि वर्तमानानि चार्जुन ।
भविष्याणि च भूतानि मां तु वेद न कश्चन ॥
translation_en: I know the beings that are past, that are present, and that are to come, O Arjuna; but no one knows me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इच्छाद्वेषसमुत्थेन द्वन्द्वमोहेन भारत ।
सर्वभूतानि सम्मोहं सर्गे यान्ति परन्तप ॥
translation_en: By the delusion of the pairs of opposites, sprung from desire and aversion, O Bharata, all beings fall into bewilderment at birth, O scorcher of foes.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
येषां त्वन्तगतं पापं जनानां पुण्यकर्मणाम् ।
ते द्वन्द्वमोहनिर्मुक्ता भजन्ते मां दृढव्रताः ॥
translation_en: But those of pure deeds, whose sins have ended, freed from the delusion of the pairs of opposites, worship me with firm resolve.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
जरामरणमोक्षाय मामाश्रित्य यतन्ति ये ।
ते ब्रह्म तद्विदुः कृत्स्नमध्यात्मं कर्म चाखिलम् ॥
translation_en: Those who, taking refuge in me, strive for release from old age and death know that Brahman, the whole inner principle, and the whole of action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
साधिभूताधिदैवं मां साधियज्ञं च ये विदुः ।
प्रयाणकालेऽपि च मां ते विदुर्युक्तचेतसः ॥
translation_en: Those who know me together with the material domain, the divine principle, and the sacrificial principle, their minds yoked, know me even at the hour of death.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
किं तद् ब्रह्म किमध्यात्मं किं कर्म पुरुषोत्तम ।
अधिभूतं च किं प्रोक्तमधिदैवं किमुच्यते ॥
translation_en: What is that Brahman? What is the inner principle? What is action, O Purushottama? What is called the material domain, and what the divine principle?

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अधियज्ञः कथं कोऽत्र देहेऽस्मिन्मधुसूदन ।
प्रयाणकाले च कथं ज्ञेयोऽसि नियतात्मभिः ॥
translation_en: Who, and how, is the sacrificial principle here in this body, O Madhusudana? And how, at the hour of death, are you to be known by the self-controlled?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अक्षरं ब्रह्म परमं स्वभावोऽध्यात्ममुच्यते ।
भूतभावोद्भवकरो विसर्गः कर्मसंज्ञितः ॥
translation_en: Brahman is the supreme, the imperishable; one's own essential being is called the inner principle. The creative force that brings beings into existence is called action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अधिभूतं क्षरो भावः पुरुषश्चाधिदैवतम् ।
अधियज्ञोऽहमेवात्र देहे देहभृतां वर ॥
translation_en: The material domain is perishable existence; the purusha is the divine principle; and the sacrificial principle is I myself, here within the body, O best of the embodied.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अन्तकाले च मामेव स्मरन्मुक्त्वा कलेवरम् ।
यः प्रयाति स मद्भावं याति नास्त्यत्र संशयः ॥
translation_en: And whoever, at the hour of death, remembering me alone, departs, leaving the body, attains my being; of this there is no doubt.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यं यं वापि स्मरन्भावं त्यजत्यन्ते कलेवरम् ।
तं तमेवैति कौन्तेय सदा तद्भावभावितः ॥
translation_en: Whatever state one calls to mind as he leaves the body at the end, to that very state he goes, O son of Kunti, ever shaped by that thought.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तस्मात्सर्वेषु कालेषु मामनुस्मर युध्य च ।
मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्मामेवैष्यस्यसंशयः ॥
translation_en: Therefore at all times remember me, and fight; with mind and understanding fixed on me, you shall come to me, beyond doubt.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अभ्यासयोगयुक्तेन चेतसा नान्यगामिना ।
परमं पुरुषं दिव्यं याति पार्थानुचिन्तयन् ॥
translation_en: With the mind disciplined by practice and not straying elsewhere, one reaches the supreme divine person, meditating on him, O Partha.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कविं पुराणमनुशासितार-
मणोरणीयंसमनुस्मरेद्यः ।
सर्वस्य धातारमचिन्त्यरूप-
मादित्यवर्णं तमसः परस्तात् ॥
translation_en: Whoever meditates on the ancient seer, the ruler of all, smaller than the small, the sustainer of all, of form beyond thought, sunlike in color, beyond the darkness—

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रयाणकाले मनसाऽचलेन
भक्त्या युक्तो योगबलेन चैव ।
भ्रुवोर्मध्ये प्राणमावेश्य सम्यक्
स तं परं पुरुषमुपैति दिव्यम् ॥
translation_en: at the hour of departure, with mind unmoving, joined with devotion and the power of yoga, setting the breath rightly between the brows, he reaches that supreme divine person.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदक्षरं वेदविदो वदन्ति
विशन्ति यद्यतयो वीतरागाः ।
यदिच्छन्तो ब्रह्मचर्यं चरन्ति
तत्ते पदं सङ्ग्रहेण प्रवक्ष्ये ॥
translation_en: That state which the knowers of the Veda call imperishable, which ascetics free of passion enter, and seeking which they live in celibacy—that state I shall declare to you in brief.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वद्वाराणि संयम्य मनो हृदि निरुध्य च ।
मूर्ध्न्याधायात्मनः प्राणमास्थितो योगधारणाम् ॥
translation_en: Closing all the gates, confining the mind in the heart, drawing the breath up into the head, established in the concentration of yoga,

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ओमित्येकाक्षरं ब्रह्म व्याहरन्मामनुस्मरन् ।
यः प्रयाति त्यजन्देहं स याति परमां गतिम् ॥
translation_en: uttering the single syllable Om, which is Brahman, and remembering me, he who departs, leaving the body, reaches the supreme goal.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनन्यचेताः सततं यो मां स्मरति नित्यशः ।
तस्याहं सुलभः पार्थ नित्ययुक्तस्य योगिनः ॥
translation_en: For the yogin who, undistracted, remembers me constantly, day after day, I am easy to reach, O Partha—for the ever-disciplined one.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मामुपेत्य पुनर्जन्म दुःखालयमशाश्वतम् ।
नाप्नुवन्ति महात्मानः संसिद्धिं परमां गताः ॥
translation_en: Having reached me, the great souls are not born again into this fleeting home of sorrow; they have attained the highest perfection.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आब्रह्मभुवनाल्लोकाः पुनरावर्तिनोऽर्जुन ।
मामुपेत्य तु कौन्तेय पुनर्जन्म न विद्यते ॥
translation_en: From the world of Brahma downward, all worlds are subject to return, Arjuna; but having reached me, O son of Kunti, there is no rebirth.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सहस्रयुगपर्यन्तमहर्यद् ब्रह्मणो विदुः ।
रात्रिं युगसहस्रान्तां तेऽहोरात्रविदो जनाः ॥
translation_en: Those who know that Brahma's day lasts a thousand ages, and his night ends after a thousand ages—they are the people who know day and night.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अव्यक्ताद् व्यक्तयः सर्वाः प्रभवन्त्यहरागमे ।
रात्र्यागमे प्रलीयन्ते तत्रैवाव्यक्तसंज्ञके ॥
translation_en: At the coming of day all manifest things arise from the unmanifest; at the coming of night they dissolve into that same unmanifest.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
भूतग्रामः स एवायं भूत्वा भूत्वा प्रलीयते ।
रात्र्यागमेऽवशः पार्थ प्रभवत्यहरागमे ॥
translation_en: This same host of beings, coming to be again and again, dissolves helplessly at the coming of night, O Partha, and comes forth at the coming of day.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
परस्तस्मात्तु भावोऽन्योऽव्यक्तोऽव्यक्तात्सनातनः ।
यः स सर्वेषु भूतेषु नश्यत्सु न विनश्यति ॥
translation_en: But beyond this unmanifest is another, eternal unmanifest, which does not perish when all beings perish.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अव्यक्तोऽक्षर इत्युक्तस्तमाहुः परमां गतिम् ।
यं प्राप्य न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम ॥
translation_en: This unmanifest, called the imperishable, is named the supreme goal; reaching it, they do not return—that is my supreme abode.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पुरुषः स परः पार्थ भक्त्या लभ्यस्त्वनन्यया ।
यस्यान्तःस्थानि भूतानि येन सर्वमिदं ततम् ॥
translation_en: That supreme person, O Partha, in whom all beings dwell and by whom all this is pervaded, is to be reached by undivided devotion.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यत्र काले त्वनावृत्तिमावृत्तिं चैव योगिनः ।
प्रयाता यान्ति तं कालं वक्ष्यामि भरतर्षभ ॥
translation_en: Now I shall tell you, O best of the Bharatas, the time at which yogins who depart go to no return, and the time at which they return.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अग्निर्ज्योतिरहः शुक्लः षण्मासा उत्तरायणम् ।
तत्र प्रयाता गच्छन्ति ब्रह्म ब्रह्मविदो जनाः ॥
translation_en: Fire, light, day, the bright fortnight, the six months of the sun's northward course—departing then, those who know Brahman go to Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
धूमो रात्रिस्तथा कृष्णः षण्मासा दक्षिणायनम् ।
तत्र चान्द्रमसं ज्योतिर्योगी प्राप्य निवर्तते ॥
translation_en: Smoke, night, the dark fortnight, the six months of the southward course—by these the yogin reaches the lunar light and returns.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
शुक्लकृष्णे गती ह्येते जगतः शाश्वते मते ।
एकया यात्यनावृत्तिमन्ययावर्तते पुनः ॥
translation_en: These two paths of the world, the bright and the dark, are held to be eternal; by the one a person goes to no return, by the other he returns again.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नैते सृती पार्थ जानन्योगी मुह्यति कश्चन ।
तस्मात्सर्वेषु कालेषु योगयुक्तो भवार्जुन ॥
translation_en: Knowing these two paths, O Partha, no yogin is deluded; therefore at all times be yoked in yoga, Arjuna.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
वेदेषु यज्ञेषु तपःसु चैव
दानेषु यत्पुण्यफलं प्रदिष्टम् ।
अत्येति तत्सर्वमिदं विदित्वा
योगी परं स्थानमुपैति चाद्यम् ॥
translation_en: The yogin who knows all this passes beyond whatever fruit of merit is laid down for the Vedas, for sacrifices, austerities, and gifts, and reaches the supreme, primal abode.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इदं तु ते गुह्यतमं प्रवक्ष्याम्यनसूयवे ।
ज्ञानं विज्ञानसहितं यज्ज्ञात्वा मोक्ष्यसेऽशुभात् ॥
translation_en: To you, who do not cavil, I shall declare this most secret knowledge together with its realization; knowing it, you will be freed from ill.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
राजविद्या राजगुह्यं पवित्रमिदमुत्तमम् ।
प्रत्यक्षावगमं धर्म्यं सुसुखं कर्तुमव्ययम् ॥
translation_en: This is the royal knowledge, the royal secret, the supreme purifier, plain to direct experience, in keeping with dharma, easy to practice, and imperishable.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अश्रद्दधानाः पुरुषा धर्मस्यास्य परन्तप ।
अप्राप्य मां निवर्तन्ते मृत्युसंसारवर्त्मनि ॥
translation_en: People who put no faith in this dharma, O scorcher of foes, fail to reach me and return to the path of death and samsara.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मया ततमिदं सर्वं जगदव्यक्तमूर्तिना ।
मत्स्थानि सर्वभूतानि न चाहं तेष्ववस्थितः ॥
translation_en: By me, in my unmanifest form, this whole world is pervaded; all beings dwell in me, but I do not dwell in them.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न च मत्स्थानि भूतानि पश्य मे योगमैश्वरम् ।
भूतभृन्न च भूतस्थो ममात्मा भूतभावनः ॥
translation_en: And yet beings do not dwell in me—behold my sovereign yoga! Sustaining beings, yet not dwelling in them, my self is what brings beings into being.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यथाकाशस्थितो नित्यं वायुः सर्वत्रगो महान् ।
तथा सर्वाणि भूतानि मत्स्थानीत्युपधारय ॥
translation_en: As the vast wind, moving everywhere, abides always in space, so, understand, all beings abide in me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वभूतानि कौन्तेय प्रकृतिं यान्ति मामिकाम् ।
कल्पक्षये पुनस्तानि कल्पादौ विसृजाम्यहम् ॥
translation_en: All beings, O son of Kunti, pass into my prakriti at the end of an aeon, and at the aeon's beginning I send them forth again.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रकृतिं स्वामवष्टभ्य विसृजामि पुनः पुनः ।
भूतग्राममिमं कृत्स्नमवशं प्रकृतेर्वशात् ॥
translation_en: Resting on my own prakriti, I send forth again and again this whole host of beings, helpless under the sway of prakriti.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न च मां तानि कर्माणि निबध्नन्ति धनञ्जय ।
उदासीनवदासीनमसक्तं तेषु कर्मसु ॥
translation_en: Nor do these actions bind me, O Dhananjaya; I remain seated apart, unattached to those actions.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मयाध्यक्षेण प्रकृतिः सूयते सचराचरम् ।
हेतुनानेन कौन्तेय जगद्विपरिवर्तते ॥
translation_en:
Under my oversight, prakriti gives birth to all that moves and does not move;  
by this cause, O son of Kunti, the world turns round.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अवजानन्ति मां मूढा मानुषीं तनुमाश्रितम् ।
परं भावमजानन्तो मम भूतमहेश्वरम् ॥
translation_en: The foolish scorn me when I have taken on a human form, not knowing my higher being as the great lord of beings.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मोघाशा मोघकर्माणो मोघज्ञाना विचेतसः ।
राक्षसीमासुरीं चैव प्रकृतिं मोहिनीं श्रिताः ॥
translation_en: Their hopes vain, their actions vain, their knowledge vain, bereft of sense, they fall into a deluding, fiendish, and brutish nature.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
महात्मानस्तु मां पार्थ दैवीं प्रकृतिमाश्रिताः ।
भजन्त्यनन्यमनसो ज्ञात्वा भूतादिमव्ययम् ॥
translation_en: But the great souls, O Partha, sheltering in the divine nature, worship me with undivided mind, knowing me as the imperishable source of beings.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सततं कीर्तयन्तो मां यतन्तश्च दृढव्रताः ।
नमस्यन्तश्च मां भक्त्या नित्ययुक्ता उपासते ॥
translation_en: Ever glorifying me, striving with firm vows, bowing before me with devotion, they worship me, ever steadfast.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ज्ञानयज्ञेन चाप्यन्ये यजन्तो मामुपासते ।
एकत्वेन पृथक्त्वेन बहुधा विश्वतोमुखम् ॥
translation_en: Others, offering the sacrifice of knowledge, worship me as the one, as the many, and as facing in all directions, in my manifold forms.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अहं क्रतुरहं यज्ञः स्वधाहमहमौषधम् ।
मन्त्रोऽहमहमेवाज्यमहमग्निरहं हुतम् ॥
translation_en:
I am the rite, I am the sacrifice, I am the offering to the ancestors, I am the herb;  
I am the sacred formula, I am the melted butter, I am the fire, I am the oblation.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पिताहमस्य जगतो माता धाता पितामहः ।
वेद्यं पवित्रमोङ्कार ऋक्साम यजुरेव च ॥
translation_en: I am the father of this world, its mother, sustainer, and forefather; the object of knowledge, the purifier, the syllable Om, and the Rig, the Sama, and the Yajur Veda.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
गतिर्भर्ता प्रभुः साक्षी निवासः शरणं सुहृत् ।
प्रभवः प्रलयः स्थानं निधानं बीजमव्ययम् ॥
translation_en: I am the goal, the upholder, the lord, the witness, the dwelling, the refuge, the friend; the origin, the dissolution, the ground, the resting place, the imperishable seed.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तपाम्यहमहं वर्षं निगृह्णाम्युत्सृजामि च ।
अमृतं चैव मृत्युश्च सदसच्चाहमर्जुन ॥
translation_en: I give heat; I hold back the rain and send it forth; I am immortality and death, being and nonbeing, Arjuna.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
त्रैविद्या मां सोमपाः पूतपापा
यज्ञैरिष्ट्वा स्वर्गतिं प्रार्थयन्ते ।
ते पुण्यमासाद्य सुरेन्द्रलोक-
मश्नन्ति दिव्यान्दिवि देवभोगान् ॥
translation_en: The knowers of the three Vedas, who drink the soma and are cleansed of sin, worship me with sacrifices and seek the way to heaven; reaching the holy world of Indra, they taste in heaven the divine pleasures of the gods.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ते तं भुक्त्वा स्वर्गलोकं विशालं
क्षीणे पुण्ये मर्त्यलोकं विशन्ति ।
एवं त्रयीधर्ममनुप्रपन्ना
गतागतं कामकामा लभन्ते ॥
translation_en: Having enjoyed that vast world of heaven, their merit spent, they enter the world of mortals; so, following the dharma of the three Vedas and craving pleasures, they come and go.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनन्याश्चिन्तयन्तो मां ये जनाः पर्युपासते ।
तेषां नित्याभियुक्तानां योगक्षेमं वहाम्यहम् ॥
translation_en: But those who, thinking of no other, worship me, ever yoked to me—for them I secure what they lack and preserve what they have.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
येऽप्यन्यदेवता भक्ता यजन्ते श्रद्धयान्विताः ।
तेऽपि मामेव कौन्तेय यजन्त्यविधिपूर्वकम् ॥
translation_en: Even those devoted to other gods who worship them with faith, O son of Kunti, worship me too, though not in the appointed way.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अहं हि सर्वयज्ञानां भोक्ता च प्रभुरेव च ।
न तु मामभिजानन्ति तत्त्वेनातश्च्यवन्ति ते ॥
translation_en: For I am the enjoyer and the lord of all sacrifices; but they do not know me in truth, and so they fall.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यान्ति देवव्रता देवान्पितॄन्यान्ति पितृव्रताः ।
भूतानि यान्ति भूतेज्या यान्ति मद्याजिनोऽपि माम् ॥
translation_en: Those who worship the gods go to the gods; those who worship the ancestors go to the ancestors; those who worship the spirits go to the spirits; but those who worship me come to me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पत्रं पुष्पं फलं तोयं यो मे भक्त्या प्रयच्छति ।
तदहं भक्त्युपहृतमश्नामि प्रयतात्मनः ॥
translation_en: Whoever offers me with devotion a leaf, a flower, a fruit, or water—that offering of devotion, given by the pure of heart, I accept.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यत्करोषि यदश्नासि यज्जुहोषि ददासि यत् ।
यत्तपस्यसि कौन्तेय तत्कुरुष्व मदर्पणम् ॥
translation_en: Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer or give, whatever austerity you practice, O son of Kunti, do it as an offering to me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
शुभाशुभफलैरेवं मोक्ष्यसे कर्मबन्धनैः ।
संन्यासयोगयुक्तात्मा विमुक्तो मामुपैष्यसि ॥
translation_en:
So you will be freed from the bonds of action, from its good and evil fruits;  
your self yoked in the yoga of renunciation, set free, you will come to me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
समोऽहं सर्वभूतेषु न मे द्वेष्योऽस्ति न प्रियः ।
ये भजन्ति तु मां भक्त्या मयि ते तेषु चाप्यहम् ॥
translation_en:
I am the same toward all beings; none is hateful to me and none dear;  
but those who worship me with devotion are in me, and I too am in them.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अपि चेत्सुदुराचारो भजते मामनन्यभाक् ।
साधुरेव स मन्तव्यः सम्यग्व्यवसितो हि सः ॥
translation_en: Even one of very evil conduct who worships me, devoted to none else, is to be reckoned among the good, for his resolve is right.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
क्षिप्रं भवति धर्मात्मा शश्वच्छान्तिं निगच्छति ।
कौन्तेय प्रतिजानीहि न मे भक्तः प्रणश्यति ॥
translation_en:
Swiftly he becomes righteous of soul and attains lasting peace;  
know it for certain, O son of Kunti—no one devoted to me is lost.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मां हि पार्थ व्यपाश्रित्य येऽपि स्युः पापयोनयः ।
स्त्रियो वैश्यास्तथा शूद्रास्तेऽपि यान्ति परां गतिम् ॥
translation_en: For those who take refuge in me, O Partha, even if born of a lowly womb—women, vaishyas, and shudras—even they reach the supreme goal.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
किं पुनर्ब्राह्मणाः पुण्या भक्ता राजर्षयस्तथा ।
अनित्यमसुखं लोकमिमं प्राप्य भजस्व माम् ॥
translation_en: How much more, then, the pure brahmins and devoted royal sages! Having come to this fleeting, joyless world, worship me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु ।
मामेवैष्यसि युक्त्वैवमात्मानं मत्परायणः ॥
translation_en: Fix your mind on me, be devoted to me, sacrifice to me, bow before me; so, yoking yourself, with me as your highest goal, you will come to me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
भूय एव महाबाहो श‍ृणु मे परमं वचः ।
यत्तेऽहं प्रीयमाणाय वक्ष्यामि हितकाम्यया ॥
translation_en: Once more, O mighty-armed one, hear my supreme word, which I shall speak to you, who are dear to me, desiring your good.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न मे विदुः सुरगणाः प्रभवं न महर्षयः ।
अहमादिर्हि देवानां महर्षीणां च सर्वशः ॥
translation_en: Neither the hosts of gods nor the great sages know my origin; for I am the source of the gods and of the great sages in every way.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यो मामजमनादिं च वेत्ति लोकमहेश्वरम् ।
असम्मूढः स मर्त्येषु सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते ॥
translation_en: He who knows me as unborn and beginningless, the great lord of the worlds, is undeluded among mortals and freed from all sins.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बुद्धिर्ज्ञानमसम्मोहः क्षमा सत्यं दमः शमः ।
सुखं दुःखं भवोऽभावो भयं चाभयमेव च ॥
translation_en: Understanding, knowledge, freedom from delusion, patience, truth, self-restraint, and calm; pleasure and pain, becoming and passing away, fear and fearlessness;

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अहिंसा समता तुष्टिस्तपो दानं यशोऽयशः ।
भवन्ति भावा भूतानां मत्त एव पृथग्विधाः ॥
translation_en: nonviolence, evenness, contentment, austerity, charity, fame and disrepute—these varied states of beings arise from me alone.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
महर्षयः सप्त पूर्वे चत्वारो मनवस्तथा ।
मद्भावा मानसा जाता येषां लोक इमाः प्रजाः ॥
translation_en: The seven great sages of old, and the four Manus too, sharing my nature, were born of my mind; from them sprang these creatures of the world.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एतां विभूतिं योगं च मम यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः ।
सोऽविकम्पेन योगेन युज्यते नात्र संशयः ॥
translation_en: He who knows in truth this glory and yoga of mine is yoked with unshaken yoga; of this there is no doubt.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अहं सर्वस्य प्रभवो मत्तः सर्वं प्रवर्तते ।
इति मत्वा भजन्ते मां बुधा भावसमन्विताः ॥
translation_en: I am the origin of all; from me everything proceeds. Knowing this, the wise, filled with feeling, worship me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मच्चित्ता मद्गतप्राणा बोधयन्तः परस्परम् ।
कथयन्तश्च मां नित्यं तुष्यन्ति च रमन्ति च ॥
translation_en: Their minds set on me, their lives given over to me, enlightening one another and ever speaking of me, they are content and glad.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तेषां सततयुक्तानां भजतां प्रीतिपूर्वकम् ।
ददामि बुद्धियोगं तं येन मामुपयान्ति ते ॥
translation_en: To these, ever yoked to me and worshiping me with love, I give that yoga of understanding by which they come to me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तेषामेवानुकम्पार्थमहमज्ञानजं तमः ।
नाशयाम्यात्मभावस्थो ज्ञानदीपेन भास्वता ॥
translation_en: Out of compassion for them, dwelling within their hearts, I destroy the darkness born of ignorance with the shining lamp of knowledge.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
परं ब्रह्म परं धाम पवित्रं परमं भवान् ।
पुरुषं शाश्वतं दिव्यमादिदेवमजं विभुम् ॥
translation_en: You are the supreme Brahman, the supreme abode, the supreme purifier; the eternal divine person, the primal god, unborn and all-pervading.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आहुस्त्वामृषयः सर्वे देवर्षिर्नारदस्तथा ।
असितो देवलो व्यासः स्वयं चैव ब्रवीषि मे ॥
translation_en: So all the sages declare you—the divine seer Narada, Asita, Devala, and Vyasa—and you yourself tell me so.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वमेतदृतं मन्ये यन्मां वदसि केशव ।
न हि ते भगवन्व्यक्तिं विदुर्देवा न दानवाः ॥
translation_en: I hold all this that you tell me to be true, O Keshava; for neither the gods nor the danavas know your manifestation, O Bhagavan.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
स्वयमेवात्मनात्मानं वेत्थ त्वं पुरुषोत्तम ।
भूतभावन भूतेश देवदेव जगत्पते ॥
translation_en: You alone know yourself by yourself, O Purushottama—source of beings, lord of beings, god of gods, lord of the world.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
वक्तुमर्हस्यशेषेण दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः ।
याभिर्विभूतिभिर्लोकानिमांस्त्वं व्याप्य तिष्ठसि ॥
translation_en: Tell me, then, in full, of your divine glories, by which you pervade these worlds and abide in them.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कथं विद्यामहं योगिंस्त्वां सदा परिचिन्तयन् ।
केषु केषु च भावेषु चिन्त्योऽसि भगवन्मया ॥
translation_en: How shall I know you, O yogin, by constant meditation? In what forms, O Bhagavan, are you to be contemplated by me?

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
विस्तरेणात्मनो योगं विभूतिं च जनार्दन ।
भूयः कथय तृप्तिर्हि श‍ृण्वतो नास्ति मेऽमृतम् ॥
translation_en: Tell me again, in detail, O Janardana, of your yoga and your glories; for I am not sated with hearing this nectar.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
हन्त ते कथयिष्यामि दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः ।
प्राधान्यतः कुरुश्रेष्ठ नास्त्यन्तो विस्तरस्य मे ॥
translation_en: So be it: I shall tell you of my divine glories—but only the chief of them, O best of the Kurus, for there is no end to my extent.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अहमात्मा गुडाकेश सर्वभूताशयस्थितः ।
अहमादिश्च मध्यं च भूतानामन्त एव च ॥
translation_en: I am the true Self (Atman), O Gudakesha, seated in the heart of all beings; I am the beginning, the middle, and the end of beings.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आदित्यानामहं विष्णुर्ज्योतिषां रविरंशुमान् ।
मरीचिर्मरुतामस्मि नक्षत्राणामहं शशी ॥
translation_en:
Among the Adityas I am Vishnu; among lights, the radiant sun;  
among the Maruts I am Marichi; among the stars, the moon.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
वेदानां सामवेदोऽस्मि देवानामस्मि वासवः ।
इन्द्रियाणां मनश्चास्मि भूतानामस्मि चेतना ॥
translation_en:
Among the Vedas I am the Sama Veda; among the gods, Vasava;  
among the senses I am the mind; in beings I am consciousness.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
रुद्राणां शङ्करश्चास्मि वित्तेशो यक्षरक्षसाम् ।
वसूनां पावकश्चास्मि मेरुः शिखरिणामहम् ॥
translation_en: Among the Rudras I am Shankara; among the yakshas and rakshasas, the lord of wealth; among the Vasus I am fire; among mountains, Meru.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पुरोधसां च मुख्यं मां विद्धि पार्थ बृहस्पतिम् ।
सेनानीनामहं स्कन्दः सरसामस्मि सागरः ॥
translation_en: Among household priests, O Partha, know me to be their chief, Brihaspati; among generals I am Skanda; among waters, the ocean.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
महर्षीणां भृगुरहं गिरामस्म्येकमक्षरम् ।
यज्ञानां जपयज्ञोऽस्मि स्थावराणां हिमालयः ॥
translation_en: Among the great sages I am Bhrigu; among utterances, the single syllable Om; among sacrifices, the sacrifice of silent repetition; among the unmoving, the Himalaya.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अश्वत्थः सर्ववृक्षाणां देवर्षीणां च नारदः ।
गन्धर्वाणां चित्ररथः सिद्धानां कपिलो मुनिः ॥
translation_en:
Among all trees I am the ashvattha; among divine sages, Narada;  
among the gandharvas, Chitraratha; among the perfected, the sage Kapila.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
उच्चैःश्रवसमश्वानां विद्धि माममृतोद्भवम् ।
ऐरावतं गजेन्द्राणां नराणां च नराधिपम् ॥
translation_en: Know me, among horses, as Uchchaihshravas, born of the nectar; among lordly elephants, Airavata; and among men, the king.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आयुधानामहं वज्रं धेनूनामस्मि कामधुक् ।
प्रजनश्चास्मि कन्दर्पः सर्पाणामस्मि वासुकिः ॥
translation_en:
Among weapons I am the thunderbolt; among cows, the wish-granting cow;  
among begetters I am Kandarpa; among serpents, Vasuki.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनन्तश्चास्मि नागानां वरुणो यादसामहम् ।
पितॄणामर्यमा चास्मि यमः संयमतामहम् ॥
translation_en:
Among the nagas I am Ananta; among water-dwellers, Varuna;  
among the ancestors, Aryaman; among those who govern, Yama.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रह्लादश्चास्मि दैत्यानां कालः कलयतामहम् ।
मृगाणां च मृगेन्द्रोऽहं वैनतेयश्च पक्षिणाम् ॥
translation_en: Among the daityas I am Prahlada; among reckoners, time itself; among beasts I am the lion, and among birds, Garuda.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पवनः पवतामस्मि रामः शस्त्रभृतामहम् ।
झषाणां मकरश्चास्मि स्रोतसामस्मि जाह्नवी ॥
translation_en: Among purifiers I am the wind; among warriors, Rama; among fishes I am the makara, and among rivers, the Ganges.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्गाणामादिरन्तश्च मध्यं चैवाहमर्जुन ।
अध्यात्मविद्या विद्यानां वादः प्रवदतामहम् ॥
translation_en: Of creations I am the beginning, the end, and the middle, Arjuna; of branches of knowledge, the knowledge of the self; among debaters, I am the reasoned argument.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अक्षराणामकारोऽस्मि द्वन्द्वः सामासिकस्य च ।
अहमेवाक्षयः कालो धाताहं विश्वतोमुखः ॥
translation_en:
Of letters I am the letter A; of compounds, the dual;  
I am imperishable time, and the sustainer facing in every direction.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मृत्युः सर्वहरश्चाहमुद्भवश्च भविष्यताम् ।
कीर्तिः श्रीर्वाक्च नारीणां स्मृतिर्मेधा धृतिः क्षमा ॥
translation_en:
I am all-devouring death, and the origin of things to come;  
among feminine powers I am fame, fortune, speech, memory, wisdom, steadfastness, and patience.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बृहत्साम तथा साम्नां गायत्री छन्दसामहम् ।
मासानां मार्गशीर्षोऽहमृतूनां कुसुमाकरः ॥
translation_en: Of the Sama chants I am the Brihat-sama; of meters, the Gayatri; of months I am Margashirsha; of seasons, the flowering spring.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
द्यूतं छलयतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम् ।
जयोऽस्मि व्यवसायोऽस्मि सत्त्वं सत्त्ववतामहम् ॥
translation_en: I am the gambling of the deceitful and the splendor of the splendid; I am victory, I am resolve, I am the sattva of the good.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
वृष्णीनां वासुदेवोऽस्मि पाण्डवानां धनञ्जयः ।
मुनीनामप्यहं व्यासः कवीनामुशना कविः ॥
translation_en:
Among the Vrishnis I am Vasudeva; among the Pandavas, Dhananjaya;  
among sages I am Vyasa; among seers, Ushanas the seer.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दण्डो दमयतामस्मि नीतिरस्मि जिगीषताम् ।
मौनं चैवास्मि गुह्यानां ज्ञानं ज्ञानवतामहम् ॥
translation_en: Among those who chastise I am the rod; among those who seek victory, statecraft; among secrets I am silence; and I am the knowledge of the knowing.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यच्चापि सर्वभूतानां बीजं तदहमर्जुन ।
न तदस्ति विना यत्स्यान्मया भूतं चराचरम् ॥
translation_en: And I am the seed of all beings, Arjuna; there is nothing moving or unmoving that could exist without me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नान्तोऽस्ति मम दिव्यानां विभूतीनां परन्तप ।
एष तूद्देशतः प्रोक्तो विभूतेर्विस्तरो मया ॥
translation_en: There is no end to my divine glories, O scorcher of foes; what I have declared of my extent is but a glimpse of it.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यद्यद्विभूतिमत्सत्त्वं श्रीमदूर्जितमेव वा ।
तत्तदेवावगच्छ त्वं मम तेजोंऽशसम्भवम् ॥
translation_en: Whatever being is glorious, prosperous, or mighty, know that it springs from a fragment of my splendor.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अथवा बहुनैतेन किं ज्ञातेन तवार्जुन ।
विष्टभ्याहमिदं कृत्स्नमेकांशेन स्थितो जगत् ॥
translation_en: But what need have you to know all this in detail, Arjuna? I stand supporting this whole world with a single fragment of myself.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मदनुग्रहाय परमं गुह्यमध्यात्मसंज्ञितम् ।
यत्त्वयोक्तं वचस्तेन मोहोऽयं विगतो मम ॥
translation_en: Out of grace toward me you have spoken the supreme secret known as the teaching of the self; by it this delusion of mine is dispelled.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
भवाप्ययौ हि भूतानां श्रुतौ विस्तरशो मया ।
त्वत्तः कमलपत्राक्ष माहात्म्यमपि चाव्ययम् ॥
translation_en: For I have heard from you in full of the coming-to-be and passing-away of beings, O lotus-eyed one, and of your imperishable majesty.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एवमेतद्यथात्थ त्वमात्मानं परमेश्वर ।
द्रष्टुमिच्छामि ते रूपमैश्वरं पुरुषोत्तम ॥
translation_en: As you describe yourself, O supreme lord, even so I long to see your sovereign form, O Purushottama.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मन्यसे यदि तच्छक्यं मया द्रष्टुमिति प्रभो ।
योगेश्वर ततो मे त्वं दर्शयात्मानमव्ययम् ॥
translation_en: If you think it can be seen by me, O lord, then show me your imperishable self, O lord of yoga.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पश्य मे पार्थ रूपाणि शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ।
नानाविधानि दिव्यानि नानावर्णाकृतीनि च ॥
translation_en: Behold, O Partha, my forms by the hundreds and thousands, of many kinds, divine, of many colors and shapes.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पश्यादित्यान्वसून्रुद्रानश्विनौ मरुतस्तथा ।
बहून्यदृष्टपूर्वाणि पश्याश्चर्याणि भारत ॥
translation_en:
See the Adityas, the Vasus, the Rudras, the two Ashvins, and the Maruts;  
see many wonders never seen before, O Bharata.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इहैकस्थं जगत्कृत्स्नं पश्याद्य सचराचरम् ।
मम देहे गुडाकेश यच्चान्यद् द्रष्टुमिच्छसि ॥
translation_en: See now, gathered here in one place, the whole world, moving and unmoving, within my body, O Gudakesha, and whatever else you wish to see.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न तु मां शक्यसे द्रष्टुमनेनैव स्वचक्षुषा ।
दिव्यं ददामि ते चक्षुः पश्य मे योगमैश्वरम् ॥
translation_en: But you cannot see me with this, your own eye; I give you a divine eye—behold my sovereign yoga.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
एवमुक्त्वा ततो राजन्महायोगेश्वरो हरिः ।
दर्शयामास पार्थाय परमं रूपमैश्वरम् ॥
translation_en: Having spoken thus, O king, Hari, the great lord of yoga, then showed Partha his supreme sovereign form:

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनेकवक्त्रनयनमनेकाद्भुतदर्शनम् ।
अनेकदिव्याभरणं दिव्यानेकोद्यतायुधम् ॥
translation_en: with many mouths and eyes, with many wondrous aspects, with many divine ornaments, and many divine weapons raised on high;

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
दिव्यमाल्याम्बरधरं दिव्यगन्धानुलेपनम् ।
सर्वाश्चर्यमयं देवमनन्तं विश्वतोमुखम् ॥
translation_en: wearing divine garlands and garments, anointed with divine perfumes, all-wondrous, resplendent, boundless, with faces on every side.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
दिवि सूर्यसहस्रस्य भवेद्युगपदुत्थिता ।
यदि भाः सदृशी सा स्याद्भासस्तस्य महात्मनः ॥
translation_en: If the light of a thousand suns were to blaze forth at once in the sky, it might resemble the splendor of that great being.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
तत्रैकस्थं जगत्कृत्स्नं प्रविभक्तमनेकधा ।
अपश्यद्देवदेवस्य शरीरे पाण्डवस्तदा ॥
translation_en: There the Pandava saw the whole world in its manifold divisions, gathered together in one place, in the body of the god of gods.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
ततः स विस्मयाविष्टो हृष्टरोमा धनञ्जयः ।
प्रणम्य शिरसा देवं कृताञ्जलिरभाषत ॥
translation_en: Then Dhananjaya, filled with wonder, his hair standing on end, bowed his head before the god and, with joined palms, spoke:

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पश्यामि देवांस्तव देव देहे
सर्वांस्तथा भूतविशेषसङ्घान् ।
ब्रह्माणमीशं कमलासनस्थ-
मृषींश्च सर्वानुरगांश्च दिव्यान् ॥
translation_en: I see all the gods in your body, O god, and the various hosts of beings—Brahma the lord seated on the lotus, the seers, and the divine serpents.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनेकबाहूदरवक्त्रनेत्रं
पश्यामि त्वां सर्वतोऽनन्तरूपम् ।
नान्तं न मध्यं न पुनस्तवादिं
पश्यामि विश्वेश्वर विश्वरूप ॥
translation_en:
I see you with countless arms, bellies, mouths, and eyes,  
endless on every side;  
I see in you no end, no middle, no beginning,  
O lord of all, O universal form.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
किरीटिनं गदिनं चक्रिणं च
तेजोराशिं सर्वतो दीप्तिमन्तम् ।
पश्यामि त्वां दुर्निरीक्ष्यं समन्ताद्
दीप्तानलार्कद्युतिमप्रमेयम् ॥
translation_en:
I see you with crown, mace, and discus,  
a mass of radiance blazing on every side,  
hard to look upon, immeasurable,  
like the flaming fire and the sun.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
त्वमक्षरं परमं वेदितव्यं
त्वमस्य विश्वस्य परं निधानम् ।
त्वमव्ययः शाश्वतधर्मगोप्ता
सनातनस्त्वं पुरुषो मतो मे ॥

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनादिमध्यान्तमनन्तवीर्य-
मनन्तबाहुं शशिसूर्यनेत्रम् ।
पश्यामि त्वां दीप्तहुताशवक्त्रं
स्वतेजसा विश्वमिदं तपन्तम् ॥
translation_en: I see you without beginning, middle, or end, of boundless power, with countless arms, the moon and sun your eyes, your face a blazing fire, scorching this whole universe with your radiance.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
द्यावापृथिव्योरिदमन्तरं हि
व्याप्तं त्वयैकेन दिशश्च सर्वाः ।
दृष्ट्वाद्भुतं रूपमुग्रं तवेदं
लोकत्रयं प्रव्यथितं महात्मन् ॥
translation_en: For this space between heaven and earth, and all the quarters, is pervaded by you alone; seeing this wondrous, terrible form of yours, the three worlds tremble, O great soul.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अमी हि त्वां सुरसङ्घा विशन्ति
केचिद्भीताः प्राञ्जलयो गृणन्ति ।
स्वस्तीत्युक्त्वा महर्षिसिद्धसङ्घाः
स्तुवन्ति त्वां स्तुतिभिः पुष्कलाभिः ॥

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
रुद्रादित्या वसवो ये च साध्या
विश्वेऽश्विनौ मरुतश्चोष्मपाश्च ।
गन्धर्वयक्षासुरसिद्धसङ्घा
वीक्षन्ते त्वां विस्मिताश्चैव सर्वे ॥
translation_en:
The Rudras, Adityas, Vasus, and Sadhyas,  
the Vishvedevas, the two Ashvins, the Maruts, and the ancestors,  
the hosts of gandharvas, yakshas, asuras, and the perfected—  
all gaze upon you in amazement.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
रूपं महत्ते बहुवक्त्रनेत्रं
महाबाहो बहुबाहूरुपादम् ।
बहूदरं बहुदंष्ट्राकरालं
दृष्ट्वा लोकाः प्रव्यथितास्तथाहम् ॥
translation_en:
Seeing your great form, O mighty-armed one, with its many mouths and eyes,  
its many arms, thighs, and feet,  
its many bellies, and terrible with many fangs—  
the worlds tremble, and so do I.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नभःस्पृशं दीप्तमनेकवर्णं
व्यात्ताननं दीप्तविशालनेत्रम् ।
दृष्ट्वा हि त्वां प्रव्यथितान्तरात्मा
धृतिं न विन्दामि शमं च विष्णो ॥
translation_en: Seeing you touch the sky, ablaze with many colors, your mouths gaping, your great eyes aflame, my inmost self trembles, O Vishnu; I find neither steadiness nor peace.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दंष्ट्राकरालानि च ते मुखानि
दृष्ट्वैव कालानलसन्निभानि ।
दिशो न जाने न लभे च शर्म
प्रसीद देवेश जगन्निवास ॥
translation_en: Seeing your mouths, terrible with fangs, like the fire at the world's end, I lose all sense of direction and find no refuge; be gracious, O lord of the gods, O dwelling of the world.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अमी च त्वां धृतराष्ट्रस्य पुत्राः
सर्वे सहैवावनिपालसङ्घैः ।
भीष्मो द्रोणः सूतपुत्रस्तथासौ
सहास्मदीयैरपि योधमुख्यैः ॥
translation_en: And all these sons of Dhritarashtra, along with the throng of kings—Bhishma, Drona, and that son of a charioteer too, together with the chief warriors on our side—

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
वक्त्राणि ते त्वरमाणा विशन्ति
दंष्ट्राकरालानि भयानकानि ।
केचिद्विलग्ना दशनान्तरेषु
सन्दृश्यन्ते चूर्णितैरुत्तमाङ्गैः ॥
translation_en:
rush into your mouths, fearsome with fangs;  
some are seen with their heads crushed, caught between your teeth.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यथा नदीनां बहवोऽम्बुवेगाः
समुद्रमेवाभिमुखा द्रवन्ति ।
तथा तवामी नरलोकवीरा
विशन्ति वक्त्राण्यभिविज्वलन्ति ॥
translation_en: As the many torrents of rivers rush toward the sea, so these heroes of the human world enter your blazing mouths.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यथा प्रदीप्तं ज्वलनं पतङ्गा
विशन्ति नाशाय समृद्धवेगाः ।
तथैव नाशाय विशन्ति लोका-
स्तवापि वक्त्राणि समृद्धवेगाः ॥
translation_en: As moths fly headlong into a blazing flame to their destruction, so do these worlds rush headlong into your mouths to their destruction.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
लेलिह्यसे ग्रसमानः समन्ताल्-
लोकान्समग्रान्वदनैर्ज्वलद्भिः ।
तेजोभिरापूर्य जगत्समग्रं
भासस्तवोग्राः प्रतपन्ति विष्णो ॥
translation_en:
You lick up all the worlds on every side with your flaming mouths, devouring them;  
your fierce rays fill the whole universe with light and scorch it, O Vishnu.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आख्याहि मे को भवानुग्ररूपो
नमोऽस्तु ते देववर प्रसीद ।
विज्ञातुमिच्छामि भवन्तमाद्यं
न हि प्रजानामि तव प्रवृत्तिम् ॥
translation_en: Tell me who you are, so terrible in form. Homage to you, O best of gods; be gracious. I long to know you, the primal one, for I do not comprehend your working.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कालोऽस्मि लोकक्षयकृत्प्रवृद्धो
लोकान्समाहर्तुमिह प्रवृत्तः ।
ऋतेऽपि त्वां न भविष्यन्ति सर्वे
येऽवस्थिताः प्रत्यनीकेषु योधाः ॥
translation_en: I am time, grown mighty, the destroyer of the worlds, here at work to gather the worlds in. Even without you, the warriors arrayed in the opposing ranks shall all cease to be.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तस्मात्त्वमुत्तिष्ठ यशो लभस्व
जित्वा शत्रून् भुङ्क्ष्व राज्यं समृद्धम् ।
मयैवैते निहताः पूर्वमेव
निमित्तमात्रं भव सव्यसाचिन् ॥
translation_en: Therefore rise up and win glory; conquer your foes and enjoy a flourishing kingdom. By me they are already slain; be only the instrument, O Savyasachin.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
द्रोणं च भीष्मं च जयद्रथं च
कर्णं तथान्यानपि योधवीरान् ।
मया हतांस्त्वं जहि मा व्यथिष्ठा
युध्यस्व जेतासि रणे सपत्नान् ॥
translation_en:
Drona, Bhishma, Jayadratha, Karna, and the other warrior-heroes as well—  
slay them, already slain by me; do not waver. Fight! You will conquer your rivals in battle.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
एतच्छ्रुत्वा वचनं केशवस्य
कृताञ्जलिर्वेपमानः किरीटी ।
नमस्कृत्वा भूय एवाह कृष्णं
सगद्गदं भीतभीतः प्रणम्य ॥
translation_en: Hearing these words of Keshava, the diademed one, trembling, with folded hands, bowed again to Krishna and, faltering and terrified, prostrate, spoke once more:

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
स्थाने हृषीकेश तव प्रकीर्त्या
जगत्प्रहृष्यत्यनुरज्यते च ।
रक्षांसि भीतानि दिशो द्रवन्ति
सर्वे नमस्यन्ति च सिद्धसङ्घाः ॥
translation_en: Rightly, O Hrishikesha, does the world rejoice and delight in your praise; the rakshasas flee in terror in every direction, and all the hosts of the perfected bow down.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कस्माच्च ते न नमेरन्महात्मन्
गरीयसे ब्रह्मणोऽप्यादिकर्त्रे ।
अनन्त देवेश जगन्निवास
त्वमक्षरं सदसत्तत्परं यत् ॥
translation_en: And why should they not bow to you, O great soul, greater even than Brahma, the first creator? O boundless one, lord of the gods, dwelling of the world, you are the imperishable—being, nonbeing, and what is beyond both.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
त्वमादिदेवः पुरुषः पुराण-
स्त्वमस्य विश्वस्य परं निधानम् ।
वेत्तासि वेद्यं च परं च धाम
त्वया ततं विश्वमनन्तरूप ॥

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
वायुर्यमोऽग्निर्वरुणः शशाङ्कः
प्रजापतिस्त्वं प्रपितामहश्च ।
नमो नमस्तेऽस्तु सहस्रकृत्वः
पुनश्च भूयोऽपि नमो नमस्ते ॥
translation_en:
You are Vayu, Yama, Agni, Varuna, the moon, Prajapati, and the great-grandsire;  
homage to you a thousandfold, and again and again, homage, homage to you.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नमः पुरस्तादथ पृष्ठतस्ते
नमोऽस्तु ते सर्वत एव सर्व ।
अनन्तवीर्यामितविक्रमस्त्वं
सर्वं समाप्नोषि ततोऽसि सर्वः ॥
translation_en:
Homage to you before and behind, homage to you on every side, O all;  
boundless in power and measureless in might, you encompass all, and so you are all.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सखेति मत्वा प्रसभं यदुक्तं
हे कृष्ण हे यादव हे सखेति ।
अजानता महिमानं तवेदं
मया प्रमादात्प्रणयेन वापि ॥
translation_en: Thinking you a friend, I addressed you rashly—'Krishna! Yadava! my friend!'—not knowing this greatness of yours, through heedlessness or through love.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यच्चावहासार्थमसत्कृतोऽसि
विहारशय्यासनभोजनेषु ।
एकोऽथवाप्यच्युत तत्समक्षं
तत्क्षामये त्वामहमप्रमेयम् ॥
translation_en: And if, in jest, I slighted you while at play, resting, sitting, or at meals, alone or before others, O Acyuta, I beg your forgiveness, O immeasurable one.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पितासि लोकस्य चराचरस्य
त्वमस्य पूज्यश्च गुरुर्गरीयान् ।
न त्वत्समोऽस्त्यभ्यधिकः कुतोऽन्यो
लोकत्रयेऽप्यप्रतिमप्रभाव ॥
translation_en: You are the father of this world, moving and unmoving, its object of worship and most venerable teacher; there is none equal to you—how then a greater?—O unrivaled in power in the three worlds.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तस्मात्प्रणम्य प्रणिधाय कायं
प्रसादये त्वामहमीशमीड्यम् ।
पितेव पुत्रस्य सखेव सख्युः
प्रियः प्रियायार्हसि देव सोढुम् ॥
translation_en: Therefore, bowing low and prostrating my body, I beg your grace, O lord worthy of praise; as a father with a son, a friend with a friend, a lover with the beloved, bear with me, O god.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अदृष्टपूर्वं हृषितोऽस्मि दृष्ट्वा
भयेन च प्रव्यथितं मनो मे ।
तदेव मे दर्शय देव रूपं
प्रसीद देवेश जगन्निवास ॥
translation_en: Glad am I to have seen what was never seen before, yet my mind trembles with fear; show me, O god, that very form again; be gracious, O lord of the gods, O dwelling of the world.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
किरीटिनं गदिनं चक्रहस्तं
इच्छामि त्वां द्रष्टुमहं तथैव ।
तेनैव रूपेण चतुर्भुजेन
सहस्रबाहो भव विश्वमूर्ते ॥
translation_en: I wish to see you as before, crowned, bearing mace and discus in hand; take on again that four-armed form, O thousand-armed one, O universal form.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मया प्रसन्नेन तवार्जुनेदं
रूपं परं दर्शितमात्मयोगात् ।
तेजोमयं विश्वमनन्तमाद्यं
यन्मे त्वदन्येन न दृष्टपूर्वम् ॥
translation_en: Out of grace toward you, Arjuna, by my own yoga I have shown you this supreme form—radiant, universal, boundless, primeval—which none but you has ever seen.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न वेदयज्ञाध्ययनैर्न दानै-
र्न च क्रियाभिर्न तपोभिरुग्रैः ।
एवंरूपः शक्य अहं नृलोके
द्रष्टुं त्वदन्येन कुरुप्रवीर ॥
translation_en: Not by the Vedas and sacrifices, not by study, gifts, rites, or severe austerities can I be seen in this form in the world of men by any but you, O foremost of the Kurus.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मा ते व्यथा मा च विमूढभावो
दृष्ट्वा रूपं घोरमीदृङ्ममेदम् ।
व्यपेतभीः प्रीतमनाः पुनस्त्वं
तदेव मे रूपमिदं प्रपश्य ॥
translation_en: Do not tremble, do not be bewildered, at seeing this terrible form of mine; with fear gone and glad heart, behold once more this very form of mine.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
इत्यर्जुनं वासुदेवस्तथोक्त्वा
स्वकं रूपं दर्शयामास भूयः ।
आश्वासयामास च भीतमेनं
भूत्वा पुनः सौम्यवपुर्महात्मा ॥
translation_en: Having spoken thus to Arjuna, Vasudeva showed his own form again; and the great soul, taking on once more his gentle shape, comforted the terrified one.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दृष्ट्वेदं मानुषं रूपं तव सौम्यं जनार्दन ।
इदानीमस्मि संवृत्तः सचेताः प्रकृतिं गतः ॥
translation_en: Seeing your gentle human form again, O Janardana, I have come to my senses and returned to my own nature.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सुदुर्दर्शमिदं रूपं दृष्टवानसि यन्मम ।
देवा अप्यस्य रूपस्य नित्यं दर्शनकाङ्क्षिणः ॥
translation_en: This form of mine that you have seen is very hard to behold; even the gods long forever to see this form.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नाहं वेदैर्न तपसा न दानेन न चेज्यया ।
शक्य एवंविधो द्रष्टुं दृष्टवानसि मां यथा ॥
translation_en: Not by the Vedas, nor by austerity, nor by gifts, nor by sacrifice can I be seen in the form in which you have seen me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
भक्त्या त्वनन्यया शक्य अहमेवंविधोऽर्जुन ।
ज्ञातुं द्रष्टुं च तत्त्वेन प्रवेष्टुं च परन्तप ॥
translation_en: But by undivided devotion, O Arjuna, I can be known, and seen, and truly entered into, O scorcher of foes.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मत्कर्मकृन्मत्परमो मद्भक्तः सङ्गवर्जितः ।
निर्वैरः सर्वभूतेषु यः स मामेति पाण्डव ॥
translation_en: He who does his work for me, who holds me as the highest, devoted to me, free of attachment, without enmity toward any being—he comes to me, O Pandava.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एवं सततयुक्ता ये भक्तास्त्वां पर्युपासते ।
ये चाप्यक्षरमव्यक्तं तेषां के योगवित्तमाः ॥
translation_en: Those devotees who, ever steadfast, worship you, and those who worship the imperishable and unmanifest—which of them better know yoga?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मय्यावेश्य मनो ये मां नित्ययुक्ता उपासते ।
श्रद्धया परयोपेताः ते मे युक्ततमा मताः ॥
translation_en: Those who fix their minds on me and, ever steadfast, worship me with supreme faith—them I hold to be the most perfect in yoga.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ये त्वक्षरमनिर्देश्यमव्यक्तं पर्युपासते ।
सर्वत्रगमचिन्त्यञ्च कूटस्थमचलन्ध्रुवम् ॥
translation_en: But those who worship the imperishable, the undefinable, the unmanifest, all-pervading and unthinkable, unchanging, fixed, and still—

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सन्नियम्येन्द्रियग्रामं सर्वत्र समबुद्धयः ।
ते प्राप्नुवन्ति मामेव सर्वभूतहिते रताः ॥
translation_en: having restrained the whole set of senses, even-minded in all circumstances, rejoicing in the welfare of all beings—they too attain me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
क्लेशोऽधिकतरस्तेषामव्यक्तासक्तचेतसाम् ।
अव्यक्ता हि गतिर्दुःखं देहवद्भिरवाप्यते ॥
translation_en: Greater is the toil of those whose minds are set on the unmanifest; for the unmanifest goal is hard for the embodied to reach.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ये तु सर्वाणि कर्माणि मयि संन्यस्य मत्पराः ।
अनन्येनैव योगेन मां ध्यायन्त उपासते ॥
translation_en: But those who, surrendering all actions to me, intent on me, worship me, meditating with undivided yoga,

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तेषामहं समुद्धर्ता मृत्युसंसारसागरात् ।
भवामि नचिरात्पार्थ मय्यावेशितचेतसाम् ॥
translation_en: For them, whose minds are absorbed in me, I soon become the deliverer from the ocean of death and samsara, O Partha.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मय्येव मन आधत्स्व मयि बुद्धिं निवेशय ।
निवसिष्यसि मय्येव अत ऊर्ध्वं न संशयः ॥
translation_en: Fix your mind on me alone, rest your understanding in me; thereafter you will dwell in me alone—of this there is no doubt.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अथ चित्तं समाधातुं न शक्नोषि मयि स्थिरम् ।
अभ्यासयोगेन ततो मामिच्छाप्तुं धनञ्जय ॥
translation_en: But if you cannot fix your mind steadily on me, then seek to reach me by the yoga of practice, O Dhananjaya.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अभ्यासेऽप्यसमर्थोऽसि मत्कर्मपरमो भव ।
मदर्थमपि कर्माणि कुर्वन्सिद्धिमवाप्स्यसि ॥
translation_en: If you are unable even to practice, be intent on working for me; doing your actions for my sake, you too will attain perfection.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अथैतदप्यशक्तोऽसि कर्तुं मद्योगमाश्रितः ।
सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं ततः कुरु यतात्मवान् ॥
translation_en: And if even this you cannot do, then, taking refuge in my yoga, with self subdued, give up the fruit of all action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
श्रेयो हि ज्ञानमभ्यासाज्ज्ञानाद्ध्यानं विशिष्यते ।
ध्यानात्कर्मफलत्यागस्त्यागाच्छान्तिरनन्तरम् ॥
translation_en: Knowledge is better than practice; meditation surpasses knowledge; better than meditation is the renouncing of the fruits of action—from such renouncing, peace follows at once.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अद्वेष्टा सर्वभूतानां मैत्रः करुण एव च ।
निर्ममो निरहङ्कारः समदुःखसुखः क्षमी ॥
translation_en: He who hates no being, who is friendly and compassionate, free of possessiveness and ego, even in pain and pleasure, and forgiving;

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सन्तुष्टः सततं योगी यतात्मा दृढनिश्चयः ।
मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः ॥
translation_en: ever content, the yogin self-controlled, of firm resolve, his mind and understanding given to me—he who is devoted to me is dear to me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यस्मान्नोद्विजते लोको लोकान्नोद्विजते च यः ।
हर्षामर्षभयोद्वेगैर्मुक्तो यः स च मे प्रियः ॥
translation_en: He from whom the world is not disturbed, and who is not disturbed by the world, who is free from joy, resentment, fear, and agitation—he is dear to me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनपेक्षः शुचिर्दक्ष उदासीनो गतव्यथः ।
सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः ॥
translation_en: He who is not dependent on anything, pure, capable, impartial, and untroubled, who has renounced all undertakings—that devotee of mine is dear to me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यो न हृष्यति न द्वेष्टि न शोचति न काङ्क्षति ।
शुभाशुभपरित्यागी भक्तिमान्यः स मे प्रियः ॥
translation_en: He who neither rejoices nor hates, neither grieves nor longs, who has given up concern with good and bad outcomes—that devotee of mine is dear to me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
समः शत्रौ च मित्रे च तथा मानापमानयोः ।
शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु समः सङ्गविवर्जितः ॥
translation_en: The same to foe and friend, the same in honor and dishonor, the same in cold and heat, in pleasure and pain, free of attachment;

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तुल्यनिन्दास्तुतिर्मौनी सन्तुष्टो येन केनचित् ।
अनिकेतः स्थिरमतिर्भक्तिमान्मे प्रियो नरः ॥
translation_en: weighing blame and praise alike, silent, content with whatever comes, without a fixed home, steady of mind, full of devotion—such a person is dear to me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ये तु धर्म्यामृतमिदं यथोक्तं पर्युपासते ।
श्रद्दधाना मत्परमा भक्तास्तेऽतीव मे प्रियाः ॥
translation_en: But those who honor this nectar of dharma, as I have told it, full of faith and holding me as the highest—those devotees are exceedingly dear to me.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव क्षेत्रं क्षेत्रज्ञमेव च ।
एतद्वेदितुमिच्छामि ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं च केशव ॥
translation_en: I wish to know prakriti and purusha, the field and the knower of the field, and knowledge and what is to be known, O Keshava.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इदं शरीरं कौन्तेय क्षेत्रमित्यभिधीयते ।
एतद्यो वेत्ति तं प्राहुः क्षेत्रज्ञ इति तद्विदः ॥
translation_en: This body, O son of Kunti, is called the field; and the one who knows it, those who know call the knower of the field.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
क्षेत्रज्ञं चापि मां विद्धि सर्वक्षेत्रेषु भारत ।
क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोर्ज्ञानं यत्तज्ज्ञानं मतं मम ॥
translation_en: Know me too, O Bharata, as the knower of the field in all fields; and the knowledge of the field and its knower—that, I hold, is true knowledge.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तत्क्षेत्रं यच्च यादृक्च यद्विकारि यतश्च यत् ।
स च यो यत्प्रभावश्च तत्समासेन मे श‍ृणु ॥
translation_en: What that field is, of what kind, with what changes, whence it arises, and who he is and what his powers are—hear this from me in brief.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ऋषिभिर्बहुधा गीतं छन्दोभिर्विविधैः पृथक् ।
ब्रह्मसूत्रपदैश्चैव हेतुमद्भिर्विनिश्चितैः ॥
translation_en: This has been sung by seers in many ways, in various hymns, and in the well-reasoned, definitive words of the aphorisms on Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
महाभूतान्यहङ्कारो बुद्धिरव्यक्तमेव च ।
इन्द्रियाणि दशैकं च पञ्च चेन्द्रियगोचराः ॥
translation_en: The great elements, the ego, the understanding, and the unmanifest; the ten senses and the one mind, and the five fields of sense;

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इच्छा द्वेषः सुखं दुःखं सङ्घातश्चेतना धृतिः ।
एतत्क्षेत्रं समासेन सविकारमुदाहृतम् ॥
translation_en: desire and aversion, pleasure and pain, the bodily whole, awareness, and steadfastness—this, in brief, is the field together with its changes.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अमानित्वमदम्भित्वमहिंसा क्षान्तिरार्जवम् ।
आचार्योपासनं शौचं स्थैर्यमात्मविनिग्रहः ॥
translation_en: Humility, sincerity, nonviolence, patience, uprightness, service to the teacher, purity, steadiness, and self-restraint;

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इन्द्रियार्थेषु वैराग्यमनहङ्कार एव च ।
जन्ममृत्युजराव्याधिदुःखदोषानुदर्शनम् ॥
translation_en: indifference to the objects of sense, absence of ego, and a clear sight of the evil in birth, death, old age, sickness, and pain;

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
असक्तिरनभिष्वङ्गः पुत्रदारगृहादिषु ।
नित्यं च समचित्तत्वमिष्टानिष्टोपपत्तिषु ॥
translation_en: non-attachment, freedom from clinging to son, wife, home, and the rest, and a constant evenness of mind toward the welcome and the unwelcome;

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मयि चानन्ययोगेन भक्तिरव्यभिचारिणी ।
विविक्तदेशसेवित्वमरतिर्जनसंसदि ॥
translation_en: unswerving devotion to me through undistracted yoga, resort to secluded places, and no delight in crowds of people;

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अध्यात्मज्ञाननित्यत्वं तत्त्वज्ञानार्थदर्शनम् ।
एतज्ज्ञानमिति प्रोक्तमज्ञानं यदतोऽन्यथा ॥
translation_en: constancy in knowledge of the self and insight into the aim of true knowledge—this is declared to be knowledge; what differs from it is ignorance.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ज्ञेयं यत्तत्प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वामृतमश्नुते ।
अनादिमत्परं ब्रह्म न सत्तन्नासदुच्यते ॥
translation_en: I shall declare what is to be known, knowing which one gains the deathless: the beginningless supreme Brahman, said to be neither being nor nonbeing.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वतः पाणिपादं तत्सर्वतोऽक्षिशिरोमुखम् ।
सर्वतः श्रुतिमल्लोके सर्वमावृत्य तिष्ठति ॥
translation_en: With hands and feet on every side, with eyes, heads, and faces on every side, with ears on every side, it abides, enfolding all in the world.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वेन्द्रियगुणाभासं सर्वेन्द्रियविवर्जितम् ।
असक्तं सर्वभृच्चैव निर्गुणं गुणभोक्तृ च ॥
translation_en: Shining through the powers of all the senses, yet free of all the senses; unattached, yet bearing all; without gunas, yet enjoying the gunas;

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बहिरन्तश्च भूतानामचरं चरमेव च ।
सूक्ष्मत्वात्तदविज्ञेयं दूरस्थं चान्तिके च तत् ॥
translation_en: outside and inside all beings; unmoving, yet moving; too subtle to be known; far away, and yet near.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अविभक्तं च भूतेषु विभक्तमिव च स्थितम् ।
भूतभर्तृ च तज्ज्ञेयं ग्रसिष्णु प्रभविष्णु च ॥
translation_en: Undivided, yet seeming divided among beings; this is to be known as the sustainer of beings, their devourer and their source.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ज्योतिषामपि तज्ज्योतिस्तमसः परमुच्यते ।
ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं ज्ञानगम्यं हृदि सर्वस्य विष्ठितम् ॥
translation_en: It is the light even of lights, said to be beyond darkness; it is knowledge, the object of knowledge, to be reached by knowledge, seated in the hearts of all.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इति क्षेत्रं तथा ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं चोक्तं समासतः ।
मद्भक्त एतद्विज्ञाय मद्भावायोपपद्यते ॥
translation_en: Thus the field, knowledge, and the knowable have been told in brief; one devoted to me, knowing this, becomes fit for my being.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव विद्ध्यनादी उभावपि ।
विकारांश्च गुणांश्चैव विद्धि प्रकृतिसम्भवान् ॥
translation_en: Know that both prakriti and purusha are without beginning; and know that the changes and the gunas are born of prakriti.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कार्यकारणकर्तृत्वे हेतुः प्रकृतिरुच्यते ।
पुरुषः सुखदुःखानां भोक्तृत्वे हेतुरुच्यते ॥
translation_en:
Prakriti is said to be the cause in the working of cause and effect;  
purusha is said to be the cause in the experiencing of pleasure and pain.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पुरुषः प्रकृतिस्थो हि भुङ्क्ते प्रकृतिजान्गुणान् ।
कारणं गुणसङ्गोऽस्य सदसद्योनिजन्मसु ॥
translation_en: For purusha, seated in prakriti, experiences the gunas born of prakriti; attachment to the gunas is the cause of its birth in good and evil wombs.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
उपद्रष्टानुमन्ता च भर्ता भोक्ता महेश्वरः ।
परमात्मेति चाप्युक्तो देहेऽस्मिन्पुरुषः परः ॥
translation_en: The supreme purusha in this body is also called the witness, the consenter, the bearer, the experiencer, the great lord, and the supreme Atman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
य एवं वेत्ति पुरुषं प्रकृतिं च गुणैः सह ।
सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि न स भूयोऽभिजायते ॥
translation_en: He who thus knows purusha and prakriti, together with the gunas, in whatever state he lives, is not born again.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ध्यानेनात्मनि पश्यन्ति केचिदात्मानमात्मना ।
अन्ये साङ्ख्येन योगेन कर्मयोगेन चापरे ॥
translation_en: Some, by meditation, see the self in the self by the self; others by the yoga of Sankhya, and others by the yoga of action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अन्ये त्वेवमजानन्तः श्रुत्वान्येभ्य उपासते ।
तेऽपि चातितरन्त्येव मृत्युं श्रुतिपरायणाः ॥
translation_en: Others, not knowing this, worship having heard it from others; they too, devoted to what they hear, cross beyond death.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यावत्सञ्जायते किञ्चित्सत्त्वं स्थावरजङ्गमम् ।
क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञसंयोगात्तद्विद्धि भरतर्षभ ॥
translation_en: Whatever being is born, moving or unmoving, know it to arise from the union of kshetra and kshetrajna, O best of the Bharatas.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
समं सर्वेषु भूतेषु तिष्ठन्तं परमेश्वरम् ।
विनश्यत्स्वविनश्यन्तं यः पश्यति स पश्यति ॥
translation_en: He who sees the supreme lord dwelling alike in all beings, undying amid the dying, truly sees.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
समं पश्यन्हि सर्वत्र समवस्थितमीश्वरम् ।
न हिनस्त्यात्मनात्मानं ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥
translation_en: For seeing the lord present the same everywhere, he does not harm the self by the self, and so reaches the supreme goal.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रकृत्यैव च कर्माणि क्रियमाणानि सर्वशः ।
यः पश्यति तथात्मानमकर्तारं स पश्यति ॥
translation_en: He who sees that actions are performed in every way by prakriti alone, and that the self is not the doer, truly sees.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदा भूतपृथग्भावमेकस्थमनुपश्यति ।
तत एव च विस्तारं ब्रह्म सम्पद्यते तदा ॥
translation_en: When he sees the separate states of beings as resting in the One, and their spreading forth from that alone, then he attains Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनादित्वान्निर्गुणत्वात्परमात्मायमव्ययः ।
शरीरस्थोऽपि कौन्तेय न करोति न लिप्यते ॥
translation_en: Beginningless and without gunas, this supreme self, imperishable, though seated in the body, O son of Kunti, neither acts nor is tainted.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यथा सर्वगतं सौक्ष्म्यादाकाशं नोपलिप्यते ।
सर्वत्रावस्थितो देहे तथात्मा नोपलिप्यते ॥
translation_en: As all-pervading space, being subtle, is not tainted, so the self, seated everywhere in the body, is not tainted.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यथा प्रकाशयत्येकः कृत्स्नं लोकमिमं रविः ।
क्षेत्रं क्षेत्री तथा कृत्स्नं प्रकाशयति भारत ॥
translation_en: As the one sun lights up this whole world, so the knower of the field lights up the whole field, O Bharata.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोरेवमन्तरं ज्ञानचक्षुषा ।
भूतप्रकृतिमोक्षं च ये विदुर्यान्ति ते परम् ॥
translation_en: Those who, with the eye of knowledge, thus discern the difference between kshetra and kshetrajna, and the freeing of beings from prakriti, reach the Supreme.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
परं भूयः प्रवक्ष्यामि ज्ञानानां ज्ञानमुत्तमम् ।
यज्ज्ञात्वा मुनयः सर्वे परां सिद्धिमितो गताः ॥
translation_en: I shall declare again the supreme knowledge, the best of all knowledge, by knowing which all the sages have passed from here to the highest perfection.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इदं ज्ञानमुपाश्रित्य मम साधर्म्यमागताः ।
सर्गेऽपि नोपजायन्ते प्रलये न व्यथन्ति च ॥
translation_en: Taking refuge in this knowledge, they come to share my nature; they are not born at creation, nor troubled at dissolution.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मम योनिर्महद् ब्रह्म तस्मिन्गर्भं दधाम्यहम् ।
सम्भवः सर्वभूतानां ततो भवति भारत ॥
translation_en:
The great Brahman is my womb; in it I place the seed,  
and from it, O Bharata, comes the birth of all beings.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वयोनिषु कौन्तेय मूर्तयः सम्भवन्ति याः ।
तासां ब्रह्म महद्योनिरहं बीजप्रदः पिता ॥
translation_en: Whatever forms take birth in all wombs, O son of Kunti, the great Brahman is their womb, and I am the father who gives the seed.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति गुणाः प्रकृतिसम्भवाः ।
निबध्नन्ति महाबाहो देहे देहिनमव्ययम् ॥
translation_en: Sattva, rajas, and tamas—the gunas born of prakriti—bind fast in the body, O mighty-armed one, the imperishable embodied one.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तत्र सत्त्वं निर्मलत्वात्प्रकाशकमनामयम् ।
सुखसङ्गेन बध्नाति ज्ञानसङ्गेन चानघ ॥
translation_en: Of these, sattva, being pure, is luminous and free of ill; it binds, O blameless one, by attachment to happiness and to knowledge.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
रजो रागात्मकं विद्धि तृष्णासङ्गसमुद्भवम् ।
तन्निबध्नाति कौन्तेय कर्मसङ्गेन देहिनम् ॥
translation_en: Know rajas to be of the nature of passion, born of craving and attachment; it binds the embodied one, O son of Kunti, by attachment to action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तमस्त्वज्ञानजं विद्धि मोहनं सर्वदेहिनाम् ।
प्रमादालस्यनिद्राभिस्तन्निबध्नाति भारत ॥
translation_en:
But know tamas, born of ignorance, to delude all embodied beings;  
it binds them by carelessness, sloth, and sleep, O Bharata.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सत्त्वं सुखे सञ्जयति रजः कर्मणि भारत ।
ज्ञानमावृत्य तु तमः प्रमादे सञ्जयत्युत ॥
translation_en: Sattva binds one to happiness, rajas to action, O Bharata; but tamas, veiling knowledge, binds one to carelessness.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
रजस्तमश्चाभिभूय सत्त्वं भवति भारत ।
रजः सत्त्वं तमश्चैव तमः सत्त्वं रजस्तथा ॥
translation_en: Sattva prevails by mastering rajas and tamas, O Bharata; rajas, by mastering sattva and tamas; and tamas likewise, by mastering sattva and rajas.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वद्वारेषु देहेऽस्मिन्प्रकाश उपजायते ।
ज्ञानं यदा तदा विद्याद्विवृद्धं सत्त्वमित्युत ॥
translation_en: When the light of knowledge shines forth through all the gates of this body, then one should know that sattva has grown strong.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
लोभः प्रवृत्तिरारम्भः कर्मणामशमः स्पृहा ।
रजस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे भरतर्षभ ॥
translation_en: Greed, busy activity, the undertaking of works, restlessness, and longing—these arise when rajas has grown strong, O best of the Bharatas.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अप्रकाशोऽप्रवृत्तिश्च प्रमादो मोह एव च ।
तमस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे कुरुनन्दन ॥
translation_en: Darkness, inertia, carelessness, and mere delusion—these arise when tamas has grown strong, O joy of the Kurus.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदा सत्त्वे प्रवृद्धे तु प्रलयं याति देहभृत् ।
तदोत्तमविदां लोकानमलान्प्रतिपद्यते ॥
translation_en: When the embodied one meets death while sattva prevails, he attains the spotless worlds of those who know the highest.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
रजसि प्रलयं गत्वा कर्मसङ्गिषु जायते ।
तथा प्रलीनस्तमसि मूढयोनिषु जायते ॥
translation_en:
Meeting death in rajas, he is born among those attached to action;  
and dying in tamas, he is born in the wombs of the deluded.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कर्मणः सुकृतस्याहुः सात्त्विकं निर्मलं फलम् ।
रजसस्तु फलं दुःखमज्ञानं तमसः फलम् ॥
translation_en: The fruit of good action, they say, is of the nature of sattva and untainted; but the fruit of rajas is pain, and the fruit of tamas is ignorance.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सत्त्वात्सञ्जायते ज्ञानं रजसो लोभ एव च ।
प्रमादमोहौ तमसो भवतोऽज्ञानमेव च ॥
translation_en: From sattva is born knowledge, from rajas greed, and from tamas come carelessness, delusion, and ignorance.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ऊर्ध्वं गच्छन्ति सत्त्वस्था मध्ये तिष्ठन्ति राजसाः ।
जघन्यगुणवृत्तिस्था अधो गच्छन्ति तामसाः ॥
translation_en: Those settled in sattva rise upward; those in rajas stay in the middle; those in tamas, settled in the lowest disposition, sink downward.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नान्यं गुणेभ्यः कर्तारं यदा द्रष्टानुपश्यति ।
गुणेभ्यश्च परं वेत्ति मद्भावं सोऽधिगच्छति ॥
translation_en: When the seer perceives no doer other than the gunas, and knows what is beyond the gunas, he attains my being.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
गुणानेतानतीत्य त्रीन्देही देहसमुद्भवान् ।
जन्ममृत्युजरादुःखैर्विमुक्तोऽमृतमश्नुते ॥
translation_en: Passing beyond these three gunas, which are the source of the body, the embodied one is freed from birth, death, old age, and sorrow, and attains the deathless.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कैर्लिङ्गैस्त्रीन्गुणानेतानतीतो भवति प्रभो ।
किमाचारः कथं चैतांस्त्रीन्गुणानतिवर्तते ॥
translation_en: By what marks is the one who has passed beyond these three gunas known, O lord? How does he act, and how does he cross beyond these three gunas?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रकाशं च प्रवृत्तिं च मोहमेव च पाण्डव ।
न द्वेष्टि सम्प्रवृत्तानि न निवृत्तानि काङ्क्षति ॥
translation_en: He neither shuns light, activity, or delusion when they come, O Pandava, nor longs for them when they go;

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
उदासीनवदासीनो गुणैर्यो न विचाल्यते ।
गुणा वर्तन्त इत्येवं योऽवतिष्ठति नेङ्गते ॥
translation_en: seated apart, as it were, unmoved by the gunas, he stands firm and does not waver, knowing that it is only the gunas at work;

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
समदुःखसुखः स्वस्थः समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चनः ।
तुल्यप्रियाप्रियो धीरस्तुल्यनिन्दात्मसंस्तुतिः ॥
translation_en: alike in pain and pleasure, self-possessed, holding clod, stone, and gold as one, the same toward the dear and the unwelcome, steadfast, the same under blame and praise;

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मानापमानयोस्तुल्यस्तुल्यो मित्रारिपक्षयोः ।
सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी गुणातीतः स उच्यते ॥
translation_en: the same in honor and dishonor, the same toward friend and foe, giving up all undertakings—he is said to have gone beyond the gunas.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मां च योऽव्यभिचारेण भक्तियोगेन सेवते ।
स गुणान्समतीत्यैतान्ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते ॥
translation_en: And he who serves me with unswerving devotion, passing beyond these gunas, becomes fit to become Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ब्रह्मणो हि प्रतिष्ठाहममृतस्याव्ययस्य च ।
शाश्वतस्य च धर्मस्य सुखस्यैकान्तिकस्य च ॥
translation_en: For I am the ground of Brahman, of the deathless and imperishable, of eternal dharma, and of absolute bliss.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ऊर्ध्वमूलमधःशाखमश्वत्थं प्राहुरव्ययम् ।
छन्दांसि यस्य पर्णानि यस्तं वेद स वेदवित् ॥
translation_en: They speak of an imperishable ashvattha tree, its roots above and branches below, whose leaves are the hymns; he who knows it knows the Vedas.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अधश्चोर्ध्वं प्रसृतास्तस्य शाखा
गुणप्रवृद्धा विषयप्रवालाः ।
अधश्च मूलान्यनुसन्ततानि
कर्मानुबन्धीनि मनुष्यलोके ॥
translation_en: Its branches spread below and above, nourished by the gunas, with the objects of sense for buds; and below, its roots stretch on, binding to action in the world of men.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न रूपमस्येह तथोपलभ्यते
नान्तो न चादिर्न च सम्प्रतिष्ठा ।
अश्वत्थमेनं सुविरूढमूलं
असङ्गशस्त्रेण दृढेन छित्त्वा ॥
translation_en: Its form is not perceived here as it is—no end, no beginning, no resting place. Having cut down this firm-rooted ashvattha with the strong axe of non-attachment,

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ततः पदं तत्परिमार्गितव्यं
यस्मिन्गता न निवर्तन्ति भूयः ।
तमेव चाद्यं पुरुषं प्रपद्ये ।
यतः प्रवृत्तिः प्रसृता पुराणी ॥
translation_en: one must then seek that place from which, having gone, none return again. 'I take refuge in that primal person, from whom the ancient unfolding streamed forth.'

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
निर्मानमोहा जितसङ्गदोषा
अध्यात्मनित्या विनिवृत्तकामाः ।
द्वन्द्वैर्विमुक्ताः सुखदुःखसंज्ञै-
र्गच्छन्त्यमूढाः पदमव्ययं तत् ॥
translation_en: Free of pride and delusion, the fault of attachment conquered, ever dwelling in the self, desire stilled, released from the pairs of opposites known as pleasure and pain, the undeluded reach that imperishable place.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न तद्भासयते सूर्यो न शशाङ्को न पावकः ।
यद्गत्वा न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम ॥
translation_en: Neither sun nor moon nor fire lights up that place; having gone there, they do not return—that is my supreme abode.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ममैवांशो जीवलोके जीवभूतः सनातनः ।
मनःषष्ठानीन्द्रियाणि प्रकृतिस्थानि कर्षति ॥
translation_en: A portion of myself, become an eternal living soul in the world of the living, draws to itself the senses, with the mind as the sixth, that rest in prakriti.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
शरीरं यदवाप्नोति यच्चाप्युत्क्रामतीश्वरः ।
गृहीत्वैतानि संयाति वायुर्गन्धानिवाशयात् ॥
translation_en: When it takes on a body, and when it departs from one, it carries these along, as the wind carries scents from their places.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
श्रोत्रं चक्षुः स्पर्शनं च रसनं घ्राणमेव च ।
अधिष्ठाय मनश्चायं विषयानुपसेवते ॥
translation_en: Presiding over hearing, sight, touch, taste, and smell, and over the mind, it experiences the objects of sense.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
उत्क्रामन्तं स्थितं वापि भुञ्जानं वा गुणान्वितम् ।
विमूढा नानुपश्यन्ति पश्यन्ति ज्ञानचक्षुषः ॥
translation_en: The deluded do not see it as it departs, abides, or experiences, joined with the gunas; those with the eye of knowledge see it.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यतन्तो योगिनश्चैनं पश्यन्त्यात्मन्यवस्थितम् ।
यतन्तोऽप्यकृतात्मानो नैनं पश्यन्त्यचेतसः ॥
translation_en: The yogins who strive see it seated in the self; but the witless, their selves untrained, do not see it, even though they strive.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदादित्यगतं तेजो जगद्भासयतेऽखिलम् ।
यच्चन्द्रमसि यच्चाग्नौ तत्तेजो विद्धि मामकम् ॥
translation_en: The light that dwells in the sun and lights up the whole world, and that which is in the moon and in fire—know that light to be mine.
