speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
गामाविश्य च भूतानि धारयाम्यहमोजसा ।
पुष्णामि चौषधीः सर्वाः सोमो भूत्वा रसात्मकः ॥
translation_en: And entering the earth, I uphold all beings by my power; becoming the moon, full of sap, I nourish all the plants.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अहं वैश्वानरो भूत्वा प्राणिनां देहमाश्रितः ।
प्राणापानसमायुक्तः पचाम्यन्नं चतुर्विधम् ॥
translation_en: Becoming the fire of digestion, dwelling in the bodies of living beings, joined with the inward and outward breaths, I digest the four kinds of food.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वस्य चाहं हृदि सन्निविष्टो
मत्तः स्मृतिर्ज्ञानमपोहनञ्च ।
वेदैश्च सर्वैरहमेव वेद्यो
वेदान्तकृद्वेदविदेव चाहम् ॥
translation_en:
I am seated in the heart of all; from me come memory, knowledge, and their loss;  
I am what is to be known through all the Vedas; I am the author of the Vedanta and the knower of the Vedas.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
द्वाविमौ पुरुषौ लोके क्षरश्चाक्षर एव च ।
क्षरः सर्वाणि भूतानि कूटस्थोऽक्षर उच्यते ॥
translation_en: There are two persons in the world, the perishable and the imperishable: the perishable is all beings, the unchanging is called the imperishable.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
उत्तमः पुरुषस्त्वन्यः परमात्मेत्युदाहृतः ।
यो लोकत्रयमाविश्य बिभर्त्यव्यय ईश्वरः ॥
translation_en: But there is another, the supreme person, called the supreme Atman, the imperishable lord who enters the three worlds and sustains them.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यस्मात्क्षरमतीतोऽहमक्षरादपि चोत्तमः ।
अतोऽस्मि लोके वेदे च प्रथितः पुरुषोत्तमः ॥
translation_en: Since I surpass the perishable and am higher even than the imperishable, I am renowned in the world and in the Vedas as Purushottama, the supreme person.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यो मामेवमसम्मूढो जानाति पुरुषोत्तमम् ।
स सर्वविद्भजति मां सर्वभावेन भारत ॥
translation_en: He who, undeluded, thus knows me as the supreme person knows all; he worships me with his whole being, O Bharata.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इति गुह्यतमं शास्त्रमिदमुक्तं मयानघ ।
एतद्बुद्ध्वा बुद्धिमान्स्यात्कृतकृत्यश्च भारत ॥
translation_en: Thus I have taught you this most secret teaching, O blameless one; knowing it, one becomes wise, his task accomplished, O Bharata.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अभयं सत्त्वसंशुद्धिर्ज्ञानयोगव्यवस्थितिः ।
दानं दमश्च यज्ञश्च स्वाध्यायस्तप आर्जवम् ॥
translation_en:
Fearlessness, purity of heart, steadfastness in the yoga of knowledge,  
charity, self-restraint, sacrifice, study of scripture, austerity, and uprightness;

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अहिंसा सत्यमक्रोधस्त्यागः शान्तिरपैशुनम् ।
दया भूतेष्वलोलुप्त्वं मार्दवं ह्रीरचापलम् ॥
translation_en: nonviolence, truth, freedom from anger, renunciation, calm, freedom from slander, compassion for all beings, freedom from greed, gentleness, modesty, and steadiness;

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तेजः क्षमा धृतिः शौचमद्रोहो नातिमानिता ।
भवन्ति सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातस्य भारत ॥
translation_en: vigor, patience, fortitude, purity, freedom from malice and from pride—these belong to one born to a divine lot, O Bharata.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दम्भो दर्पोऽभिमानश्च क्रोधः पारुष्यमेव च ।
अज्ञानं चाभिजातस्य पार्थ सम्पदमासुरीम् ॥
translation_en: Hypocrisy, arrogance, conceit, anger, harshness, and ignorance belong, O Partha, to one born to a demonic lot.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दैवी सम्पद्विमोक्षाय निबन्धायासुरी मता ।
मा शुचः सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातोऽसि पाण्डव ॥
translation_en: The divine lot leads to liberation, the demonic to bondage; do not grieve, O Pandava—you are born to a divine lot.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
द्वौ भूतसर्गौ लोकेऽस्मिन्दैव आसुर एव च ।
दैवो विस्तरशः प्रोक्त आसुरं पार्थ मे श‍ृणु ॥
translation_en: In this world there are two orders of created beings, the divine and the demonic; the divine has been described at length, now hear from me of the demonic, O Partha.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रवृत्तिं च निवृत्तिं च जना न विदुरासुराः ।
न शौचं नापि चाचारो न सत्यं तेषु विद्यते ॥
translation_en: The demonic do not know either action or its cessation; in them is found neither purity, nor right conduct, nor truth.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
असत्यमप्रतिष्ठं ते जगदाहुरनीश्वरम् ।
अपरस्परसम्भूतं किमन्यत्कामहैतुकम् ॥
translation_en: They say the world is without truth, without ground, without a lord, arising from no orderly cause, brought about by desire alone.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एतां दृष्टिमवष्टभ्य नष्टात्मानोऽल्पबुद्धयः ।
प्रभवन्त्युग्रकर्माणः क्षयाय जगतोऽहिताः ॥
translation_en: Holding to this view, these lost souls of feeble understanding and cruel deeds rise up as enemies for the ruin of the world.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
काममाश्रित्य दुष्पूरं दम्भमानमदान्विताः ।
मोहाद्गृहीत्वासद्ग्राहान्प्रवर्तन्तेऽशुचिव्रताः ॥
translation_en: Clinging to insatiable desire, full of hypocrisy, pride, and arrogance, holding false notions through delusion, they act on impure resolves.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
चिन्तामपरिमेयां च प्रलयान्तामुपाश्रिताः ।
कामोपभोगपरमा एतावदिति निश्चिताः ॥
translation_en: Given over to measureless cares that end only with death, holding the satisfaction of desires to be the highest, certain that this is all,

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आशापाशशतैर्बद्धाः कामक्रोधपरायणाः ।
ईहन्ते कामभोगार्थमन्यायेनार्थसञ्चयान् ॥
translation_en: bound by a hundred cords of hope, devoted to desire and anger, they strive to amass hoards of wealth, by foul means, for the enjoyment of desires.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इदमद्य मया लब्धमिमं प्राप्स्ये मनोरथम् ।
इदमस्तीदमपि मे भविष्यति पुनर्धनम् ॥
translation_en: "This I have gained today; this longing I shall now fulfill; this is mine, and this wealth too shall be mine;

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
असौ मया हतः शत्रुर्हनिष्ये चापरानपि ।
ईश्वरोऽहमहं भोगी सिद्धोऽहं बलवान्सुखी ॥
translation_en: that foe I have slain, and I shall slay the others too; I am the lord, I am the enjoyer, I am successful, strong, and happy;

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आढ्योऽभिजनवानस्मि कोऽन्योऽस्ति सदृशो मया ।
यक्ष्ये दास्यामि मोदिष्य इत्यज्ञानविमोहिताः ॥
translation_en: I am rich and high-born; who else is equal to me? I will sacrifice, I will give, I will rejoice"—so they say, deluded by ignorance.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनेकचित्तविभ्रान्ता मोहजालसमावृताः ।
प्रसक्ताः कामभोगेषु पतन्ति नरकेऽशुचौ ॥
translation_en: Led astray by many fancies, caught in the net of delusion, addicted to the enjoyment of desires, they fall into a foul hell.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आत्मसम्भाविताः स्तब्धा धनमानमदान्विताः ।
यजन्ते नामयज्ञैस्ते दम्भेनाविधिपूर्वकम् ॥
translation_en: Self-important, stubborn, filled with the pride and intoxication of wealth, they offer sacrifices in name only, with show and contrary to the rules.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अहङ्कारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं च संश्रिताः ।
मामात्मपरदेहेषु प्रद्विषन्तोऽभ्यसूयकाः ॥
translation_en: Clinging to ego, force, insolence, desire, and anger, these spiteful people hate me in their own and others' bodies.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तानहं द्विषतः क्रूरान्संसारेषु नराधमान् ।
क्षिपाम्यजस्रमशुभानासुरीष्वेव योनिषु ॥
translation_en: These haters, cruel and base among men, the worst of the world, I hurl again and again into demonic wombs, into the rounds of samsara.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आसुरीं योनिमापन्ना मूढा जन्मनि जन्मनि ।
मामप्राप्यैव कौन्तेय ततो यान्त्यधमां गतिम् ॥
translation_en: Cast into demonic wombs, birth after birth, the deluded, never reaching me, O son of Kunti, sink from there to the lowest state.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
त्रिविधं नरकस्येदं द्वारं नाशनमात्मनः ।
कामः क्रोधस्तथा लोभस्तस्मादेतत्त्रयं त्यजेत् ॥
translation_en: Threefold is this gate of hell, ruin of the self: desire, anger, and greed; therefore one should give up these three.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एतैर्विमुक्तः कौन्तेय तमोद्वारैस्त्रिभिर्नरः ।
आचरत्यात्मनः श्रेयस्ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥
translation_en: Freed from these three gates of darkness, O son of Kunti, a person works for his own good and so reaches the supreme goal.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यः शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य वर्तते कामकारतः ।
न स सिद्धिमवाप्नोति न सुखं न परां गतिम् ॥
translation_en: But he who casts aside the rule of scripture and acts on the urge of desire reaches neither perfection, nor happiness, nor the supreme goal.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तस्माच्छास्त्रं प्रमाणं ते कार्याकार्यव्यवस्थितौ ।
ज्ञात्वा शास्त्रविधानोक्तं कर्म कर्तुमिहार्हसि ॥
translation_en: Therefore let scripture be your measure in deciding what should and should not be done; knowing what the rule of scripture declares, you should act here in the world.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ये शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य यजन्ते श्रद्धयान्विताः ।
तेषां निष्ठा तु का कृष्ण सत्त्वमाहो रजस्तमः ॥
translation_en: Those who, setting aside the rule of scripture, yet worship with faith—what is their standing, O Krishna? Sattva, rajas, or tamas?

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
त्रिविधा भवति श्रद्धा देहिनां सा स्वभावजा ।
सात्त्विकी राजसी चैव तामसी चेति तां श‍ृणु ॥
translation_en: The faith of the embodied is of three kinds, born of their own nature—sattvic, rajasic, and tamasic; hear of it.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सत्त्वानुरूपा सर्वस्य श्रद्धा भवति भारत ।
श्रद्धामयोऽयं पुरुषो यो यच्छ्रद्धः स एव सः ॥
translation_en: The faith of each, O Bharata, accords with his nature; a person is made of faith—as his faith is, so is he.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यजन्ते सात्त्विका देवान्यक्षरक्षांसि राजसाः ।
प्रेतान्भूतगणांश्चान्ये यजन्ते तामसा जनाः ॥
translation_en: The sattvic worship the gods; the rajasic, the yakshas and rakshasas; and the others, the tamasic, worship the dead and the hosts of spirits.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अशास्त्रविहितं घोरं तप्यन्ते ये तपो जनाः ।
दम्भाहङ्कारसंयुक्ताः कामरागबलान्विताः ॥
translation_en: Those who practice fierce austerities not enjoined by scripture, full of hypocrisy and ego, driven by the force of desire and attachment,

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कर्षयन्तः शरीरस्थं भूतग्राममचेतसः ।
मां चैवान्तःशरीरस्थं तान्विद्ध्यासुरनिश्चयान् ॥
translation_en: tormenting, in their folly, the cluster of elements within the body, and me too who dwell within—know them to be of demonic resolve.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आहारस्त्वपि सर्वस्य त्रिविधो भवति प्रियः ।
यज्ञस्तपस्तथा दानं तेषां भेदमिमं श‍ृणु ॥
translation_en: Food, too, dear to each, is of three kinds, and so are sacrifice, austerity, and giving; hear how they differ.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
आयुःसत्त्वबलारोग्यसुखप्रीतिविवर्धनाः ।
रस्याः स्निग्धाः स्थिरा हृद्या आहाराः सात्त्विकप्रियाः ॥
translation_en: Foods that are juicy, smooth, firm, and pleasing to the heart are dear to the sattvic; they foster life, vitality, strength, health, ease, and good cheer.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कट्वम्ललवणात्युष्णतीक्ष्णरूक्षविदाहिनः ।
आहारा राजसस्येष्टा दुःखशोकामयप्रदाः ॥
translation_en: Foods that are bitter, sour, salty, very hot, sharp, harsh, and burning are craved by the rajasic, and bring pain, grief, and sickness.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यातयामं गतरसं पूति पर्युषितं च यत् ।
उच्छिष्टमपि चामेध्यं भोजनं तामसप्रियम् ॥
translation_en: Food that is stale, tasteless, putrid, and left overnight, leavings, and what is unfit for offering, is dear to the tamasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्यज्ञो विधिदृष्टो य इज्यते ।
यष्टव्यमेवेति मनः समाधाय स सात्त्विकः ॥
translation_en: The sacrifice offered as enjoined, by those who seek no fruit, their minds settled that it is simply to be done—that is sattvic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अभिसन्धाय तु फलं दम्भार्थमपि चैव यत् ।
इज्यते भरतश्रेष्ठ तं यज्ञं विद्धि राजसम् ॥
translation_en: But the sacrifice offered with an eye to its fruit, or for show, know that to be rajasic, O best of the Bharatas.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
विधिहीनमसृष्टान्नं मन्त्रहीनमदक्षिणम् ।
श्रद्धाविरहितं यज्ञं तामसं परिचक्षते ॥
translation_en: A sacrifice offered against the rules, with no food distributed, without sacred words, gifts, or faith, they call tamasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
देवद्विजगुरुप्राज्ञपूजनं शौचमार्जवम् ।
ब्रह्मचर्यमहिंसा च शारीरं तप उच्यते ॥
translation_en: Worship of the gods, the brahmins, teachers, and the wise; purity, uprightness, celibacy, and nonviolence—these are called austerity of the body.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनुद्वेगकरं वाक्यं सत्यं प्रियहितं च यत् ।
स्वाध्यायाभ्यसनं चैव वाङ्मयं तप उच्यते ॥
translation_en: Speech that gives no offense, truthful, kindly, and beneficial, and the regular recitation of scripture—this is called austerity of speech.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मनः प्रसादः सौम्यत्वं मौनमात्मविनिग्रहः ।
भावसंशुद्धिरित्येतत्तपो मानसमुच्यते ॥
translation_en: Serenity of mind, gentleness, silence, self-control, and purity of heart—this is called austerity of the mind.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
श्रद्धया परया तप्तं तपस्तत्त्रिविधं नरैः ।
अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्युक्तैः सात्त्विकं परिचक्षते ॥
translation_en: This threefold austerity, practiced with supreme faith by the steadfast who seek no fruit, is called sattvic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सत्कारमानपूजार्थं तपो दम्भेन चैव यत् ।
क्रियते तदिह प्रोक्तं राजसं चलमध्रुवम् ॥
translation_en: Austerity done for the sake of respect, honor, and reverence, or with hypocrisy, is called rajasic here—unsteady and fleeting.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मूढग्राहेणात्मनो यत्पीडया क्रियते तपः ।
परस्योत्सादनार्थं वा तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥
translation_en: Austerity done with deluded obstinacy, by torturing oneself, or to harm another, is called tamasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दातव्यमिति यद्दानं दीयतेऽनुपकारिणे ।
देशे काले च पात्रे च तद्दानं सात्त्विकं स्मृतम् ॥
translation_en: A gift given because it ought to be given, with no thought of return, in the right place and time and to a worthy person, is held to be sattvic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यत्तु प्रत्युपकारार्थं फलमुद्दिश्य वा पुनः ।
दीयते च परिक्लिष्टं तद्दानं राजसं स्मृतम् ॥
translation_en: But a gift given for the sake of return, or with an eye to its fruit, or grudgingly, is held to be rajasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अदेशकाले यद्दानमपात्रेभ्यश्च दीयते ।
असत्कृतमवज्ञातं तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥
translation_en: A gift given at the wrong place and time, to the unworthy, without respect or with contempt, is called tamasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ॐतत्सदिति निर्देशो ब्रह्मणस्त्रिविधः स्मृतः ।
ब्राह्मणास्तेन वेदाश्च यज्ञाश्च विहिताः पुरा ॥
translation_en: Om Tat Sat—this is held to be the threefold designation of Brahman; by it the brahmins, the Vedas, and the sacrifices were ordained of old.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तस्मादोमित्युदाहृत्य यज्ञदानतपःक्रियाः ।
प्रवर्तन्ते विधानोक्ताः सततं ब्रह्मवादिनाम् ॥
translation_en: Therefore the acts of sacrifice, gift, and austerity prescribed by the rules are always begun with the utterance 'Om' by the expounders of Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तदित्यनभिसन्धाय फलं यज्ञतपःक्रियाः ।
दानक्रियाश्च विविधाः क्रियन्ते मोक्षकाङ्क्षिभिः ॥
translation_en: With 'Tat,' and seeking no fruit, the various acts of sacrifice, austerity, and gift are performed by those who long for liberation.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सद्भावे साधुभावे च सदित्येतत्प्रयुज्यते ।
प्रशस्ते कर्मणि तथा सच्छब्दः पार्थ युज्यते ॥
translation_en: The word 'Sat' is used for what is real and for what is good; and 'Sat,' O Partha, is also applied to a praiseworthy act.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यज्ञे तपसि दाने च स्थितिः सदिति चोच्यते ।
कर्म चैव तदर्थीयं सदित्येवाभिधीयते ॥
translation_en: Steadfastness in sacrifice, austerity, and gift is also called 'Sat,' and action for the sake of that is likewise called 'Sat.'

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अश्रद्धया हुतं दत्तं तपस्तप्तं कृतं च यत् ।
असदित्युच्यते पार्थ न च तत्प्रेत्य नो इह ॥
translation_en: Whatever is offered or given or done as austerity without faith is called 'Asat,' O Partha; it counts for nothing, here or hereafter.

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
संन्यासस्य महाबाहो तत्त्वमिच्छामि वेदितुम् ।
त्यागस्य च हृषीकेश पृथक्केशिनिषूदन ॥
translation_en: I wish to know, O mighty-armed one, the truth of renunciation, and of relinquishment as well, each apart, O Hrishikesha, slayer of Keshin.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
काम्यानां कर्मणां न्यासं संन्यासं कवयो विदुः ।
सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं प्राहुस्त्यागं विचक्षणाः ॥
translation_en: The giving up of desire-driven actions, the sages call renunciation; the surrender of the fruit of all actions, the discerning call relinquishment.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
त्याज्यं दोषवदित्येके कर्म प्राहुर्मनीषिणः ।
यज्ञदानतपःकर्म न त्याज्यमिति चापरे ॥
translation_en: Action should be given up as an evil, say some thoughtful men; others say the acts of sacrifice, gift, and austerity should not be given up.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
निश्चयं श‍ृणु मे तत्र त्यागे भरतसत्तम ।
त्यागो हि पुरुषव्याघ्र त्रिविधः सम्प्रकीर्तितः ॥
translation_en: Hear my conclusion on this relinquishment, O best of the Bharatas; for relinquishment, O tiger among men, is declared to be of three kinds.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यज्ञदानतपःकर्म न त्याज्यं कार्यमेव तत् ।
यज्ञो दानं तपश्चैव पावनानि मनीषिणाम् ॥
translation_en: Acts of sacrifice, gift, and austerity should not be given up but performed; for sacrifice, gift, and austerity are purifiers of the wise.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
एतान्यपि तु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलानि च ।
कर्तव्यानीति मे पार्थ निश्चितं मतमुत्तमम् ॥
translation_en: Yet even these actions should be done giving up attachment and their fruits; this, O Partha, is my settled and highest conviction.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नियतस्य तु संन्यासः कर्मणो नोपपद्यते ।
मोहात्तस्य परित्यागस्तामसः परिकीर्तितः ॥
translation_en: But the giving up of an enjoined action is not fitting; to abandon it through delusion is held to be tamasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
दुःखमित्येव यत्कर्म कायक्लेशभयात्त्यजेत् ।
स कृत्वा राजसं त्यागं नैव त्यागफलं लभेत् ॥
translation_en: He who gives up an action merely as troublesome, fearing bodily pain, performs a rajasic relinquishment and gains no fruit of it.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कार्यमित्येव यत्कर्म नियतं क्रियतेऽर्जुन ।
सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलं चैव स त्यागः सात्त्विको मतः ॥
translation_en: But when an enjoined action is done simply because it ought to be, O Arjuna, giving up attachment and fruit—that relinquishment is held sattvic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न द्वेष्ट्यकुशलं कर्म कुशले नानुषज्जते ।
त्यागी सत्त्वसमाविष्टो मेधावी छिन्नसंशयः ॥
translation_en: The relinquisher filled with sattva, wise and free of doubt, neither shuns disagreeable action nor clings to agreeable action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न हि देहभृता शक्यं त्यक्तुं कर्माण्यशेषतः ।
यस्तु कर्मफलत्यागी स त्यागीत्यभिधीयते ॥
translation_en: For the embodied cannot give up actions entirely; but he who gives up the fruit of action is called a relinquisher.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनिष्टमिष्टं मिश्रं च त्रिविधं कर्मणः फलम् ।
भवत्यत्यागिनां प्रेत्य न तु संन्यासिनां क्वचित् ॥
translation_en: For those who do not relinquish, the fruit of action after death is threefold—unwanted, wanted, and mixed; but for those who renounce, there is none at all.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पञ्चैतानि महाबाहो कारणानि निबोध मे ।
साङ्ख्ये कृतान्ते प्रोक्तानि सिद्धये सर्वकर्मणाम् ॥
translation_en: Learn from me, O mighty-armed one, these five factors, declared in the teaching of Sankhya for the accomplishment of all actions:

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अधिष्ठानं तथा कर्ता करणं च पृथग्विधम् ।
विविधाश्च पृथक्चेष्टा दैवं चैवात्र पञ्चमम् ॥
translation_en: the seat of action, the agent, the various instruments, the many and diverse movements, and divine providence as the fifth.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
शरीरवाङ्मनोभिर्यत्कर्म प्रारभते नरः ।
न्याय्यं वा विपरीतं वा पञ्चैते तस्य हेतवः ॥
translation_en: Whatever action a person sets in motion with body, speech, or mind, right or wrong, these five are its factors.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तत्रैवं सति कर्तारमात्मानं केवलं तु यः ।
पश्यत्यकृतबुद्धित्वान्न स पश्यति दुर्मतिः ॥
translation_en: That being so, the one who, with untrained understanding, sees the self alone as the agent—that fool does not see truly.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यस्य नाहङ्कृतो भावो बुद्धिर्यस्य न लिप्यते ।
हत्वाऽपि स इमाँल्लोकान्न हन्ति न निबध्यते ॥
translation_en: He who is free of the sense of 'I,' whose understanding is untainted, though he slay these people, does not slay, and is not bound.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं परिज्ञाता त्रिविधा कर्मचोदना ।
करणं कर्म कर्तेति त्रिविधः कर्मसङ्ग्रहः ॥
translation_en: Knowledge, the object of knowledge, and the knower—these are the threefold spur to action; the instrument, the act, and the agent—these are the threefold makeup of action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ज्ञानं कर्म च कर्ता च त्रिधैव गुणभेदतः ।
प्रोच्यते गुणसङ्ख्याने यथावच्छृणु तान्यपि ॥
translation_en: Knowledge, action, and the agent are said, in the reckoning of the gunas, to be of three kinds, by the difference of the gunas; hear of these too, as they are.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वभूतेषु येनैकं भावमव्ययमीक्षते ।
अविभक्तं विभक्तेषु तज्ज्ञानं विद्धि सात्त्विकम् ॥
translation_en: The knowledge by which one sees in all beings a single, imperishable being, undivided amid the divided—know that knowledge to be sattvic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
पृथक्त्वेन तु यज्ज्ञानं नानाभावान्पृथग्विधान् ।
वेत्ति सर्वेषु भूतेषु तज्ज्ञानं विद्धि राजसम् ॥
translation_en: But the knowledge that sees in all beings only manifold separate beings, each apart—know that knowledge to be rajasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यत्तु कृत्स्नवदेकस्मिन्कार्ये सक्तमहैतुकम् ।
अतत्त्वार्थवदल्पं च तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥
translation_en: But the knowledge that clings to one single thing as if it were the whole, without reason, missing the truth, and narrow—that is called tamasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नियतं सङ्गरहितमरागद्वेषतः कृतम् ।
अफलप्रेप्सुना कर्म यत्तत्सात्त्विकमुच्यते ॥
translation_en: Action that is enjoined, done without attachment, without craving or aversion, by one who seeks no fruit, is called sattvic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यत्तु कामेप्सुना कर्म साहङ्कारेण वा पुनः ।
क्रियते बहुलायासं तद्राजसमुदाहृतम् ॥
translation_en: But action done with great effort by one seeking to satisfy desire, or out of egotism, is called rajasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अनुबन्धं क्षयं हिंसामनपेक्ष्य च पौरुषम् ।
मोहादारभ्यते कर्म यत्तत्तामसमुच्यते ॥
translation_en: Action undertaken through delusion, heedless of its consequence, of loss and harm, and of one's own power, is called tamasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मुक्तसङ्गोऽनहंवादी धृत्युत्साहसमन्वितः ।
सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योर्निर्विकारः कर्ता सात्त्विक उच्यते ॥
translation_en: An agent free of attachment and ego, possessed of resolve and zeal, unmoved in success and failure, is called sattvic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
रागी कर्मफलप्रेप्सुर्लुब्धो हिंसात्मकोऽशुचिः ।
हर्षशोकान्वितः कर्ता राजसः परिकीर्तितः ॥
translation_en: An agent driven by passion, craving the fruit of action, greedy, cruel, impure, swept by joy and grief, is called rajasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अयुक्तः प्राकृतः स्तब्धः शठो नैष्कृतिकोऽलसः ।
विषादी दीर्घसूत्री च कर्ता तामस उच्यते ॥
translation_en: An agent undisciplined, vulgar, stubborn, dishonest, malicious, slothful, despondent, and procrastinating is called tamasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बुद्धेर्भेदं धृतेश्चैव गुणतस्त्रिविधं श‍ृणु ।
प्रोच्यमानमशेषेण पृथक्त्वेन धनञ्जय ॥
translation_en: Now hear, O Dhananjaya, the threefold division of understanding and of resolve, according to the gunas, set out fully and one by one.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
प्रवृत्तिं च निवृत्तिं च कार्याकार्ये भयाभये ।
बन्धं मोक्षं च या वेत्ति बुद्धिः सा पार्थ सात्त्विकी ॥
translation_en: The understanding that knows action and renunciation, what is to be done and not done, fear and fearlessness, bondage and liberation, O Partha, is sattvic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यया धर्ममधर्मं च कार्यं चाकार्यमेव च ।
अयथावत्प्रजानाति बुद्धिः सा पार्थ राजसी ॥
translation_en: The understanding by which one wrongly grasps dharma and adharma, and what is to be done and not done, O Partha, is rajasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अधर्मं धर्ममिति या मन्यते तमसावृता ।
सर्वार्थान्विपरीतांश्च बुद्धिः सा पार्थ तामसी ॥
translation_en: The understanding that, wrapped in darkness, takes adharma to be dharma and sees all things inverted, O Partha, is tamasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
धृत्या यया धारयते मनःप्राणेन्द्रियक्रियाः ।
योगेनाव्यभिचारिण्या धृतिः सा पार्थ सात्त्विकी ॥
translation_en: The resolve by which one holds steady the workings of the mind, the breath, and the senses, through unwavering yoga—that resolve, O Partha, is sattvic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यया तु धर्मकामार्थान्धृत्या धारयतेऽर्जुन ।
प्रसङ्गेन फलाकाङ्क्षी धृतिः सा पार्थ राजसी ॥
translation_en: But the resolve by which one clings, with attachment to fruit, to dharma, pleasure, and wealth, longing for their reward, O Partha, is rajasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यया स्वप्नं भयं शोकं विषादं मदमेव च ।
न विमुञ्चति दुर्मेधा धृतिः सा पार्थ तामसी ॥
translation_en: The resolve by which the dull-witted will not let go of sleep, fear, grief, despair, and folly—that resolve, O Partha, is tamasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सुखं त्विदानीं त्रिविधं श‍ृणु मे भरतर्षभ ।
अभ्यासाद्रमते यत्र दुःखान्तं च निगच्छति ॥
translation_en: Now hear from me, O best of the Bharatas, the threefold happiness, that in which one comes to delight through practice and reaches the end of suffering.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यत्तदग्रे विषमिव परिणामेऽमृतोपमम् ।
तत्सुखं सात्त्विकं प्रोक्तमात्मबुद्धिप्रसादजम् ॥
translation_en: The happiness that is like poison at first but like nectar in the end, born of the serene clarity of one's own understanding, is called sattvic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
विषयेन्द्रियसंयोगाद्यत्तदग्रेऽमृतोपमम् ।
परिणामे विषमिव तत्सुखं राजसं स्मृतम् ॥
translation_en: The happiness born of the contact of the senses with their objects, like nectar at first but like poison in the end, is held to be rajasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदग्रे चानुबन्धे च सुखं मोहनमात्मनः ।
निद्रालस्यप्रमादोत्थं तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥
translation_en: The happiness that deludes the self both at first and in the end, arising from sleep, sloth, and heedlessness, is called tamasic.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न तदस्ति पृथिव्यां वा दिवि देवेषु वा पुनः ।
सत्त्वं प्रकृतिजैर्मुक्तं यदेभिः स्यात्त्रिभिर्गुणैः ॥
translation_en: There is no being, on earth or among the gods in heaven, that is free of these three gunas born of prakriti.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ब्राह्मणक्षत्रियविशां शूद्राणां च परन्तप ।
कर्माणि प्रविभक्तानि स्वभावप्रभवैर्गुणैः ॥
translation_en: The duties of brahmins, kshatriyas, vaishyas, and shudras, O scorcher of foes, are apportioned by the gunas that arise from their own natures.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
शमो दमस्तपः शौचं क्षान्तिरार्जवमेव च ।
ज्ञानं विज्ञानमास्तिक्यं ब्रह्मकर्म स्वभावजम् ॥
translation_en: Calm, restraint, austerity, purity, patience, and uprightness; knowledge, realization, and faith in the unseen—these are the duty of a brahmin, born of his nature.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
शौर्यं तेजो धृतिर्दाक्ष्यं युद्धे चाप्यपलायनम् ।
दानमीश्वरभावश्च क्षात्रं कर्म स्वभावजम् ॥
translation_en: Valor, vigor, fortitude, skill, not fleeing in battle, generosity, and lordly bearing—these are the duty of a kshatriya, born of his nature.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कृषिगौरक्ष्यवाणिज्यं वैश्यकर्म स्वभावजम् ।
परिचर्यात्मकं कर्म शूद्रस्यापि स्वभावजम् ॥
translation_en: Farming, herding, and trade are the duty of a vaishya, born of his nature; and service is the duty of a shudra, born of his nature.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
स्वे स्वे कर्मण्यभिरतः संसिद्धिं लभते नरः ।
स्वकर्मनिरतः सिद्धिं यथा विन्दति तच्छृणु ॥
translation_en: Devoted each to his own work, a person attains perfection; hear how one devoted to his own work finds it.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यतः प्रवृत्तिर्भूतानां येन सर्वमिदं ततम् ।
स्वकर्मणा तमभ्यर्च्य सिद्धिं विन्दति मानवः ॥
translation_en: He from whom all beings spring, and by whom all this is pervaded—worshiping him through his own work, a person attains perfection.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुणः परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात् ।
स्वभावनियतं कर्म कुर्वन्नाप्नोति किल्बिषम् ॥
translation_en: Better one's own dharma, imperfectly done, than another's dharma well performed; doing the work set by one's own nature, one incurs no fault.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सहजं कर्म कौन्तेय सदोषमपि न त्यजेत् ।
सर्वारम्भा हि दोषेण धूमेनाग्निरिवावृताः ॥
translation_en: One should not give up the work born of one's nature, O son of Kunti, even if it is flawed; for all undertakings are wrapped in flaw, as fire in smoke.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
असक्तबुद्धिः सर्वत्र जितात्मा विगतस्पृहः ।
नैष्कर्म्यसिद्धिं परमां संन्यासेनाधिगच्छति ॥
translation_en: He whose understanding is unattached everywhere, his self subdued, his longing gone, attains through renunciation the supreme perfection of freedom from action.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सिद्धिं प्राप्तो यथा ब्रह्म तथाप्नोति निबोध मे ।
समासेनैव कौन्तेय निष्ठा ज्ञानस्य या परा ॥
translation_en: Hear from me in brief, O son of Kunti, how one who has attained perfection attains Brahman as well—that highest consummation of knowledge.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
बुद्ध्या विशुद्धया युक्तो धृत्यात्मानं नियम्य च ।
शब्दादीन्विषयांस्त्यक्त्वा रागद्वेषौ व्युदस्य च ॥
translation_en: Yoked with a purified understanding, restraining the self with firmness, turning away from sound and the other objects of sense, casting off craving and aversion,

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
विविक्तसेवी लघ्वाशी यतवाक्कायमानसः ।
ध्यानयोगपरो नित्यं वैराग्यं समुपाश्रितः ॥
translation_en: dwelling apart, eating little, his speech, body, and mind controlled, ever absorbed in the yoga of meditation, taking refuge in dispassion,

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अहङ्कारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं परिग्रहम् ।
विमुच्य निर्ममः शान्तो ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते ॥
translation_en: freed from ego, force, pride, desire, anger, and possession, without a sense of 'mine,' and at peace—he is fit for the state of Brahman.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ब्रह्मभूतः प्रसन्नात्मा न शोचति न काङ्क्षति ।
समः सर्वेषु भूतेषु मद्भक्तिं लभते पराम् ॥
translation_en: Become Brahman, serene in self, he neither grieves nor craves; the same toward all beings, he attains supreme devotion to me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
भक्त्या मामभिजानाति यावान्यश्चास्मि तत्त्वतः ।
ततो मां तत्त्वतो ज्ञात्वा विशते तदनन्तरम् ॥
translation_en: By devotion he comes to know me, who and how great I truly am; and knowing me in truth, he enters into me forthwith.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वकर्माण्यपि सदा कुर्वाणो मद्व्यपाश्रयः ।
मत्प्रसादादवाप्नोति शाश्वतं पदमव्ययम् ॥
translation_en: Ever performing all actions, taking refuge in me, by my grace he reaches the eternal, imperishable abode.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
चेतसा सर्वकर्माणि मयि संन्यस्य मत्परः ।
बुद्धियोगमुपाश्रित्य मच्चित्तः सततं भव ॥
translation_en: Surrendering all actions to me in your thought, intent on me, taking refuge in the yoga of understanding, keep your mind ever fixed on me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मच्चित्तः सर्वदुर्गाणि मत्प्रसादात्तरिष्यसि ।
अथ चेत्त्वमहङ्कारान्न श्रोष्यसि विनङ्क्ष्यसि ॥
translation_en: With your mind on me, you will cross over all difficulties by my grace; but if through ego you will not listen, you will be lost.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
यदहङ्कारमाश्रित्य न योत्स्य इति मन्यसे ।
मिथ्यैष व्यवसायस्ते प्रकृतिस्त्वां नियोक्ष्यति ॥
translation_en: If, relying on your ego, you think, 'I will not fight,' vain is this resolve of yours; your own nature will compel you.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
स्वभावजेन कौन्तेय निबद्धः स्वेन कर्मणा ।
कर्तुं नेच्छसि यन्मोहात्करिष्यस्यवशोऽपि तत् ॥
translation_en: Bound by your own work, born of your nature, O son of Kunti, what through delusion you wish not to do, you will do even against your will.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
ईश्वरः सर्वभूतानां हृद्देशेऽर्जुन तिष्ठति ।
भ्रामयन्सर्वभूतानि यन्त्रारूढानि मायया ॥
translation_en: The lord dwells in the heart of all beings, Arjuna, whirling them all about by his maya, as though mounted on a machine.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
तमेव शरणं गच्छ सर्वभावेन भारत ।
तत्प्रसादात्परां शान्तिं स्थानं प्राप्स्यसि शाश्वतम् ॥
translation_en: Take refuge in him alone with all your being, O Bharata; by his grace you will attain supreme peace and the eternal abode.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इति ते ज्ञानमाख्यातं गुह्याद्गुह्यतरं मया ।
विमृश्यैतदशेषेण यथेच्छसि तथा कुरु ॥
translation_en: Thus I have declared to you the knowledge more secret than all secrets; ponder it fully, and then do as you wish.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वगुह्यतमं भूयः श‍ृणु मे परमं वचः ।
इष्टोऽसि मे दृढमिति ततो वक्ष्यामि ते हितम् ॥
translation_en: Hear again my supreme word, the most secret of all; you are greatly loved by me, and so I shall tell you what is for your good.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु ।
मामेवैष्यसि सत्यं ते प्रतिजाने प्रियोऽसि मे ॥
translation_en: Fix your mind on me, be devoted to me, sacrifice to me, bow before me; so you will come to me. I promise you truly, for you are dear to me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज ।
अहं त्वा सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः ॥
translation_en: Giving up all dharmas, take refuge in me alone; I will free you from all sins—do not grieve.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
इदं ते नातपस्काय नाभक्ताय कदाचन ।
न चाशुश्रूषवे वाच्यं न च मां योऽभ्यसूयति ॥
translation_en: This is never to be told by you to one without austerity or devotion, to one who will not listen, or to one who scorns me.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
य इदं परमं गुह्यं मद्भक्तेष्वभिधास्यति ।
भक्तिं मयि परां कृत्वा मामेवैष्यत्यसंशयः ॥
translation_en: He who shall declare this supreme secret among those devoted to me, offering me the highest devotion, will come to me beyond doubt.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
न च तस्मान्मनुष्येषु कश्चिन्मे प्रियकृत्तमः ।
भविता न च मे तस्मादन्यः प्रियतरो भुवि ॥
translation_en: Among men there is none who does me dearer service, and there will be no one on earth dearer to me than he.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
अध्येष्यते च य इमं धर्म्यं संवादमावयोः ।
ज्ञानयज्ञेन तेनाहमिष्टः स्यामिति मे मतिः ॥
translation_en: And he who studies this our dialogue on dharma—by him I shall be worshiped with the sacrifice of knowledge; so I hold.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
श्रद्धावाननसूयश्च श‍ृणुयादपि यो नरः ।
सोऽपि मुक्तः शुभाँल्लोकान्प्राप्नुयात्पुण्यकर्मणाम् ॥
translation_en: And the one who merely hears it, full of faith and free of scorn—he too, set free, will attain the happy worlds of those who do right.

speaker: (Shri Bhagavan Krishna)
sanskrit_sloka:
कच्चिदेतच्छ्रुतं पार्थ त्वयैकाग्रेण चेतसा ।
कच्चिदज्ञानसम्मोहः प्रनष्टस्ते धनञ्जय ॥
translation_en: Have you heard this, O Partha, with mind one-pointed? Has your delusion born of ignorance been destroyed, O Dhananjaya?

speaker: (Arjuna)
sanskrit_sloka:
नष्टो मोहः स्मृतिर्लब्धा त्वत्प्रसादान्मयाच्युत ।
स्थितोऽस्मि गतसन्देहः करिष्ये वचनं तव ॥
translation_en: My delusion is gone; by your grace, O Acyuta, I have regained my memory. I stand firm, my doubts dispelled; I will do your word.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
इत्यहं वासुदेवस्य पार्थस्य च महात्मनः ।
संवादमिममश्रौषमद्भुतं रोमहर्षणम् ॥
translation_en: Thus have I heard this wondrous dialogue between Vasudeva and the great-souled Partha, making my hair stand on end.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
व्यासप्रसादाच्छ्रुतवानेतद्गुह्यमहं परम् ।
योगं योगेश्वरात्कृष्णात्साक्षात्कथयतः स्वयम् ॥
translation_en: By the grace of Vyasa I have heard this supreme and most secret yoga directly from Krishna, the lord of yoga, as he himself declared it.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
राजन्संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य संवादमिममद्भुतम् ।
केशवार्जुनयोः पुण्यं हृष्यामि च मुहुर्मुहुः ॥
translation_en: O king, recalling again and again this wondrous and holy dialogue of Keshava and Arjuna, I rejoice again and again.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
तच्च संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य रूपमत्यद्भुतं हरेः ।
विस्मयो मे महान् राजन्हृष्यामि च पुनः पुनः ॥
translation_en: And recalling again and again that most wondrous form of Hari, great is my wonder, O king, and I rejoice again and again.

speaker: (Sanjaya)
sanskrit_sloka:
यत्र योगेश्वरः कृष्णो यत्र पार्थो धनुर्धरः ।
तत्र श्रीर्विजयो भूतिर्ध्रुवा नीतिर्मतिर्मम ॥
translation_en: Wherever Krishna, the lord of yoga, is, and wherever Partha, the archer, is, there, I hold, are fortune, victory, well-being, and steadfast right conduct.
